observations.recommendation:
We therefore recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. the Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly BRS as required by COA Cir. No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 to ensure the accuracy of the Cash in Bank accounts in the FS. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their ledger balances and cashbook balances, respectively every end of the month and adjust any reconciling items regularly so that the validity and accuracy of the balances of the Cash in Bank accounts in the FS could be ascertained; and
b. The Municipal Treasurer to transfer the bank account not included in the FS in the General Fund bank account, in order to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management. Reiterated in Observation No. 1 page 35 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We therefore recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
1. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain SLs, PPELCs and RPLCs duly supplied with data as prescribed by pertinent provisions of the NGAS Manual;
2. Municipal Treasurer as the GSO to:
a. Prepare and maintain SCs and PCs for each item of property; and,
b. Conduct annual physical inventory taking to establish validity of existence of all properties; and,
3. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to take the lead in complying all the general and procedural guidelines embodied in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the one-time cleansing of the PPE account balances.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management. Reiterated in Observation No. 2 page 41 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer, and GSO to adhere strictly to the accounting and reporting guidelines concerning the management of local roads assets, and to earnestly perform their respective duties and responsibilities set forth under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015. Likewise, we recommended the Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical count of all its LRN in accordance with the aforementioned circular and make sure to produce the details necessary to be reported separately in the RPCLRN for submission to the Auditor and Accounting Office not later than January 31of each year.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management. Reiterated in Observation No. 3 page 45 of this report.

observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation:
1.9. We therefore recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. The Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly BRS as required by
COA Cir. No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 to ensure the accuracy of the
Cash in Bank accounts in the FS. Likewise, require the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the ledger and
cashbook balances, respectively every end of the month and adjust any
reconciling items regularly so that the validity and accuracy of the
balances of the Cash in Bank accounts in the FS could be ascertained; and
b. The Municipal Treasurer to transfer the bank account balance of
₱32,207.93 which was not included in the FS of the General Fund bank
account, in order to avoid accounts dormancy, incurrence of penalties
and surcharges, which ultimately results in the loss of government funds.

2.14. In order to present fairly the PPE accounts in the FS, we recommended that
the Municipal Mayor direct the:
1. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain SLs, PPELCs and
RPLCs duly supplied with data as prescribed by pertinent provisions of
the NGAS Manual;
2. Municipal Treasurer as the GSO to:
a. Prepare and maintain SCs and PCs for each item of property;
and,
b. Conduct annual physical inventory taking to establish validity of
existence of all properties; and,
3. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer/Acting GSO to take
advantage of the provisions of COA Circular 2020-006 and lead in
complying all the general and procedural guidelines embodied in COA
Circular for the one-time cleansing of the PPE account balances and
COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024 for the supplemental
guidelines relative to the increase in the capitalization threshold from
P15,000.00 to P50,000.00.

3.4. We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant,
Municipal Engineer, and GSO to adhere strictly to the accounting and
reporting guidelines concerning the management of local roads assets, and to
earnestly perform their respective duties and responsibilities set forth under
COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

3.5. Likewise, require the Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical count
of all its Local Road Networks in accordance with regulations and prepare
the RPCLRN using the prescribed form and submit a copy to the Auditor and
Accounting Office not later than January 31of each year.

3.6. Also, require the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to prepare the LRNPC
and LRNLC, respectively, hence facilitate the preparation of the RPCLRN
and reconciliation of the report with the accounting report.

4.6. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer
to submit to the Accounting Office a copy of the SWA of all infrastructure
projects which are still in progress and still unpaid as of year-end to comply
with the provisions of paragraph 4.6 of COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated
September 30, 2016. Also, instruct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that
all lawful expenditures and obligations incurred during the year arising from
the submitted SWAs shall be recorded/taken up in the accounts of that year,
in order to present the affected account fairly in the financial statements.

5.4. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to see to it that at the end of the year, all obligations/expenses
incurred which payments are still pending has to be obligated in consonance
with Section 119 of PD 1445. Likewise, instruct the Municipal Budget Officer
to see to it that only current obligations are charged to current
appropriations, hence expenses incurred during the year shall be taken
up/recognized in the accounts of that year.

Recommendations:
a. Municipal Accountant to (i) promptly prepare and effect the appropriate adjusting entries necessary to take up the reconciling items; and (ii) submit to the AT the reconciliation statement substantiated with appropriate documentation.
Ensure timely preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statement to avoid accumulation of reconciling items.
Close coordination with the banks must also be maintained to ensure the timely receipt of bank statements, along with regular follow-ups with the MTO for the submission of outstanding documents.
b. Acting Municipal Treasurer to (i) account for all unclaimed checks which had been outstanding for over six months from the date of issue wherein the original DVs and supporting documents are still in the custody of the treasury office and cancel these stale checks; (ii) notify the payees of the identified stale checks to return or cancel the previously issued checks, and (iii) a status report should be submitted to the Audit Team.

Recommendations:
a. Municipal Mayor, thru the Municipal Accountant to (i) continue sending demand letter to all concerned AOs to settle of their outstanding cash advances; and (ii) initiate the preparation and consolidation of necessary documents in support of the request for write-off of the accounts of deceased employees and those dormant unliquidated cash advances according to the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005; and (iii) initiate the filing of appropriate action against the erring AOs who still failed to settle their accounts after their demand, if warranted;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to monitor the status of the request, and promptly address any COA queries or requests for additional documentation.
Once approve by COA, ensure that the Municipal Accountant finalizes the write-off and updates financial records accordingly.
Ensure the inclusion and submission of subsidiary ledgers (SLs) in the annual report.
b. Municipal Accountant to (i) immediately submit to the Audit Team the complete SLs for the cash advances of officers and employees as of December 31, 2024.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to promptly reconstruct and submit the SLs for audit disallowances totaling ₱1,420,937.98, ensuring compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-006

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Sangguniang Bayan to update the existing ordinance on the daily rate of JOs and COs personnel and to ensure alignment with the standard and prevailing minimum wage rates for JOs and COs employees issued by the RTWPB.

Recommendations:
(i) Direct the requisitioning officer/units to (a) Submit a written explanation/justification for their non-compliance with the requirements provided under E.O. No. 49, s. 1993 and require to attached a certification that the drugs and products requisitioned or procured fall within and conform with the PNDF. If procurement of necessary medicines is not in the list, request for inclusion in the PNF; and (b) Submit a written explanation/ justification for indicating the brand names of the drugs/medicines in the PRs and instruct them to ensure that the description of drugs and medicines is generic and not tailored to a particular brand, based on relevant characteristics and completely disclosed in the Purchase Requests.
(ii) Direct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) coordinate with relevant offices to submit required documents per COA Circular No. 2012-001; and (b) review supporting documents for completeness before processing payments
(iii) thru the Municipal Accountant, BAC and BAC Secretariat, (i) facilitate the submission of the lacking documents; (ii) require the concerned officials to render satisfactory explanation for the deficiencies noted in audit; and (iii) ensure compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, and RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR; and
(iv) take appropriate action to collect the liquidated damages from the aforementioned suppliers who incurred delays in the delivery of the procured drugs and medicines. Additionally, ensure that the terms and conditions indicated in the contracts are strictly complied with.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that:

We recommended that:

After such evaluation, the MAccO determine the total amount of tangible items acquired and issued prior to CY 2024 with amounts below ₱50,000.00 capitalization threshold which can be derecognized from the books; and
Before any derecognition, the Property and Supply Officer-Designee should be able to document first the accountability over these tangible items acquired and issued prior to CY 2024 with amounts below ₱50,000.00 capitalization threshold through duly signed ICSs, as well as, the registries and reports required under COA Circular No. 2022-004, as supplemented by COA Circular No. 2024-006.

Adopt appropriate measures to ensure that sufficient cash is available to meet the obligations of the Municipality, ensuring that monies held in trust be used only for the intended purpose, pursuant to Section No. 337 of RA No. 7160 and Section 4(3) of PD No. 1445; and
Require the MAccO and Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO) to conduct constant review of the cash liquidity to assist Management in keeping track of or improving its cash position.

a.	Prepare and cause to be submitted by the Local DRRM Officer, to OCD, DILG and COA, the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRM Funds;

b.	Report the unexpended balance of Local DRRM Fund in the Notes to the Financial Statement using the format prescribed in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.  A copy of the prescribed format is attached as Appendix F.

Likewise, for the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain and update Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for DRRM funds.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare Subsidiary Ledger for each person liable and draw JEV to record the disallowances with Notice of Finality of Decision as receivables in the books of accounts.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to (1) direct the Municipal Accountant to look into the history of the account, trace accounting entries to ascertain the cause of negative balances and 

(2) reclassify payments of tax deficiency to Other Payables account and establish a receivable account.  We further recommend that the officials responsible be personally liable for interest, surcharges and penalties from their failure to withhold the appropriate taxes from payments of goods/services and compensation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze and trace transactions and accounting entries to ascertain the cause of the negative balance and effect necessary adjustments/reclassifications to correct the account balance reported in the financial statements.

We recommended that management strictly observe Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and Section 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and demand for the necessary documents to facilitate the transfer of ownership or title of the property in the name of the municipality.
complete data / bank statements from LGUs bank accounts. To prepare necessary adjusting entries for stale checks and outstanding checks and others.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation on all depreciable assets and prepare the necessary journal entries in compliance with IPSASs Nos. 1 and 17 to ensure fair presentation of these assets in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Trust Liabilities - DRRMF account amounting to ₱10,987,201.67 with the Special Trust Fund disclosures totaling ₱12,315,279.87 as presented in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the erroneous classification of the semi-expendable property and henceforth strictly observe the appropriate recognition of tangible items in compliance with COA Circular No. 2022-004 and supplemental guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024 to correct the misstatements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor and track the recording of Due to BIR account and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the negative balance of Due to BIR account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the disbursement vouchers together with all the necessary supporting documents in a timely manner.  Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct reconciliation on the amounts withheld and remitted to the GSIS and the necessary adjusting entries should be prepared to correct the negative balance of the Due to GSIS account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate permanent personnel responsible for the timely remittance of Pag-IBIG contributions to ensure compliance with existing regulations, prevent the risk of fund mismanagement, and avoid imposition of penalties and surcharges on the Municipality and the accountable officials.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

1) 	 Ensure the remittance of PhilHealth contributions is made between the 11th and 15th day of the month following the applicable period; and

2) 	 Submit a written explanation regarding the late remittance of Philhealth contributions for January and March 2024, which resulted in the payment of interest and surcharges amounting to ₱10,185.67, including the late remittance for October 2024.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with Item 1.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 by requiring an authorization letter and valid identification documents before releasing payments to job order personnel when claimed by someone other than the payee.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the following:

a.)	The LDRRM Officer to submit the CY 2024 Annual LDRRM Fund Investment Plan; and

b.)	The Municipal Accountant to submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for CY 2024.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to prepare and submit the CY 2024 GAD Plan and Budget and the CY 2024 GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local School Board to ensure the full implementation of SEF-funded PPAs and the optimal utilization of SEF to effectively address the annual budgetary needs in the operation and maintenance of public schools within the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and other officials concerned to immediately submit the seventy-seven (77) disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents totaling ₱8,865,127.70.  Further, strict compliance should be enforced that all paid disbursement vouchers and supporting documents are submitted to the Auditor within ten (10) days of the ensuing month, as required by law.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and other officials concerned to strictly comply with the prescribed timeline by submitting disbursement vouchers, supporting documents, official receipts, deposit slips, payrolls and all pertinent accounting reports within ten (10) days following the end of each ensuing month, in accordance with existing laws and regulations.

We commended the management of LGU San Vicente for appropriating and utilizing the 20% DF in full compliance with existing guidelines.  The municipality adhered to Section 3.2.5 of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 1, ensuring that no prohibited PPAs were funded.  This demonstrates sound financial management and a strong commitment to sustainable local development.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries and strictly observe the appropriate use of accounts prescribed in the Revised Chart of Accounts to attain fair presentation of financial statements in compliance with IPSASs 1.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to record the fund balances received from PhilHealth and from BGCM.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the prescribed accounting treatment for PPE and its related allowance for depreciation and to prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the errors made in compliance with IPSASs Nos. 1 and 17 in order to attain fair presentation of the pertinent assets in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMC, with close coordination with the MDRRM Officer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to regularly monitor and evaluate the implementations of the LDRRM Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) to ensure the full implementation of LDRRM PPAs in the budget year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer-Designate to monitor the implementation of the projects to promote towards public health and environmental protection of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to return the unexpended balances of fund transfers totaling ₱217,178.98 to the source agencies.

We recommended that Management:
 
1.	Exhaust all available remedies provided under the laws, rules and regulations in enforcing immediate compliance of the audit suspensions and the refund/settlement of the disallowances as provided in the RRSA.

2.	Avail of all possible means as provided by law to effect collection from those who are already separated from government service, and those who are not connected with the Municipality, otherwise the officers and employees determined to be liable shall be required to settle the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

b.	Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Card and Property Ledger Card, respectively, for all PPEs of the agency.
 

c. Require the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to observe and exhaust all the necessary procedures provided in the COA Circular No. 2020-006 to come up with a reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to their existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to reflect the correct balances of the Trust Liabilities and Due to NGAs account and to strictly adhere to the prescribed Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units provided in COA Circular No. 2015-009 in recording of the transactions of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor establish close coordination with the GFPS and the implementing offices in GAD planning and monitoring to ascertain the full implementation of GAD PPAs and the full utilization of GAD Fund to effectively and efficiently address the gender sensitivity issues and gender mainstreaming in the operations of the Municipality.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:

b) 	Make a formal request with the Commission on Audit for the writing-off of unliquidated cash advances of those who are already deceased, and those separated from the service whose whereabouts are unknown, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005;


c) 	Consider withholding the salary of those employees and officers still connected with the LGU, to enforce full settlement thereof.


d) 	Require the Municipal Accountant to regularly issue Demand Letters to AOs with unsettled cash advances, particularly those that have already been outstanding for several years; and to see to it that previous cash accountabilities of officers and employees are fully settled/liquidated and duly accounted for in the books of accounts before additional cash advances are granted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all the undeposited collections amounting to ₱345,301.78 in order to avoid possible loss or misuse and we further recommended to fully adhere to Section 32 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) on the Deposit of Collections.

• 	 Maintain subsidiary records for road and road components.

• 	Prepare lapsing schedule for the depreciation of the depreciable components of the Local Road Network.

c.Inventory Committee to regularly conduct physical count of Inventory and reconcile the results thereof with the records maintained in the Supply Office and the Accounting Office; and

The GFPS, with the assistance of the GFPS Technical Working Group and in coordination with the HRMO, recommend and plan an appropriate capacity development program on GAD, not only for the members of the GFPS organizational structure but also for all LGU employees (including, but not be limited to, gender sensitivity training, gender-responsive planning and budgeting, gender analysis, gender audit and GAD tools such as the Gender Mainstreaming Evaluation Framework, Gender Responsive LGU Ka Ba Self-Assessment Tool, Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines and other succeeding GAD tools) as part of and to be implemented under its regular human resource development program; and 

The GFPS, in coordination with the concerned Implementing Units, ensure the implementation of aforesaid PPAs on capacity development on GAD appropriated with funds in its annual GPBs and Annual Budget, in compliance with Section 37 (C) (1) of the IRR of MCW and Section 4.1, (A), (3) of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 as amended by 2016-01.

The LCE consider and prioritize the creation of GAD M&E Team through an EO or AO in accordance with Section 5.0 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 as amended by 2016-01. Alternatively, when practicable, expand the function and membership of the existing Local Project Monitoring Committee complimenting the GAD M&E Team in accordance with the same JMC;

When already constituted, the GAD M&E Team ensure to:

i. Evaluate the outcomes of the LGU GAD policies, programs and projects annually; and

ii. Prepare and submit the LGU GAD Evaluation Report to the DILG Regional Office at the end of the LCE's three-year term.

The LGU GFPS utilize the outcome of monitoring and evaluation system as inputs in improving the LGU’s gender mainstreaming efforts and gender-responsive planning and policy-making geared towards the achievement of gender equality and women empowerment goals of the LGU.
