observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. 	Prepare and cause to be submitted by the Local DRRM Officer, to OCD, DILG and COA, the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRM Funds;

b. 	Report the unexpended balance of Local DRRM Fund in the Notes to the Financial Statement using the format prescribed in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.  A copy of the prescribed format is attached as Appendix F.

Likewise, for the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain and update Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for DRRM funds.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare Subsidiary Ledger for each person liable and draw JEV to record the disallowances with Notice of Finality of Decision as receivables in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Updated subsidiary ledger and prepare adjusting journal entries to record disallowance properly.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated prior years’ recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the ledgers and cashbooks in order to establish the accurate balances of Cash in Bank accounts. Correspondingly, oblige the Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements in order to determine entries in both the books and bank accounts which may need adjustment, thus, the validity and accuracy of the balances of the Cash in Bank accounts as appearing in the Municipality’s financial statements could be ascertained.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 1 page 33 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to: a. Instruct the BPLO to furnish the Accounting Office a copy of uncollected rental of stall owners and business taxes due as at year-end; and b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary journal entry/entries to correct the above-computed understatement of the Receivable and the corresponding Income accounts in order to present fairly the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The BPLO has furnished the Accounting Office a copy of uncollected rentals as of year-end. The Municipal Accountant did not prepare correcting journal entries during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to locate the source documents pertaining to these obligations in order to return or remit and released to the source NGAs, LGUs and specific payees so that the funds could be used by them to finance their projects and programs. In the event that reconciliation and tracing back is no longer practical, we recommended that the funds will be reverted to the General Fund and be declared as savings which could be used to finance different programs and projects of the municipality. Further, require the Municipal Accountant to prepare proper adjusting entries in order to fairly present the financial statements of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: For the proper management and handling of the PPE, we recommended Management to: a. Require the Inventory Committee to follow the guidelines and procedures as provided for in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 202 in the conduct of physical count of PPE, recognition of those found at station and disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts of the Municipality to establish existence, condition and accuracy of the PPE balances. Conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of December 31 of every year. The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the physical existence of the PPE and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets. Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments and to exact responsibility/ accountability/ liability for missing PPEs; and b. Maintain/update the subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger cards, AREs and placing of property tags for proper control and monitoring of the PPE and for a facility in identifying them.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 2 page 35 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Property Officer to notify the Audit Team of all deliveries of procured items within twenty-four (24) hours from the acceptance of the Management so that the Auditor could conduct the timely inspection/validation of same and deficiencies, if any, would be communicated to Management for immediate and appropriate action thereof.
observations.managementAction: The property officer did not notify the Audit Team of all the deliveries for the latter’s timely inspection/validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the BAC Secretariat to post on the PhilGEPS website all awards within the prescribed period of three calendar days to manifest and attain transparency in the procurement.
observations.managementAction: The NOAs were still posted late in Philgeps website.

observations.recommendation: In order to preempt the risk of non-performance and non-recoupment of advance payments, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to require the contractor before granting the payment of mobilization to submit an irrevocable standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial bank, a bank guarantee, or a surety bond. Likewise, deduct from every progress billing the corresponding percentage for the recoupment of the advance payments to ensure that the amount will be fully recovered.
observations.managementAction: No surety bond was acquired yet to support payment of mobilization fee.

observations.recommendation:
1.8. We therefore recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. the Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly BRS as required by COA Cir. No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 to ensure the accuracy of the Cash in Bank accounts in the FS. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their ledger balances and cashbook balances, respectively every end of the month and adjust any reconciling items regularly so that the validity and accuracy of the balances of the Cash in Bank accounts in the FS could be ascertained; and
b. The Municipal Treasurer to transfer the bank account not included in the FS in the General Fund bank account, in order to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges.

2.13. In order to present fairly the PPE accounts in the FS, we recommended that
the Municipal Mayor direct the:
1. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain SLs, PPELCs and
RPLCs duly supplied with data as prescribed by pertinent provisions
of the NGAS Manual;
2. Municipal Treasurer as the GSO to:
a. Prepare and maintain SCs and PCs for each item of property; and,
b. Conduct annual physical inventory taking to establish validity of
existence of all properties; and,
3. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to take the lead in
complying all the general and procedural guidelines embodied in COA
Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the one-time
cleansing of the PPE account balances.

3.4. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant, Municipal Engineer, and GSO to adhere strictly to the
accounting and reporting guidelines concerning the management of local
roads assets, and to earnestly perform their respective duties and
responsibilities set forth under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November
23, 2015.

3.5. Likewise, we recommended the Inventory Committee to conduct annual
physical count of all its LRN in accordance with the aforementioned circular
and make sure to produce the details necessary to be reported separately in
the RPCLRN for submission to the Auditor and Accounting Office not later
than January 31of each year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to record adjusting entries in order to present fairly the balances of the inventory accounts in the financial statement.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had already adjusted the account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that gasoline expenses are supported with properly accomplished Trip Ticket to ensure the reasonableness of fuel consumption. Further, we recommended that Management designate employee who will be in charged in the issuance of trip ticket and monitor the fuel consumption of the Municipality in order to avoid wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption.
observations.managementAction: The Management had prepared Trip Tickets.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to require the MSWDO employees to prepare and submit written report as to the nature of work accomplished certified by the head of the agency to determine whether or not they were exposed to the hazard environment or occupational risk, thus, the validity, propriety and completeness of the payment of hazard allowance could be ascertained.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO had prepared supporting documents for the claim.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the BAC Secretariat to indicate accurate data in order for the prospective bidders to be well informed regarding the precise data to be used in the preparation of the bids and avoid the potential situation that the awarded contract exceeded the ABC.
observations.managementAction: The ABC was already indicated in the Invitation to Bid by the BAC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the BAC Secretariat to provide a provision in the SCC such as the terms and conditions of deliveries, issuance of delivery receipt for every delivery of goods for easy verification if the goods delivered were in accordance with what were stated in the purchase order/contract and in order to determine whether the suppliers perform their obligations to deliver and to ensure payments are proper and valid.
observations.managementAction: The payment and delivery terms were included in the SCC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to withhold income taxes from the payee at all times in order to help the national government generate additional taxes for immediate use for its operations.
observations.managementAction: No payment of notarial fess were effected during the year.

observations.recommendation:
1.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Health Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer
when there are transfers or donations in-kind which are directly
delivered to the Municipal Health Office.
b. Municipal Treasurer to acknowledge receipt of the transferred vaccines
and other medical supplies from the Provincial Health Office and
prepare the necessary documents to be submitted to the Municipal
Accountant. Henceforth, require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare an Acknowledgement Receipt of In-Kind donations every time there is
a donation from other government, private sector and international
institutions, and prepare a report of the in-kind donations to be use as
basis of the Municipal Accountant to draw journal entry voucher for
the recognition of the inventory in the books of accounts.
c. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries.
d. The MHO to coordinate with the MTO, particularly, the Property and
Supply Officer as he is responsible for the deliveries of supplies either
through procurement or donation.

2.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Create an Inventory Committee to conduct one-time cleansing of PPE
accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006.
b. Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to
maintain Property Card and Property Ledger Card, respectively, for all
PPEs of the agency.
c. Require the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to
observe and exhaust all the necessary procedures provided in the COA
Circular No. 2020-006 to come up with a reliable PPE balances that are
verifiable as to their existence, condition and accountability.

3.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to reflect the
correct balances of the Trust Liabilities and Due to NGAs account and to
strictly adhere to the prescribed Revised Chart of Accounts for Local
Government Units provided in COA Circular No. 2015-009 in recording of
the transactions of the LGU.

4.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to provide adequate disclosures of LDRRM Fund in consonance
to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

5.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning
and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer to monitor and give
utmost priority in the implementation of all PPAs funded out of 20%
Development Fund (DF) in the annual budget within the schedule of
implementation.

6.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Human Resource
Management Officer to prepare contracts between the Municipality and the
job order workers stating the date of payment of wages and other necessary
stipulations, certified by the Municipal Accountant as to availability of funds,
and duly acknowledged and entered into by the JO workers.

7.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an oversight committee
that will be responsible for the update of the Municipal Revenue Code of
2008 pursuant to Section 191 of RA 7160 so that the LGU can impose more
reasonable rate that could generate additional income and maximize revenue
for its operation.

8.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRM Officer
to:
1. Review the PPAs embodied in the LDRRMF Investment Plan and ensure
that the PPAs address the mandated four (4) thematic areas and strictly
utilize and implement with the highest priority in terms of urgency or
impact as required in COA Circular No. 2012-02.
2. Conduct proper evaluation and monitoring of the implementation of the
Projects/Programs/Activities (PPAs) of the DRRMP and reassess the
necessity of pursuing the unimplemented programs to help build the
disaster resilience and preparedness of the public.

9.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor establish close coordination
with the GFPS and the implementing offices in GAD planning and
monitoring to ascertain the full implementation of GAD PPAs and the full
utilization of GAD Fund to effectively and efficiently address the gender
sensitivity issues and gender mainstreaming in the operations of the
Municipality.

10.13 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
a) Ensure strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002
on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, to forestall the
accumulation of unsettled balances;
b) Make a formal request with the Commission on Audit for the
writing-off of unliquidated cash advances of those who are already
deceased, and those separated from the service whose whereabouts are
unknown, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005;
c) Consider withholding the salary of those employees and officers still
connected with the LGU, to enforce full settlement thereof.
d) Require the Municipal Accountant to regularly issue Demand
Letters to AOs with unsettled cash advances, particularly those that
have already been outstanding for several years; and to see to it that
previous cash accountabilities of officers and employees are fully
settled/liquidated and duly accounted for in the books of accounts before
additional cash advances are granted.

11.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to deposit all the undeposited collections amounting to ₱345,301.78
in order to avoid possible loss or misuse and we further recommended to
fully adhere to Section 32 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting
System (MNGAS) on the Deposit of Collections.

12.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the HRMO-Designate
to ensure that seminars, trainings, workshops and other related activities
attended by the officials/employees are embodied in the CapDev Agenda
while taking into consideration the prudent use of the financial resources of
the Municipality.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the ledgers and cashbooks, to establish the accurate balances of Cash in Bank accounts. Correspondingly, oblige the Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements in order to determine entries in both the books and bank which need adjustment in order to ascertain the validity and accuracy of the balances of the Cash in Bank accounts as appearing in the Municipality’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR No monthly bank reconciliation statement was prepared and submitted by the Municipal Account.
observations.reason: Reiterated in observation No. 2, page 37 of this report.

observations.recommendation: For the proper management and handling of the PPE, we reiterate our prior year’s AAR recommendations that a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of December 31 of every year. The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the physical existence of the PPE and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets. Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments and to exact responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs; and a. Require the Supply Officer/Property Custodian to maintain/update the subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger cards, AREs and placing of property tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control and monitoring of the PPEs and for facility in identifying them. b. Lastly, we recommend that the Local Chief Executive request for authority to derecognize non-existing/missing PPE to the COA for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability. The procedure in the request for authority to derecognize is prescribed under COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: AAR No prior year’s 2020 Action. Verified on file that no inventory report was submitted by the Management. Conducted interview with the Municipal Treasurer if she already maintaining subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger cards, AREs and complete file of Acknowledgement Receipt for Property Receipt for Property and she answered in negative.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to look for other the documents, like journal vouchers, subsidiary ledgers, previous financial statements or other relevant records to be used as a basis in recording back the value of Public Infrastructures in the books accounts. Instruct also the Municipal Engineer to conduct actual inventory of the road networks of the municipality and provide the necessary information to the Municipal Accountant in order for the latter have a basis in recording the assets in the books of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR No 2020 action. Reviewed and verified the financial statements and observed that no adjustments made to the PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit the collections daily and intact in order to avoid accumulation of large amount of cash in the vault and prevent possible risk of loss or misuse of funds.
observations.managementAction: AAR Collections were not yet deposited daily. Verified during the post audit of collections and deposits.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to either withhold/deduct the retention money as prescribed by law from the payment to suppliers or require the latter to submit bank guaranty as a warranty for the delivered goods in order to protect the interest of the Management in case of manufacturing defect.
observations.managementAction: AAR No warranty was posted for purchased goods.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC Secretariat to post all Requests for Quotations and Awards for alternative mode of procurement above ₱ 50,000.00 in the PhilGEPS website in order to attain efficient and transparent procurement. Moreover, copies of printouts of these posting be attached to the disbursement vouchers as part of the supporting documents to serve as evidence that posting have been made. In case the website is not available due to system glitch, a copy of the screenshot be submitted in order to prove that there was an attempt or effort to post in the website.
observations.managementAction: AAR Requests for Quotations and alternative mode of procurement above P 50,000.00 were not yet posted in the PhilGEPS website. Verified during post audit.

observations.recommendation: In order to attain fair presentation of the account balances in the financial statements we recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant record the transfer of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF from General Fund to the Trust Fund books by recording the following entries: General Fund Dr. Subsidy to other Funds 2,614,321.25 Cr. Cash in Bank 2,614,321.25 Trust Fund Dr. Cash in Bank 2,614,321.25 Cr. Trust Liability – LDRRMF 2,614,321.25
observations.managementAction: AAR No transfer was made of the unspent LDRRMF to Trust Fund.
observations.reason: Reiterated in observation No. 11 page 49 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor (i) require the Acting Municipal Treasurer (MT) and the
Municipal Accountant to immediately reconcile their records as of December
31, 2021 and submit a reconciliation statement thereon and take-up the
necessary adjusting entries in their respective records; (ii) demand
from/require the former MT as well as from the Acting MT and other
accountable officers concerned to immediately submit to the Municipal
Accountant the source documents of the unrecorded disbursements and
deposits, and other book reconciling items, after which, the same shall be
submitted to the Audit Team for audit purposes; and (iii) make
representations with the bank to regularly furnish the Municipality with
copies of debit and credit memos of said reconciling items for recording in the
books of accounts;
b. Acting MT maintain complete and updated cashbooks for cash accounts of all
funds and regularly reconcile cashbook balances with accounting records and
monitor outstanding checks on a regular basis and notify the payees at least
one month before the check becomes stale in accordance with the provisions of
Section 63 of the LTOM; and
c. Municipal Accountant (i) regularly prepare the monthly BRS with complete
details and supporting documents for all reconciling items and submit to the
Audit Team the lacking BRS and copies of bank statements of two bank
accounts; (ii) record the reconciling items that need adjustment in the books
including the adjustment of CIB and payables balances due to stale checks;
and (iii) fast track the completion of the subsidiary ledgers of all cash
accounts, including the cash accountability of the former MT, and submit the
same to the Audit Team for verification and audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) exert more efforts to
complete the reconciliation of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds
accounts in all funds, determine the cause/s of disparity in the balances and record
the necessary adjustments in the books to present the correct balances in the
financial statements; and (ii) reconstruct the subsidiary ledgers of the Due from
Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts totaling ₱1,048,406.75 and
₱4,059,263.13, respectively.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant (a) serve written notices
to all concerned accountable officers requiring them to immediately settle
their outstanding cash advances and institute legal action against them, if
warranted;
b. Municipal Accountant:
 prepare the schedule of all receivables, unliquidated cash advances, and
fund transfers pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December
19, 2016 and COA Memorandum No. 2017-010 dated May 15, 2017
(RUCA-quarterly and SUCA-semestral); and
 immediately submit to the Audit Team the SLs for the cash advances of
officers and employees totaling ₱59,667,867.40 as of December 31, 2021;
and
c. LCE and the Municipal Accountant, in all instances, ensure that each
provision of COA Circular No. 97-002 are strictly observed and that all cash
advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of all supplies and
materials and prepare the report thereon, reconcile the results of the count
with available records in the Accounting and General Services Offices and
submit the RPCI to the Auditor pursuant to Section 124 of the MNGAS for
LGUs, Volume I;
b. Concerned offices to immediately submit the required distribution lists and
proof of receipts of the relief goods given to various beneficiaries and the fuel
consumption reports;
c. Municipal GSO (i) ensure that purchases and issuances of supplies and
materials are supported with duly signed RIS to establish accountability on
the part of the end-users, particularly those departments assigned to
distribute relief goods; and
d. Municipal Accountant (i) upon submission of the required summary report
and distribution list by the concerned offices, make the necessary adjustments
to reflect the correct balances of the Inventory accounts; and (ii) record the
supplies and materials to the inventory account upon purchase and recognize
expense only upon issuance thereof pursuant to the asset method of accounting
for recording purchases of supplies and materials as prescribed under Section
114 of the MNGAs for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor consider the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances as
provided under COA Circular No. 2020-06 dated January 31, 2020 to come up
with a reliable PPE balance;
b. Inventory Committee (i) immediately complete the Report on the Physical
Count of PPE and the Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road
Network System and reconcile these reports with the Property Cards, PPE
Ledger Cards, Road Network Ledger Cards and other accounting records;
and (ii) submit a copy thereof to the Audit Team;
c. Municipal Accountant (i) reconcile the year-end balances of the PPE and the
differences of total acquisition costs of PPE account balances reported in the
financial statements and in the PPE schedules; and (ii) recompute and provide
for the correct accumulated depreciation of the Road Networks account; and
d. Municipal Engineer (i) submit a complete report on the costs, description and
specifications and cost segregation of the road components to the Municipal
Accountant as basis for disclosures in the NTFS; and (ii) regularly assess each
road component for impairment.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant investigate the variances
between the balances per subsidiary schedule and per FS, reconstruct the
subsidiary ledgers of Construction in Progress accounts and thereafter make the
necessary adjustments in the books.

We recommended that the a) Municipal Accountant immediately complete the
reconciliation of their records on Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth and
provide subsidiary schedules for balances totaling ₱6,949,645.40 to facilitate
remittance thereof to the concerned agencies and to determine and record the
necessary adjustments in the books; and b) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer ensure that funds collected by the municipal government in behalf of other
government agencies are fully remitted within the prescribed due dates to avoid
incurrence of interests and penalties.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace, locate and recover the
documents related to the above accounts and record the details obtained in the
respective subsidiary ledgers and submit to the Audit Team the lacking subsidiary
ledgers and schedules pursuant to Section 114(2) of P.D. 1445 to substantiate the
account balances reported in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) thoroughly review the
Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) before approving them; and (b) ensure the accuracy
and consistency of the details of the JEVs that are recorded in the journals and that
the financial transactions are timely recorded in the journals in the same month they
occurred.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Accountant maintain separate books
of accounts and prepare separate financial statements for each economic enterprise
and public utility as required in Sections 105, 107 and 110 of the MNGAS for LGUs,
Volume I, so that adequate information as to the amount of assets, liabilities and
equity, cash flows and results of operations will be available for Management’s
decision making and monitoring purposes; and (b) Acting Municipal Treasurer
deposit all collections promptly and intact with the authorized government depository
bank.

We also recommended that the management ensure that collections related to
the economic enterprises of the Municipality are collected by the Office of the
Municipal Treasurer and that all Collecting Officers are properly authorized and
bonded in accordance with law to safeguard the municipal government’s funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget
Officer (a) submit to the Auditor the revised CY 2021 SCBAA and SAAOB after
identifying and verifying the reconciling items; and (b) reconcile their records on
allotments and obligations monthly, verify resulting discrepancies and submit the
required budget reports and reconciliation statement within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Acting MT and the other
members of the Local Finance Committee submit to the Audit Team the detailed
computation/schedules and related documents supporting the declared savings for
various SBudgets of CYs 2020 and 2021 for verification and audit to determine
whether the declared of sources of fund totaling ₱194,629,171.66 have valid basis and
in accordance with the provisions of Sections 321 and 336 of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) immediately
reconcile their records on DRRMF in coordination with the MDRRM Officer
to present the correct amounts in the NTFS, SCBAA, SAAOB and in the
Quarterly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRM Fund;
b. Municipal Accountant (i) immediately reconstruct the SLs for all transfers of
unexpended DRRMF to the STF by year of transfer and record the necessary
adjustments in the books to reflect the correct balance of the Trust Liabilities-
DRRMF account and identify the amount to be transferred to the TF books
and LDRRMF STF bank account, considering the deficiencies noted in audit;
and
c. Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer cause the
immediate transfer of the unexpended DRRMF to the LDRRMF STF bank
account.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor, thru the MDRRM Officer, Municipal Accountant and
MBO, (i) ensure that the 30% of the DRRMF is appropriated for the QRF as a
stand-by fund for relief and recovery programs and discontinue the practice of
realigning/transferring the said fund to the implementing offices’ separate
budget; (ii) see that the unexpended LDRRMF balances are released and
utilized for new expenditure items only upon submission of a revised AIP duly
approved by the local sanggunian and the Mayor upon recommendation of
the LDRRMC in accordance with the approved LDRRMP without need of an
appropriation ordinance;
b. Municipal Accountant and the MBO reconcile their records and recompute
the total obligations incurred from the utilization of the CY 2021 QRF to
obtain the correct the amount of unexpended balance to be transferred to the
STF;
c. MDRRM Officer (i) ensure that the PPAs funded by STF are identified
separately from the PPAs funded by the DRRMF Continuing CO in the GF
for proper monitoring and reporting purposes; and
d. Municipal Accountant, MBO and MDRRM Officer stop the practice of
charging ineligible expenses to the DRRMF and henceforth ensure that the
utilization of DRRMF is in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular
No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No.
2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No.
2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) render
justification, duly supported with documentation, on the noted deficiencies and
inconsistencies in the procurement of goods and services for COVID-19 and
DRRM related PPAs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, (a) thru the Municipal
Accountant, review the Memoranda of Agreement pertaining to the aforesaid
unutilized balances of completed projects and communicate with the source agencies
for the immediate return of such balances; and (b) cause the refund of the remaining
amount of BGCM to the national treasury.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) direct the Municipal
Accountant to immediately submit the lacking documents to support the validity and
legality of the fund transfer to the NGO; and (b) henceforth, strictly comply with the
guidelines laid down by COA Circular No. 2007-001 and the documentary
requirements enumerated in Item 2.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the fund
transfer to NGOs.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary
adjustment to correct the entries made in recording the fund transfers to the NGO
totaling ₱3,000,000.00.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator (MPDC) fast track the implementation of the infrastructure
projects and make sure that all projects are strictly monitored and
implemented in accordance with plans and schedules;
b. MPDC and the Local Development Council establish priorities during
investment programming and include in the Annual Investment Program only
those programs and projects which are feasible, specifically identified and
with resource requirements that are based on duly approved POWs and
Detailed Cost Estimates;
c. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer, in coordination with
the Municipal Engineer, reconcile their records to determine the correct total
actual cost incurred for each project;
d. Municipal Engineer (i) ensure that the status report of all infrastructure
projects of the municipality is complete and supported with SWA and other
documents required for technical evaluation and inspection; and (ii)
henceforth, strictly comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2013-
004; and
e. Municipal Accountant submit the lacking schedule of expenditures or CIP
ledgers for the aforementioned projects as well as the lacking documents
supporting payments for the hardware materials.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as head of the GAD Focal Point
System (GFPS), require the GFPS to (i) see that the Annual GPB incorporated in the
Annual Budget is reviewed and endorsed by the DILG, as well as any changes
thereto, to ensure gender responsiveness of GAD PPAs; (ii) accomplish the GAD AR
using the guide prescribed under PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01,
especially in accomplishing Columns 6, 8 and 9 to facilitate proper evaluation of
PPAs; and (iii) strictly monitor and ensure the timely and efficient planning and
implementation of GAD PPAs to achieve optimal utilization of GAD budget.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Mayor, thru the Acting Municipal
Treasurer, enforce the full settlement of the disallowances covered with COEs; and
(b) Municipal Accountant (i) reconstruct and immediately submit to the Audit Team
the SLs for unsettled audit disallowances totaling ₱1,420,937.98; and (ii) make the
necessary adjustments in the books for the disallowances erroneously recorded in the
General Fund instead of the Special Education Fund.

We reiterate our previous recommendations for the Management to:
Prepare and submit copy of the RPCPPE to the Auditor in prescribed format, not later than January 31 of each year;
Ensure that the RPCPPE reflects comprehensively and cumulatively the PPE of the LGU as of the date of the conduct of physical count; and
In the preparation of the RPCPPE:
Strictly follow the prescribed format
Make sure that all columns are properly and completely filled out to ensure that all items are properly identified and accounted for; and
Ensure that the RPCPPE is signed and certified correct by all the members of the Inventory Committee and approved by the Local Chief Executive.

We recommended that Management:
Cause the submission of the RDS indicating the names of recipients and their signatures for evaluation of the Audit Team, without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance, when warranted; and
Ensure that all claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documentation to protect the interests of the government and to prevent unwarranted disbursements of public funds.
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit collections promptly and intact as prescribed by the existing regulations.

We recommended that Management ensure that Cashbooks maintained by the AOs conform strictly with the prescribed format and instructions provided in the LTOM, such that the required columns (for date, reference, and balance) are appropriately and completely filled-up. Likewise, ensure that the required AO’s certification at the end of each month in Collector’s Cashbooks are complied with.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure to update immediately its detailed SLs; and
Strictly comply with the required monthly reconciliation between Cashbook balances and subsidiary accounting records/SL balances.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure that additional CAs are drawn only after the liquidation of previous CAs;
Strictly and religiously monitor the submission of Liquidation Reports/Report of Disbursements for the timely accounting thereof in order to avoid undue accumulation of outstanding cash advances; and
Ensure compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations on the grant and utilization of CAs.

We recommended that Management ensure that CAs pertaining to PCF (establishment and replenishments) shall be recorded in the Cashbook-PCF and CAs for other regular cash advances (salaries, wages, allowances, etc.) shall be recorded in the Cashbook-Cash Advances. Moreover, ensure that no same transactions are recorded erroneously and redundantly in these Cashbooks in order to uphold the reliability of the balances reflected therein.

We recommended that Management:
Immediately refrain from granting cash advances to elected officials for purposes other than for official travelling expenses, cash advances with specific purpose indicated in the DV at the time of grant, and cash advances to a permanently appointed official not designated as disbursing officer; and
Prospectively, ensure strict compliance of the LGU with COA Circular No. 97-002 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to avoid future suspension/disallowance in audit.

We recommended that Management reconcile the Cash and Cash Equivalent to Cash Flow Statement and Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/ (Deficit) and disclose the said reconciliation on the face of Cash Flow Statement or in the Notes to FS in compliance with Sections 56 and 29 of IPSASs 2-Cash Flow Statements.

We recommended that Management reconcile the actual amounts presented in the SCBAA and the actual amounts presented in the FS; and disclose the said reconciliation on the face of the SCBAA or in the notes to FS in compliance with Section 47 of IPSASs 24, thus, providing the users of the FS of the beneficial information that could be derived from such reconciliation.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer adhere to the prescribed period of submission as required in Section 41 (2) of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended that Management:
Enjoin the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of inventory and submit to the Audit Team the required RPCI in its prescribed format not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively; and
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Property and Supply Officer to maintain complete records (supplies ledger cards and stocks cards, respectively) and intensify reconciliation of these records against the balances of Inventory accounts in the FS to ascertain its reliability and accuracy.

We recommended that Management maintain SLs and supporting schedules for the Due to NGAs account with complete information and specific breakdown of sub-accounts to facilitate reconciliation of accounts and serve as a monitoring tool for the implementation of projects and other related activities as well.

We recommended that Management:
Strictly comply with its obligation to deduct, withhold and remit taxes on or before its prescribed due dates as enjoined under RMO No. 23-2014 and existing BIR rules and regulations; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant/Municipal Treasurer to refund the penalties and charges paid out of government funds amounting to ₱22,468.59.

We recommended that Management:
Optimize the utilization of the 20 per cent DF to fully attain the desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes essential to the promotion of the general welfare of its constituents;
Adopt a well-defined strategy to enhance the present monitoring and review activities during the planning, procurement, contract implementation and program evaluation phase, in order for the planners and implementers to continuously monitor the timely implementation, so that immediate appropriate and corrective actions could be done on noted gaps along the way; and
Realign the unexpended balances for completed PPAs to similar programs or revert to unappropriated surplus under 20 per cent DF for appropriation to other developmental projects and activities that would respond to the needs of the community.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure that the unexpended balance of QRF, DRRMF-MOOE and savings from CO at year-end will be transferred to the Special Trust Fund account and will be recorded under the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account; and
Review and reconcile the amounts and entries made in the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account and the details disclosed in the Notes to FS as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 to report accurately the transactions and information for the LDRRMF.

We recommended that Management:
Submit to the audit team copy of SB resolution for the above-mentioned disbursement for verification purposes; and
Ensure that transactions are supported with SB resolution authorizing the LCE to enter into such agreements.

We recommended that Management:
Adopt the proper procurement process of services to promote fair competition in compliance with the R-IRR of R.A. No. 9184; and
Explain the basis for the direct contracting of procurement.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure a gender issues/gaps responsive projects/activities identified for funding supported by sex disaggregated data for the attainment of gender equality and women’s empowerment; and
Refrain from incurrence of expenses that cannot be charged to the GAD Budget, to avoid disallowance in the future.

We recommended that Management require the conduct of physical inventory of the breeding stocks, prepare the required inventory report and reconcile the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments in the books, if any.

We recommended that Management ensure that RAAOs per function/program/project, allotment class, current/continuing appropriations and fund should be prepared and maintained by the LGU for the timely monitoring of appropriations, allotments, and obligations, ultimately, preventing the incurrence of obligations incurred in excess of allotments received or approved appropriations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to restore the stale
checks of ₱27,300.97 to the Cash in Bank - Local Currency, Current
Account, and exert extra effort to identify the unaccounted balances of
₱42,695.16 and ₱707,768.12. Further, instruct the Municipal Accountant
to maintain subsidiary ledger for each bank account to facilitate monitoring
and verification of the cash account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
1. The Municipal Accountant to exert effort to compute and provide
depreciation on depreciable PPEs especially those with available data
necessary for computing depreciation.
2. The Municipal Treasurer/Inventory Committee to conduct annual
physical count of PPEs and prepare RPCPPE to support the PPEs
presented in the Statement of Financial Position and help the Municipal
Accountant on data gathering needed for computation of depreciation.

We recommended the following courses of action:
a) The Municipal Engineering Office:
• Provide the Municipal Accountant and the General Service
Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of
road component for road projects, as well as the report on the
repair/maintenance of each local road network;
b) The Municipal Treasurer/Inventory Committee
• Conduct a Physical Count of the Road Network (RPCLRN)
and submit the corresponding report thereon.
• Maintain the Local Road Network Property Cards for each
roads and local road network;
c) The Municipal Accountant
• Reconcile the report from the Office of the Municipal Engineer
and the accounting records and prepare the necessary
adjusting entry for the discrepancies. Prepare the adjusting
entry to correct the error committed,
• Maintain subsidiary records for road and road components.
• Prepare lapsing schedule for the depreciation of the
depreciable components of the Local Road Network.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to provide adequate disclosures of Inventories and PPEs in
consonance to IPSAS 12 and 17, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
(1) Supply Officer or Municipal Treasurer to-
a. Demand and impose the submission of the RISs and Distribution
Sheets to support the use, consumption, or distribution of
inventories of ₱2,011,648.68 from the offices concerned; and
b. Conduct weekly consolidation of RIS through the preparation of
the SSMI to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for the
appropriate recording.
(2) Municipal Accountant, to charge inventories as expenses only when
supported with SSMIs, RISs and Distribution Sheets.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to disclose the details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in
the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with Item 5.1.16 of the COA
Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local School Board
to annually prepare SEF Budget in accordance to Republic Act No. 7160 and
DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 and 2.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Fill the vacant MDRRMO position in order to:
a. Ensure implementation of programmed
Projects/Programs/Activities to effectively increase the level of
preparedness from the occurrence of natural calamities and
mitigate the risk of damage in case of disaster, and
b. Prepare the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Investment Plan for the DRRM programs, annually.
2. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources
and Utilization of the DRRMF and submit the same to the Audit Team
on or before the 15th day after the end of each month. Henceforth,
strictly follow the accounting and reporting guidelines for Local
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) of Local
Government Units provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002, dated
September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers complete with relevant
supporting documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 and to keenly
observe the deadline set on the submission of monthly trial balances and other
financial reports. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor should
evaluate the necessity of increasing the support staff of the Municipal
Accounting Office to cope up with the required accounting reports.

We recommended that the LCE require the collectors and the Municipal
Treasurer to prepare the monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable
Forms (RAAF) and Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable
forms CRAAF) respectively, pursuant to Sec. 37 of COA Circular No. 92-382
in order to facilitate effective monitoring and control of the Accountable
Forms of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MSWDO to require
applicants of AICS program to submit the necessary documents as basis of
her assessment/evaluation in the grant of financial assistance, and direct the
Private Secretary to submit payrolls of beneficiaries of AICS program in the
liquidation of her cash advances. Furthermore, direct the Municipal
Accountant to accept and process liquidation reports for AICS Program,
only on those that are completely and properly supported with documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the LGU employees to
refrain from resorting to reimbursements as mode of payment of supplies
and meals/snacks for their activities. Instead establish a petty cash fund
sufficient for the non-recurring, emergency and petty expenses of the LGU
for one month, or else, enjoin all heads of offices to resort to the regular
disbursement process.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to submit the approved DTRs of all recipients of the AHDP and
the accomplishment reports of the BHWs for the period September 15, 2020
to December 15, 2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
1. Local Development Council, in coordination with the MPDO, to
prudently formulate annual investment plans to ensure the
programming of only those 20% DF PPAs that are attainable/
implementable;
2. Members of the Local Finance Committee to carefully evaluate the
budget proposals by various offices during the conduct of budget
technical hearings, and coordinate with other government agencies to
avoid duplications of 20% DF PPAs and budget deficiencies;
3. MPDC to coordinate with the Municipal Health Officer, Municipal
Engineer, Municipal Budget Officer and other officials concerned in the
monitoring and evaluation of the implementation of 20% DF in
accordance to the approved investment plans.

1.5 We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
instruct the Municipal Accountant to record all payments for the on-going
constructions as a debit to the Construction in Progress account and reclassify
the total amount to a particular asset account only after the completion of the
construction in order to accurately present the balances of the accounts in the
financial statements.

2.7 We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile
the ledgers and cashbooks, to establish the accurate beginning balances of
Cash in Bank accounts. Correspondingly, oblige the Municipal Accountant to
prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements in order to determine entries
in both the books and bank which may need adjustment, thus, the validity and
accuracy of the balances of the Cash in Bank accounts as appearing in the
Municipality’s financial statements could be ascertained.

3.4 We recommended and management agreed that Local Chief Executive require
the Budget Officer to provide in the municipal’s budget the aid to Barangay of
not less than P 1,000.00 for every barangay. Likewise, instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to release the said aid to Barangays for the latter to use the same as
an additional fund to finance their programs and projects that will benefit the
constituents.

4.5 We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
instruct the Municipal Accountant to deduct retention money from payment
to contractors or require them to post other form of authorized security as
prescribed by law in order to protect the Municipality’s interest in case of
structural defects in the construction of infrastructure projects, and to assure
that the contractor satisfactorily perform his work on time.

5.7 We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
instruct the Municipal Treasurer to refrain from approving employees’ loan
applications and deducting from the salaries of the corresponding loan
amortizations to private lending institution in order to meet the minimum net
take home pay as required under Section 49 of General Appropriations Act of
2021, thus the employees could possibly meet the basic family needs before the
next payroll period and the government will not act as collecting agent of the
private companies, hence additional expense of the government will be
avoided.

6.5 We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
instruct the Property Officer and Inspection Officer to indicate all the
information in the Inspection and Acceptance Report to ensure that the
supplier complied with the obligations embodied in the contract and enable
the Management to determine its proper and immediate action.

7.4 We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
instruct the Local Finance Committee and other local officials to comply with
the reporting requirements as prescribed by the law in order to inform the
stakeholders of the relevant information and promote transparency and
accountability.

8.4 We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
require the Municipal Assessor to submit the required documents for the
purchase of the lot in order that the Municipal Accountant could submit the
same to the Office of the Audit for proper evaluation and determination of the
legality of the said transactions.

9.6 We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to procure the supplies especially the
medicines and medical supplies not exceeding the two-month requirement to
protect the interest of the municipality if ever there is a price decrease during
the year. Likewise, require the BAC to enhance their procurement planning in
order to come up with proper scheduling of procurement of medicines and
medical supplies so that only the two-month requirement at a time will be
purchased.

10.6 We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to require the winning bidder to post
the proper form of performance security to guarantee faithful performance of
the latter of its obligation based on the provisions embodied in the contract
and to avoid any damage it may cause to the management.

11.4 In order to attain fair presentation of the account balances in the financial
statements we recommended that Management require the Municipal
Accountant to record the transfer of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF
from General Fund to the Trust Fund books by recording the following
entries:

General Fund Trust Fund
Dr. Subsidy to other Funds 2,773,820.87 Dr. Cash in Bank 2,773,820.87
Cr. Cash in Bank 2,773,820.87 Cr. Trust Liability –LDRRMF 2,773,820.87

We recommended that the Management instruct all Department Heads to process all unpaid transactions before year-end so that all will be paid or obligated before year-end. Also, require the Budget Officer see to it that no prior year’s expenditure to be charged to current year’s appropriations in order to observe proper matching of expenses against appropriation.

Further, we recommended that Management may designate employee who will be in charged in the issuance of trip ticket and monitoring the fuel consumption of the Municipality in order to avoid wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption.

Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer ensure that the payments be supported with accomplished payroll and payments are duly received by the payees in order to support the liquidation of the cash advance granted to her.

Also, require the Municipal Accountant strict adherence to the requirements prescribed in the aforementioned laws and regulations and ensure that all necessary documents are attached to the vouchers upon submission to the auditor in order for the latter to verify and ascertain the eligibility of the suppliers and any noted deficiencies/inconsistencies will be immediately informed to Management for its appropriate action, thus ascertaining the validity and propriety of the disbursement as a whole.

observations.recommendation:
For the proper management and handling of the PPE, we reiterated our prior year’s recommendations and Management agreed to:

 Conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of December 31 of every year.  The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the physical existence of the PPE and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets.  Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments and to exact responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs; and

Maintain/update the subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger cards, AREs and placing of property tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control and monitoring of the PPEs and for facility in identifying them.

We likewise recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Engineer conduct actual inventory of the road networks of the municipality and provide the necessary information in order for the Municipal Accountant to record all these assets in the books of the municipality.

We likewise recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant look into documents like ledgers, journals, bank statements and Treasurer’s cashbook to help her in establishing the beginning balances of Cash in Bank accounts. The BRS is a tool to enhance the internal control over cash accountability and address immediate errors in terms of recording bank transactions.

In case that there is no designated disbursing officer, cash advance should be granted to the Municipal Treasurer in order that the funds of the municipality will not be exposed to possible loss, misuse or misappropriations.

observations.recommendation:
In order to comply with the provisions of RA 9003 and achieve the state’s policy of adopting a systematic, comprehensive and ecological solid waste management program we recommended and Management agreed that:

The Legislative Department formulate ordinance regarding the stricter implementation of waste segregation in each household so that not only the wastes from the public market are being segregated; and 

The Management identify location for proposed sanitary landfill in order to eventually construct its own facility and contract with other LGU for waste disposal will no longer be necessary, thus, cost for garbage disposal is expected to decrease.
observations.managementAction: No Management Action.
observations.reason: Verified during interview with the concerned employees that pertinent provisions of RA 9003 were not yet complied by the Management.

We also recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer maintain and regularly update the Registries of Appropriation, Allotment and Balances for PS, MOOE, CO and Statutory Contractual Obligation for all funds of the municipality.
