observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements using the balances per books and bank as the Unadjusted Balances; b. Identify and reconcile the unaccounted reconciling items, and record the necessary adjusting entry; c. Cause the closure of the account with the Philippine National Bank, and transfer any remaining balance to accredited government depository account; d. Draw Journal Entry Voucher to revert to cash the value of stale checks or replace the same, whichever is appropriate; e. Prepare a subsidiary ledger for each depository bank account; f. To verify the reconciling item that has existed for several years being the existing Municipal Accountant who have the responsibility.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: See Related Findings in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately coordinate with LBP-Catarman to inquire on relevant information of the Cash in Bank Savings Account and trace corresponding document as basis for its recording in the books.
observations.managementAction: AAR Preparation of CY 2019 on-going
observations.reason: The Municipal accountant to record or include the amount as Cash in Bank-LCCA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Send demand letters for the immediate liquidation/settlement of officers and employees' long outstanding cash advances; b. Cause the withholding of salary of its employees to liquidate their outstanding cash advances as prescribed in Section 5.9 of COA Circular 97-002; c. Issue a certification that previous cash advance has been liquidated or with no outstanding cash advance for every cash advance granted to employees; d. Refrain from giving additional cash advance to officers and employees unless the previous cash advance is first settled or proper accounting thereof is made; and e. Locate source documents to at least identify the status of employment or the offices of the employees granted cash advances with no details.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: The Advances to Officers and Employees has remain unsettled to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the discrepancies between Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, and make adjustments in the books for those with adequate corroborating documents to present the correct balances of the affected accounts. Henceforth, exercise care in recording and classifying the LGU’s financial transactions to ensure that the balances of reciprocal accounts are at all times the same.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant not yet reconciled the balances of the accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee to immediately conduct the physical inventory of its depreciable Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) and submit the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) to the Auditor. Also, direct the Municipal Accountant to compute the deprecation of PPEs recognized in CY 2019, and exert effort to trace and compute for the depreciation of recognized depreciable PPEs in the prior year/s.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: See the same accounting findings in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to trace the unaccounted fund transfers, disposed of the same according to its purpose, or return unexpended balances. Also, to submit the liquidation documents totaling P42,300.00 under Check No. 120710.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: See Related Findings in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant: a. to exert efforts to trace records/documents concerning these accounts; b. confirm with concerned agencies/personalities, and draw JEV to make necessary adjusting entries; or c. verify further the dormancy of receivable and fund transfer accounts for 10 years or more to be allowed write-off under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: See Related Findings in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Office to coordinate and come up with an agreement that cut-off date for submission of unpaid claims prior to the close of accounting book of the accounts, be established, to allow these claims to be properly obligated and paid even after the cut-off date. Also, for the Municipal Accountant to observe proper recording of prior year obligated claims, which must be debited to appropriate Accounts Payable and credit to Cash in Bank.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: See Related Findings in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Health Officer to establish a program/plan addressed to implement the mandates of the Per Family Payment Rate fund transfer.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: The MHO submitted her budget proposal only on CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should direct the following officers: a. The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to observe proper development project classification and refrain from funding out of the 20% DF non-developmental projects. Henceforth, comply with Item 3.0 of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017. b. The Municipal Budget Officer to be prudent in approving Obligation Requests so as to refrain from utilizing the 20% DF for expenditures not allowed to be charged therein. Henceforth, observe Item 4.0 of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017. c. The Municipal Accountant to refrain from certifying in the Disbursement Voucher, expenditures inappropriately funded out of the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: See findings on 20% DF in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Prepare and cause to be submitted by the Local DRRM Officer, to OCD, DILG and COA, the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRM Funds; b. Revert to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, the CY 2013 unexpended balance of DRRM funds totaling P1,163,758.00; c. Coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer in determining the actual utilization of Local DRRM Fund, and thereafter transfer to Trust Liabilities-DRRM in the Trust Fund books, the CY 2019 unexpended balance of 30% QRF and the MOOE of the 70% allocation; d. Report the unexpended balance of Local DRRM Fund in the Notes to the Financial Statement using the format prescribed in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. Likewise, for the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain and update Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for DRRM funds.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: See Related Findings in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Environmental and Natural Resources Officer to: a. Submit the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan to the National Solid Waste Management Commission (NSWMC) for evaluation and approval; b. Prepare a timetable for the implementation of the solid waste management program; c. Develop a strategy every year which will contribute to achieving the SWM Plan for 10 years; and d. Utilize the amount appropriated for SWM Programs.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: The management did not acted on the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended further for the Municipal Mayor to require the Persons Liable in the Notice of Suspension or the Notice of Disallowance to settle their audit suspension or disallowance. We recommended further for the Municipal Accountant to send copy of the Notices to the Persons liable for their information on their unsettled suspensions/disallowance.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: There was an on-going refund. The total amount refunded as of December 31, 2020 amounted to P200,000.00

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare Subsidiary Ledger for each person liable and draw JEV to record the disallowances with Notice of Finality of Decision as receivables in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: A duly Notice of Finality of Decision (NFD) received by it was not yet recorded by the accountant

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive immediately institutionalize its GAD Focal Point System through issuances appointing officials and employees of concerned agencies, as members of the Executive Committee, Technical Working Group and the Secretariat. Also, if institutionalized, to observe the proper preparation and timely submission of GAD Plan and Budget and corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report.
observations.managementAction: AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all expenditures are recorded for the period when they are incurred in compliance with the provision of Section 350 of RA 7160. We further recommend that a cut-off date for the submission of unpaid claims prior to the close of the fiscal year be established to allow the obligation and recording of expenses during the year.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: See Related Findings in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to stamp PAID all disbursement vouchers including the supporting documents upon release of payment to prevent the double payment and other irregular use of the same on similar transaction.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: According to the Municipal Accountant, some Offices’ file copies of paid vouchers were stamped "Paid" but the recommendation was not implemented throughout the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Assign a full-pledged MDRRMO to identify, monitor and implement programs/projects activities in order to effectively increase the level of preparedness of the municipality from the occurrence of natural calamities and mitigate the risk of damage in case of calamities; b. Direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to determine the unutilized capital outlay from prior years on the amount transferred to Trust Fund and shall be made continuing in the General Fund books; c. Instruct the MDRRMO to include the utilization of the unexpended MOOE and QRF of prior years for DRRM which is under the Trust Liability – DRRM, in the LDRRMFIP for programs/projects/activities related to disaster management and prevention; d. Require the MDRRMO to prepare and timely submit the duly certified Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, in compliance with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and e. Require the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended balances of LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: MDRRMO designate lacks experience for the position.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to appropriate the 20% Development Fund to priority development projects and refrain from using the fund to pay salaries. We further recommended to observe the guidelines provided under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DILG and DBM in the preparation and utilization of funds. Likewise, assure that all programs/projects/activities which would redound to the immediate benefit of the constituents be prioritized for implementation.
observations.managementAction: AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU refrain from funding salaries of personnel from the Solid Waste Management Program and observe the guidelines provided under R.A. No. 9003. We further recommend that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWB) implement the monitoring and evaluation system to determine municipality’s compliance on the SWMP and update/review the SWMP to improve measures and effective implementation.
observations.managementAction: AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive appropriate the required amount of at least 5% of its annual budget to GAD programs; thoroughly prepare the GAD Plan and Budget and submits the same to the PPDO of the Provincial Government; and ensure that GAD fund is utilized in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated January 12, 2013.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: A duly endorsed GAD Plan (GPB) was not submitted. There are still utilizations from GAD that are not included in the GPB.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate that the Local Chief Executive to compel the inventory committee to conduct the complete physical count of all PPE and prepare and submit the RPCPPE. Likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to maintain and update the respective property records to support the reported balances. The Committee must prepare the required supporting documents and follow the disposal procedures quoted herein.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: There was an on-going preparation of lists of PPE, preparatory for depreciating these PPEs. The assessor is in the process of collecting necessary documents. Ownership was not yet transferred to the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly adhere to Sections 18 and 19 of PPSAS 12 and the specific provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated April 15, 2014, on the accounting and reporting of inventories and the LDRRMF. Likewise, require the submission of the lacking documents from different barangays including the distribution list of relief goods to affected families and report on receipt and distribution of in-kind donations received from DSWD RO VIII and UEP. Moreover, require the preparation of adjusting entries, reconciliation of Accounting and LDRRMO records, and the immediate transfer of unexpended LDRRMF balance as of December 31, 2016 in compliance with Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: The report prepared by the Municipal Budget Officer is not in the prescribed format thus with incomplete data.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly adhere to Sections 18 and 19 of PPSAS 12 and COA Circular No. 2014-002 on the accounting and reporting of inventories and the LDRRMF. Likewise, require the submission of the lacking documents from different barangays including the distribution list of relief goods to affected families and report on receipt and distribution of in-kind donations received from DSWD RO VIII and UEP. Moreover, require the preparation of adjusting entries, reconciliation of Accounting and LDRRMO records, and the immediate transfer of unexpended LDRRMF balance as of December 31, 2016 in compliance with Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: See related findings in Part II of this report. The staff is still collecting the distribution list of 405 packs of rice purchased.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should comply with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and Section 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, to demand for the necessary documents to facilitate the processing or transfer of ownership or title of the property in the name of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: Ownership was not yet transferred to the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that transfer of funds to implementing agencies be supported with the necessary documents as required under Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 4(6) of PD 1445. Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the reclassification of the following: a) Funds transferred to Dep-Ed and Senior Citizen Organization totaling P80,000.00 and P20,000.00, respectively, which were recorded under the Due from Officers and Employees (123) account should be reclassified to Due from NGAs (136) and Due from NGOs/PO’s (139), respectively, for proper presentation in the financial statements; b) Funds transferred to barangays totaling P300,000.00, which was recorded as outright expense should be reclassified to Due from LGUs (138) account, and credit the same upon submission of the liquidation reports and documents by the implementing agencies.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: There was an on-going reclassification of the account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should comply with the provisions of RA 656. It should insure its insurable properties with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS to secure thereof from acts of nature, accidents and man-made calamities and protect the agency from possible economic setbacks.
observations.managementAction: AAR
observations.reason: LGU properties are subject for renovation/major repair.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
a. Local Chief Executive (LCE) (i) require the Acting Municipal Treasurer and
the Municipal Accountant to immediately reconcile their records as of
December 31, 2020 and submit a reconciliation statement thereon and take-up
the necessary adjusting entries in their respective records; (ii) demand
from/require the former Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit to the
Municipal Accountant the disbursement vouchers, obligation requests and
supporting documents of the unrecorded disbursements and withdrawals,
after which, the same shall be submitted to the Auditor for audit purposes;
b. Acting Municipal Treasurer strictly observe the provisions of Section 63 of the
LTOM on the required maintenance of complete and updated cashbooks in all
accounts of all funds and the reconciliation of cashbook balances with
subsidiary accounting records as this is an important control tool for cash;
and
c. Municipal Accountant (i) immediately prepare and submit to the Audit Team
the lacking BRS and copies of bank statements for the PNB account; (ii)
further investigate and provide details and supporting documents for all
reconciling items in the BRS and thereafter make the necessary adjustments in
the books; and (iii) fast track the completion of the subsidiary ledgers of all
cash accounts, including the cash accountability of the former Municipal
Treasurer, and submit the same to the Audit Team for verification and audit.

We recommended that the (a) Acting Municipal Treasurer (i) transfer the
remaining SET collections for CY 2020 to the SEF depository account; (ii) prepare
separate reports of collections for RPT and SET; (iii) automatically deposit the
proceeds of the additional 1% RPT accruing to the SEF depository account, in
compliance with Section 272 of R.A. 7160 and Section 64(A) of the LTOM; and (iv)
see that only one real property tax receipt is issued to cover the payment of basic and
additional tax accruing to the SEF; and (b) Municipal Accountant review the related
reports to determine the cause/s of the discrepancy between the RPT and SET
collections amounting to ₱35,430.02 and adjust the affected accounts, as appropriate.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the reciprocal
accounts, determine the cause/s of disparity in balances and record the necessary
adjustments in the books to present the correct balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant reconcile the Due from LEEs account in the GF-
Regular and record the necessary adjustments in the books to present the
correct balances of the Due from LEEs under GF-Regular and Other Payables
under the MEEDO account; and
b. Municipal Treasurer immediately transfer the balances owed by the MEEDO
account to the GF-Regular and for the succeeding months, simultaneously
transfer funds from MEEDO to GF-Regular on the month the remittances are
due for payment.

We recommended that the:
a. LCE, (i) through the Municipal Accountant send written notices to all
concerned accountable officers requiring immediate settlement of their
outstanding cash advances and institute legal action against them, if
warranted; and (ii) require the former Municipal Treasurer to submit to the
Audit Team the DV and supporting documents for JEV No. 20-08-4810
covering cash advance amounting to ₱3,960,000.00.
b. Municipal Accountant:
 prepare the schedule of all receivables, unliquidated cash advances, and
fund transfers pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December
19, 2016 and COA Memorandum No. 2017-010 dated May 15, 2017
(RUCA-quarterly and SUCA-semestral); and
 immediately submit to the Audit Team the SLs for the cash advances of
officers and employees totaling ₱18,334,462.75 as of December 31, 2020;
and
c. LCE and the Municipal Accountant, in all instances, ensure that each
provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 are strictly observed and that all cash
advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year.

We recommended that the:
a. Inventory Committee, GSO and the Municipal Accountant exert efforts to
complete the reconciliation of the Report on the Physical Count of PPE,
Property Cards, PPE ledger balances and accounting records, taking into
consideration the noted deficiencies in audit;
b. Inventory Committee (i) immediately complete the physical inventory of the
Municipality’s road network system; (ii) prepare the Report on the Physical
Count of the Local Road Network System to be reconciled with the Road
Network Ledger Cards and other accounting records; and (iii) submit a copy
thereof to the Audit Team;
c. Municipal Engineer (i) submit to the Municipal Accountant a complete report
on the costs, description and specifications and cost segregation of the road
components; and (ii) regularly assess each road component for impairment; and
d. Municipal Accountant reconcile the differences of total acquisition costs of
PPE account balances reported in year-end Statement of Financial Position
and in the lapsing schedules.

We recommended that the a) Municipal Accountant immediately reconcile the
records on Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth and provide subsidiary
schedules for balances totaling ₱2,911,433.67 to facilitate remittance to the concerned
agencies; b) HRM Officer ensure that the GSIS/Pag-IBIG/PhilHealth numbers of
employees are updated in their records in order to facilitate the timely remittance of
their corresponding contributions, taxes and loan amortizations; and c) Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer ensure that funds collected by the
municipal government in behalf of other government agencies are remitted to them
fully and within the prescribed due dates to avoid incurrence of interests and
penalties.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) trace, locate and recover
the documents related to the above accounts and record the details obtained in the
respective subsidiary ledgers; and (ii) submit to the Audit Team the lacking
subsidiary ledgers and schedules pursuant to Section 114(2) of P.D. 1445 to
substantiate the account balances reported in the financial statements.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a team to help the
Municipal Accountant in the reconstruction of subsidiary ledgers of prior years to
cleanse the books and come up with reliable asset and liability balances that are
verifiable as to existence, completeness and accuracy.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the
disbursement vouchers, obligation requests, journal entry vouchers and supporting
documents for the subject transactions since these transactions were already
recognized in the books; and (b) henceforth, ensure strict adherence to the provisions
of Section 347 of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 on
the timely submission of accounts to the Auditor.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer submit certified copies of RAAOs for CYs 2016-
2018 Continuing Appropriations of the 20% Development Fund for
expenditures incurred for the period January 1, 2019 to December 31, 2020
and RAAO for CY 2019 Continuing Appropriations for the period January 1
to December 31, 2019; and
b. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer (a) reconcile their
records on allotments and obligations monthly, verify resulting discrepancies
and submit the required budget reports and reconciliation statement within
the prescribed period; and (b) exercise due care in preparing their respective
reports to prevent inaccurate or misleading information.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget
Officer immediately submit the details of reconciling items totaling ₱3,904,740.90 and
henceforth reconcile their records on allotments and obligations monthly, verify
resulting discrepancies and submit the required budget reports and reconciliation
statement within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and
other members of the Local Finance Committee submit the detailed computation/
schedules and related documents supporting the declared surplus for Supplemental
Budget Nos. 7, 8, 11 and 17, series of 2020 totaling ₱130,710,591.67 and for the
ensuing years, ensure that the declaration of sources of fund for supplemental
budgets are properly supported with valid basis and in accordance with the
provisions of Section 321 and 336 of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Budget Officer immediately reconcile their records on DRRMF in coordination with
the MDRRM Officer; (b) Municipal Accountant immediately reconstruct the SLs for
all transfers of unexpended DRRMF to the STF by year of transfer and record the
necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the correct balance of the Trust
Liabilities-DRRMF account, identify the amount to be transferred to the TF books
and LDRRMF STF bank account and to present the correct amounts in the NTFS;
(c) the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM Officer cause the immediate transfer
of the unexpended DRRMF to the LDRRMF STF bank account; and (d) the
MDRRM Officer submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on Sources and
Utilization of the DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant in
accordance with the deadline stated in Item 5.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant i) thru the BAC and the MDRRMO, submit the lacking
documents discussed in Tables14.2 and 14.3 as well as the rice and other welfare
goods distribution list; ii) ensure that all procurements are supported with PO
properly signed and with complete information; and iii) thoroughly review the
claims/DVs, as part of its internal audit function to ensure the completeness and
propriety of supporting documents before certifying the DVs; and
b. BAC, thru the BAC Secretariat, strictly comply with the posting requirements
of GPPB Circular 2020-001 for information purposes and transparency.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant i) thru the BAC and other concerned offices, submit the
lacking documents discussed in Table 15.2 as well as the rice and other welfare
goods distribution list; and ii) thoroughly review the completeness of the
supporting documents before certifying the vouchers;
b. Municipal Mayor, thru the BAC and BAC Secretariat, i) strictly comply with
the requirements of GPPB Circular No. 1-2020 and other relevant laws, rules and
regulations on procurement; ii) ensure that the complete and detailed item
description is included in the Purchase Order, in accordance with the provisions
of COA Circular No. 96-010; and (iii) cause the posting of the monthly fund
utilization and status of implementation of PPAs funded by the BGCM pursuant
to Item 4 of DBM LBC No. 125 as well the NOAs in the GPPB Online Portal; and
c. BAC consider the suggested retail price (SRP) per Department of Health
(DOH) Memorandum No. 2020-0144-A in the procurement of drugs and
medicines and provide explanation/justification on the high price of various
medical supplies procured.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator (MPDC) fast track the implementation of the infrastructure
projects and make sure that all projects are strictly monitored and
implemented in accordance with plans and schedules;
b. MPDC and the Local Development Council establish priorities during investment
programming and include in the Annual Investment Program only those programs
and projects which are feasible, specifically identified and with resource
requirements that are based on duly approved POWs and Detailed Cost Estimates;
c. Municipal Engineer ensure that the status report of all infrastructure projects of
the municipality is complete and supported with SWA and other documents
required for technical evaluation and inspection; and
d. Municipal Accountant submit the subsidiary schedule of the CIP account as well
as the subsidiary ledgers of the aforementioned on-going projects.

We recommended that the (a) GFPS (i) ensure that the GPB, as well as any
changes thereto, is reviewed and endorsed by the DILG as to the gender
responsiveness of the GAD programs and projects; and (ii) submit to the Audit Team
the supporting documents of the DILG-received GAD AR; and (b) Municipal Budget
Officer reconcile his records with the GFPS regarding the utilizations of the GAD
budget as of December 31, 2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) reconstruct and
immediately submit to the Audit Team the SLs for audit disallowances totaling
₱1,420,937.98; and (b) recognize in the books the audit disallowances totaling
₱168,000.00 that have become final and executory and henceforth, ensure that all
NDs covered with NFD are promptly recorded in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the ledgers and cashbooks, to establish the accurate balances of Cash in Bank accounts.  Correspondingly, oblige the Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements in order to determine entries in both the books and bank which need adjustment in order to ascertain the validity and accuracy of the balances of the Cash in Bank accounts as appearing in the Municipality’s financial statements.

For the proper management and handling of the PPE, we reiterate our prior year’s recommendations that Management:
Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of December 31 of every year.  The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current  year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the physical existence of the PPE and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets.  Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments and to exact responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs; and
Require the Supply Officer/Property Custodian to maintain/update the subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger cards, AREs and placing of property tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control and monitoring of the PPEs and for facility in identifying them.
Lastly, we recommend that the Local Chief Executive request for authority to derecognize non-existing/missing PPE to the COA for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability. The procedure in the request for authority to derecognize is prescribed under COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to look for other the documents, like journal vouchers, subsidiary ledgers, previous financial statements or other relevant records to be used as a basis in recording back the value of Public Infrastructures in the books accounts.  Instruct also the Municipal Engineer to conduct actual inventory of the road networks of the municipality and provide the necessary information to the Municipal Accountant in order for the latter have a basis in recording the assets in the books of the municipality.

We recommended that the Management instruct all Department Heads to process all unpaid transactions before year-end so that all will be paid or obligated before year-end. Also, require the Budget Officer see to it that no prior year’s expenditure to be charged to current year’s appropriations in order to observe proper matching of expenses against appropriation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit the collections daily and intact in order to avoid accumulation of large amount of cash in the vault and prevent possible risk of loss or misuse of funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Local Finance Committee and other local officials to comply with the reporting requirements as prescribed by the law in order to inform the stakeholders of the relevant information and promote transparency and accountability.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that gasoline expenses are supported with properly accomplished Trip Ticket to ensure the reasonableness of fuel consumption. Further, we recommended that Management may designate employee who will be in charged in the issuance of trip ticket and monitoring the fuel consumption of the Municipality in order to avoid wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to draw cash advance for the payments of honoraria and be the one to pay each payee of their respective honorarium to assure that the latter received the actual payment. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer ensure that the payments be supported with accomplished payroll and payments are duly received by the payees in order to support the liquidation of the cash advance granted to her.

We recommended that Management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to procure only to suppliers with complete documentary requirements.  Also, require the Municipal Accountant strict adherence to the requirements prescribed in the aforementioned laws and regulations and ensure that all necessary documents especially the eligibility documents are attached to the vouchers upon submission to the auditor in order for the latter to verify and ascertain the eligibility of the suppliers with the end view of also ascertaining the validity and propriety of the disbursement as a whole.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to either withhold/deduct the retention money as prescribed by law from the payment to suppliers or require the latter to submit bank guaranty as a warranty for the delivered goods in order to protect the interest of the Management in case of manufacturing defect.

We recommended that Management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee that during emergency cases, negotiate or procure only to legally, technically and financially capable suppliers and require the same of the necessary documentary requirements.
Also, require the Municipal Accountant strict adherence to the requirements prescribed in the aforementioned laws and regulations and ensure that all necessary documents are attached to the vouchers upon submission to the auditor in order for the latter to verify and ascertain the eligibility of the suppliers and any noted deficiencies/inconsistencies will be immediately informed to Management for its appropriate action, thus  ascertaining the validity and propriety of the disbursement as a whole.

We recommended that Management require the BAC Secretariat to post all Requests for Quotations and Awards for alternative mode of procurement above ₱ 50,000.00 in the PhilGEPS website in order to attain efficient and transparent procurement. Moreover, copies of printouts of these posting be attached to the disbursement vouchers as part of the supporting documents to serve as evidence that posting have been made. In case the website is not available due to system glitch, a copy of the screenshot be submitted in order to prove that there was an attempt or effort to post in the website.

deposits in a timely manner and report all transactions in the month that
they occur.

the unreported collections of ₱141,826.29 accordingly.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Maintain subsidiary ledger for each bank account to facilitate verification
of the account;
b. Prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statement for each bank
account; and
c. Determine the nature and adjust the reconciling items identified during
the preparation of the monthly BRS on a timely basis for an accurate and
correct Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account reflected in the
financial statement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:
a. Department heads/end-users to submit the distribution list/Requisition
and Issuance Slip (RIS) duly signed by the identified recipients to the
Municipal Treasurer/Supply Officer for the latter’s consolidation and

distribution list/RIS of different offices/departments; to consolidate the

monthly for recording;
c. Inventory Committee to regularly conduct physical count of Inventory
and reconcile the results thereof with the records maintained in the
Supply Office and the Accounting Office; and
d. Municipal Accountant to:
i. Regularly reconcile the inventory account balance with the
records of the Supply Office/Municipal Treasurer’s Office;
ii. Monitor the monthly submission by the Supply Office of RSMIs
and record issuances of inventories on the basis thereof; and
iii. Ensure that the year-end inventory balance is reconciled with the
property records and the balances reflected in the Report on the
Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI).

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
a. The Municipal Accountant to exert effort to compute and provide
depreciation on depreciable PPEs especially those with available data
necessary for computing depreciation.
b. The Municipal Treasurer/Inventory Committee to conduct annual
physical count of PPEs and prepare RPCPPE to support the PPEs
presented in the Statement of Financial Position and help the Municipal
Accountant on data gathering needed for computation of depreciation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to create the Special Account in the General Fund and to
reclassify to the appropriate liability account to reflect the unutilized amount
of ₱1,567,215.26 as of December 31, 2020 as Trust Liability pursuant to Item
3.7 of DBM LBC No. 125 and the chart of account for the LGUs.

We further recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the
Municipal Accountant reconcile the balances reflected in their respective
records/reports and make the necessary corrections.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the
affected accounts and henceforth, properly record all obligations as at year
end and as they occur to ensure correct amounts of unpaid obligations
incurred during the year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the LFC to strictly
observe the reportorial and posting requirement on Fund Utilization and
Status of PPA Implementation of the Bayanihan Fund Grant to
Municipalities as prescribed in LBC No. 125 dated April 13, 2020.

Further, we recommended that Fund Utilization Report and the Status of
Implementation of the Bayanihan Grant Fund shall include local funds
augmented for the COVID 19 PPAs that would reflect the accurate balance
of the fund as of December 31, 2020, duly signed by the Local Finance
Committee.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MSWDO who
is in charge of the receipts and distribution to prepare the (1) Summary/List
of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for In-Kind Donations; (2)
Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of Donated Relief Goods;
and furnish the same to the NDRRMC, through the OCD, copy furnished the
Audit Team within ten working days after the end of each quarter pursuant
to COA Circular 2014-002 and 2020-009. Henceforth, the said reports
should be prepared and submitted for in-kind donations in the future.

d. BAC or MDRRMO to submit the complete required procurement
documents as enumerated in COA Circular 2012-001;

of the amount corresponding to the undelivered items;
f. Submit explanation on the varied cost granted to the LSIs or returning
residents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC, TWG and
the Requesting Officer to:

d. Perform the duties and functions in accordance with RA 9184;
e. Evaluate all technical and financial documents submitted and follow the
basic steps in the procurement regardless of the mode of procurement;
and
f. Requisition as to quantity and items to be procured should be deliberated
by a committee before purchasing to safeguard the funds from possible
wastage due to overstocking of items.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the
year end trial balance and financial statement for the SAGF-BGCM in
compliance with the rules and regulations set under Section 110 of Chapter
6, Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs for fair presentation,
transparency and accountability of the funds utilized. Likewise, subsidiary

We recommended, that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the
designated RCLO to:

f. Update the cashbook daily based on the Report of Collections and
Deposits submitted by the deputized collectors;
g. Close the cashbooks at the end of the month, double rule and foot the
totals;
h. Reconcile the cashbook balance with the accounting records at least on a
quarterly basis;
i. Maintain subsidiary record for each deputized collector supported with
files of the RCDs submitted to facilitate easy retrieval of records for
validation and verification purposes; and
j. Require the RCCs and BTs to remit their collections within the

In view of the above deficiencies and in order to strengthen the controls in
the handling of collections we recommended that the Municipal Mayor
require the Municipal Treasurer and RCLO to:

d. Verify the Report of Collections and Deposits submitted by deputized
collectors; check the statement of accountable forms as to beginning
balances, receipts, issuances and ending balances; make a physical count
of the accountable forms remaining in the custody of the collector/teller
and check the same against the new balances on hand. The RCLO’s

issued is an indication of her verification. The RCLO should count the

certification and the receipt portion of all copies of RCD;
e. Prepare the Report of Accountability of Accountable Forms with or
without money value on monthly basis duly supported with the

to give an idea as to the serial numbers and the quantity reported on
hand.
f. Stop the practice of releasing new booklets to the collectors unless the
previous ORs were all issued and remitted intact.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory
Committee to conduct physical count on PPEs and prepare RPCPPE and
IRRUP and create a Committee on Awards that will be responsible to
ultimately dispose all unserviceable properties at the earliest possible time
pursuant to Section 79 of PD 1445, Sections 379-381 of RA 7160 and in
accordance with the procedures cited in COA Circular No. 92-386.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to require the contractors concerned to post the subject
warranty securities.

Henceforth, the LGU should strictly enforce compliance with the pertinent
provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR, and Section 9.1.1.4 of COA
Circular No. 2012-001 in the implementation of infrastructure projects,
particularly on the posting of warranty securities before final payment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Health Officer to:

a. Formulate internal control policies necessary to minimized the
occurrence of expired medicines such as establishing adequate control on
stock level monitoring through the maintenance of inventory database to
facilitate retrieval of data on inventory balances, actual condition of
stocks; and
b. Prepare an inventory report of fast moving and slow moving medicines to
gauge the BAC in the procurement of medicines for prepositioning on
their subsequent transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:

a. Local Finance Committee to appropriate no less than 20% from its IRA
for priority development projects of the LGU; and to coordinate with the
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to ensure that
the PPAs are identified/specified in terms of the name of the project and the
amount allocated; and
b. MPDC to coordinate with the Municipal Engineering Office and/or
offices concerned to monitor the immediate implementation of
development projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:

a. The MDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMFIP; monitor and implement
programmed Projects/Programs/Activities in order to effectively increase
the level of preparedness on time of occurrence of natural calamities and
mitigate the risk of damage in case of disaster, and prepare a monthly
Report on Sources and Utilization following the prescribed format.
Moreover, require him to efficiently utilize the Quick Response Fund to
alleviate difficulties of the constituents during calamities and pandemic.
b. The Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to strictly follow the
accounting and reporting guidelines for Local Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Fund (LDRRMF) of the Local Government Units
provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended the Management to:
Evaluate each item of PPE noted in this observation against the recognition criteria under COA Circular No. 2020-006 and PPSAS taking into consideration the capitalization threshold of ₱15,000 and the exercise of judgment, when warranted (such that when it may be appropriate to aggregate individually insignificant items, such as library books, computer peripherals, and small items of equipment, and to apply the criteria to the aggregate value); and
After the evaluation, effect the necessary revision in the RPCPPE and adjusting entry in the books of the LGU, when warranted.

We reiterated our previous recommendation for the Management to hasten and facilitate the preparation of documentary requirements and ensure allocation of appropriate funds for the titling of the lands owned by the LGU.

We recommended the Management to:
Ensure to determine promptly the nature of the observed error or misstatement;
Effect immediately the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct account balances of the RPT/SET receivables and the corresponding deferred income accounts; and
Prospectively, ensure strict adherence with the pro-forma journal entries provided in the MNGAS to avoid errors or misstatements.

We recommended the Management to:
Perform periodic verification, analysis and validation of the Due from LGUs account with concerned LGUs; and
Draw necessary journal entries to reflect the correct/adjusted Due from LGUs account balances.

We recommended the Management to:
Prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis; and
Perform constant review of recorded transactions to deter error and omissions.
We recommended the Management to monitor and reconcile the Due to BIR and Due to Pag-IBIG accounts to determine the cause of the abnormal balances and effect necessary adjustments.

We recommended the Management to:
Perform periodic verification, analysis and validation of the Due to LGUs account with concerned LGUs; and
Draw necessary journal entries to reflect the correct/adjusted Due to LGUs account balances.

We recommended the Management to direct the Accountant to update the preparation and submission of the monthly BRS of all depository accounts maintained by the agency within the period prescribed in Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011. Also, coordinate with the depository bank for the prompt release of the monthly BS to facilitate the timely preparation of the BRS.

We recommended the Management to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and Disbursing Officer to make the necessary corrections on the deficiency noted;
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant be directed to conduct a thorough review of the disbursement vouchers and it’s supporting documents (SDs) and sees to it that only valid and complete vouchers and its SDs shall be processed and paid; and
Strictly comply with the provisions of the Circulars pertaining to cash advances.

We recommended the Management to:
Submit the required MOA;
Ascertain that fund releases as financial assistance to POs/NGOs are supported with Memorandum of Agreement containing the necessary terms of reference as required under Section 4.5 of COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007; and
Ensure that transactions are supported by complete documentation, without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Notice of Disallowance, when warranted.

We recommended the LSB to:
Prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting;
Request for inputs from DepEd Official/ School Division Superintendent in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures in accordance with the DepEd criteria and with due regard to the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund; and
Maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its objectives and take appropriate actions such as realignment or reprogramming, if necessary.

We recommended the LSB to:
Follow strictly JC No. 1, s. 2017 of the DepEd, DBM and DILG in the budget preparation of the SEF to ensure appropriate identification of PPAs and budget allocation; and
Maximize the purpose of the SEF and ensure the attainment of the purpose for the creation of the fund.

We recommended the Management to:
Fast track the submission of the Relief Distribution Sheets indicating the names of recipients and their signatures for evaluation of the Audit Team, without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance, when warranted; and
Ensure that all claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documentation to protect the interests of the government and to prevent unwarranted disbursements of public funds.

We recommended the Management to:
Require the BAC to strictly comply with existing laws, rules and regulations on procurement;
Fast track the submission of the lacking mandatory documentary requirements for evaluation of the Audit Team, without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance, when warranted; and
Ensure that transactions are paid only when supported by complete documentation, without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Notice of Disallowance, when warranted.

We recommended the Management to:
Immediately refrain from resorting to reimbursement scheme. All acquisition of goods and services must be done through the regular and allowed mode of procurement prescribed under the 2016 R-IRR of R.A. No. 9184;
Judiciously plan the various meetings and activities of the LGU to ensure that the necessary procurement of goods or services in relation thereto are done timely and through the regular and allowed mode of procurement prescribed under the 2016 R-IRR of R.A. No. 9184; and
Revisit and strengthen the LGU’s existing policies on petty cash fund to discourage resorting to reimbursement, subject to applicable accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

We recommended the Management to ensure that, in case of specific fund transfers/grants received (such as the BGCM), the specific guidelines issued corresponding to its utilization (such as LBC No. 125) be faithfully complied and adhered with, thus, securing the achievement of its purpose and the intent of the law where the same emanated, without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Notice of Disallowance, when warranted.

We recommended the Management to:
Exercise prudence and economy in the utilization of government funds by ensuring that goods are procured for the price advantageous to the government and not reasonably far from its prevailing current market price;
Require the BAC to strictly comply with existing laws, rules and regulations on procurement; and
Ensure that transactions are paid only when supported by complete documentation, without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Notice of Disallowance, when warranted.

We recommended the Management to:
Strictly observe the internal controls for the proper utilization of government funds, specifically, ensuring complete documentations for the payment of DVs which inherently requires not merely the presence of the required supporting documents but also the completeness, consistency, and accuracy of the entries in such required supporting documents; and
Prospectively, warrant the prompt correction of the above-mentioned deficiencies.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management promptly submit requests for contract review for completed projects to the COA to avoid unnecessary delays in the conduct of contract evaluation.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer ensure that in the preparation of checks, the numerical series be carefully considered to lower the risk of omission in the recording. We further recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements in a timely manner in order to detect any reconciling item that may necessitate the preparation of adjusting entries. The immediate preparation of adjusting entry to debit Government Equity and credit Cash in Bank for the fair presentation of account balances is also being recommended.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the contractors to (a) undertake, at their own expense, the repair works on the damaged portions and complete the unperformed items of work; and (b) refund the excess contract cost on the Baywalk Development project. In case of the contractor’s failure or refusal to comply with its obligations, the municipal government should undertake the necessary works and demand full reimbursement of the expenses from the contractors. 

Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer and project engineers (a) prepare the ABC strictly in accordance with the guidelines set forth in DPWH DO No. 197 to ensure the reasonableness of contract costs; (b) supervise and thoroughly inspect work accomplishments on infrastructure projects to ensure that these were undertaken in accordance with the approved plans and specifications and that the costs pertaining to uncorrected defects and unperformed work items may be immediately deducted from the amount to be paid to the contractors pursuant to Section 5, Annex “E” of  the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and (c) regularly monitor and inspect infrastructure assets of the municipal government particularly those that are still within the defects liability period and the applicable warranty period for structural defects so that the Municipal Mayor can immediately demand repair or reconstruction works on damaged portions thereof from the contractors or any party found to be liable therefor pursuant to Section 62.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.reason:
The Management did not submit AAPSI.

The excess contract cost of ₱491,430.46 or the difference between the contract amount and the COA Cost Estimate on the Baywalk Development project was disallowed in audit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management take the following courses of action: 

Dispose the unserviceable properties in accordance with the most appropriate mode of disposition of property as provided in COA Circular Nos. 95-006 and 89-296 which are either through (a) public auction; (b) sale through negotiation; (c) transfer without cost to other offices or departments or other government agencies; or (d) by destruction;
Facilitate the immediate disposal of the aforementioned unserviceable properties to recoup any salvage value thereof; and
Ensure proper safekeeping of the unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration.

We recommended that Management prepare and submit the required quarterly and annual reports to the oversight department/agency in the prescribed form within the period, and observe the posting of utilization report in the LGU website to facilitate effective monitoring of the allocation and utilization of the SEF thereby, promoting transparency and accountability.

Also, financial transactions be scrutinized carefully to ensure that only items that are directly associated in the implementation of the targeted projects are properly charged to the Fund to maximize its utilization for more beneficial purpose.
