observations.recommendation:
For the proper management and handling of the PPE, we reiterated our prior year’s recommendations and Management agreed to:
Conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of December 31 of every year.  The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current  year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the physical existence of the PPE and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets.  Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments and to exact responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs; and
Maintain/update the subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger cards, AREs and placing of property tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control and monitoring of the PPEs and for facility in identifying them.

Since the main reason was due to lack of registries, we recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant look for other alternative documents, like journal entries, previous financial statements or other relevant records to be used as basis in recording back the value of Public Infrastructures in the books accounts.   We likewise recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Engineer conduct actual inventory of the road networks of the municipality and provide the necessary information in order for the Municipal Accountant to record all these assets in the books of the municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit to the Auditor for review and verification of the validity and accuracy of the balances of the Cash in Bank accounts as appearing in the Municipality’s financial statements. We likewise recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant look into documents like ledgers, journals, bank statements and Treasurer’s cashbook to help her in establishing the beginning balances of Cash in Bank accounts. The BRS is a tool to enhance the internal control over cash accountability and address immediate errors in terms of recording bank transactions.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer ensure that in the preparation of checks, the numerical series be carefully considered to lower the risk of omission in the recording. We further recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements in a timely manner in order to detect any reconciling item that may necessitate the preparation of adjusting entries. The immediate preparation of adjusting entry to debit Government Equity and credit Cash in Bank for the fair presentation of account balances is also being recommended.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer should only grant cash advance to employees who are designated as disbursing officers and to those who are bonded.  In case that there is no designated disbursing officer, cash advance should be granted to the Municipal Treasurer in order that the funds of the municipality will not be exposed to possible loss, misuse or misappropriations.

We recommended and Management agreed that the HRMO:
Indicate first in the application for monetization the balance of the leave credits before the monetized leave in order to reflect the real balances of the leave credits and to determine if such payment for monetized leave is proper and valid;
Update the leave cards of the employees as necessary in order to reflect accurate balances of leave credits; and
Post transactions in the leave card from the source documents as these occur instead of recording in advance avoid misleading/inaccurate balances as of a given date.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Assessor facilitate the preparation of the required documents for the purchase of the lot in order that the Municipal Accountant can submit to the Office of the Audit for proper evaluation and determination of the legality of the said transactions.

We recommended and Management agreed that Management inform the Municipal School Board to limit the utilization of the SEF to the programs and projects to those defined under RA 7160 and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017. Further, we recommended and Management agreed that Management advice the Municipal School Board to instruct the schools that they can collect school publication and Girl Scout fees from the students so that the SEF will only be disbursed for authorized expenses and that the benefits that the students and teachers could avail of under this fund will be maximized.

We recommended and Management agreed that  the BAC Secretariat post all Requests for Quotations and Awards for alternative mode of  procurement above P 50,000.00 in the PhilGEPS website in order to attain efficient and transparent procurement. Moreover, copies of printouts of these posting should be attached to the disbursement vouchers as part of the supporting documents to serve as evidence that posting have been made. In case the website is not available due to system glitch, a copy of the screenshot should be submitted in order to prove that there was an attempt or effort to post in the website.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to facilitate the transfer of the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF from the General Fund to the Trust Fund so that  the corresponding accounting entry debiting the Cash in Bank account and crediting the Trust Liability – LDRRMF account in the Trust Fund books will be recorded in compliance with the COA Circular and  for fair presentation of the account balances in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and all LGU officials and employees who are responsible for the preparation of investment plan and implementation/utilization of the 20% DF ensure that programs/projects that will be funded will be limited to those that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures not only in compliance with existing guidelines but also to avoid possible sanctions. Further, appropriate classification of programs/activities should be made in order that target social and environmental issues will be properly addressed.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Officials, especially the GAD Focal Person maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD related programs and project in order to attain the objectives for which the funds were appropriated. We recommend further that the GFPS pursue the capacity building opportunities by closely coordinating with municipal/provincial counterparts who are active in attending trainings/seminars.

In order to comply with the provisions of RA 9003 and achieve the state’s policy of adopting a systematic, comprehensive and ecological solid waste management program we recommended and Management agreed that:
The Legislative Department formulate ordinance regarding the stricter implementation of waste segregation in each household so that not only the wastes from the public market are being segregated; and
The Management identify location for proposed sanitary landfill in order to eventually construct its own facility and contract with other LGU for waste disposal will no longer be necessary, thus, cost for garbage disposal is expected to decrease.

We reiterate our previous year’s recommendations and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant submit the necessary documents to avoid the issuance of corresponding Notices of Disallowance. We also recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Treasurer immediately enforce the settlement of the audit disallowances to avoid the imposition of remedies provided in Section 23.4 of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts.

We recommended that the:
Local Chief Executive (LCE) (i) require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately reconcile their records as of December 31, 2019 and submit a reconciliation statement thereon and take-up the necessary adjusting entries in their respective records; (ii) require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit to the Municipal Accountant the disbursement vouchers, obligation requests and supporting documents of the unrecorded disbursements and withdrawals totaling ₱10,912,308.91, after which, the same shall be submitted to the Auditor for audit purposes;
Municipal Treasurer strictly observe the provisions of Sections 26 and 27 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II, Section 181(c) of  the GAAM, Volume I and Section 63(F) of the LTOM on the required maintenance of complete and updated cashbooks in all accounts of all funds and reconciliation of cashbook balances with subsidiary accounting records as this is important control tool for cash; and
Municipal Accountant (i) immediately prepare and submit to the Audit Team the lacking Bank Reconciliation Statements for the five depository accounts; (ii) further investigate and provide details and supporting documents for all reconciling items in the BRS and thereafter make the necessary adjustments in the books; (iii) investigate the variances between the reported CIB balances in the NTFS and in the unadjusted book balances indicated in the each BRS.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer (a) deposit all collections promptly and intact with the AGDB in compliance with Section 69 of P.D. No. 1445, Section 2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and (b) submit to the Audit Team a satisfactory written explanation why municipal collections totaling ₱16,587,973.59 were not deposited with the AGDB.

We recommended that a) the Municipal Treasurer update the Real Property Tax Account Registers or Taxpayer’s Index Cards, prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible at the beginning of every year; and b) the Municipal Accountant reconcile the recorded Real Property Tax Receivable, Special Education Tax Receivable, Deferred Real Property Tax Income and Deferred Special Education Tax Income accounts with the Treasury report on the unpaid taxes for the current year as well the uncollected taxes in prior years.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue exerting efforts to reconcile the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts in all funds, determine the cause/s of disparity in the balances and record the necessary adjustments in the books to present the correct balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee complete the actual physical count of all supplies and materials and prepare the report thereon, reconcile the results of the count with available records in the Accounting and General Services Offices and submit the RPCI to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal General Services Officer (a) present to the Auditor the copies of TCTs and Deed of Sale/Donation to establish ownership in favor of the muicipality; (b) conduct inventory of all its real properties whether acquired, donated or reclaimed; (c) institute proceedings for the issuance of tax declarations and TCTs in the name of the municipal government in coordination with the Municipal Assessor; and (d) regularly inspect the real properties owned by the municipal government to keep track of their condition and to safeguard them  from squatters, unlawful occupants or the like.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace, locate and recover the documents related to the above accounts and record the details obtained in the respective subsidiary ledgers.
Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the Municipal Accountant maintains the required books of accounts including subsidiary ledgers pursuant to Section 114(2) of P.D. 1445 and submits the required subsidiary schedules of GL balances and subsidiary ledgers to substantiate the account balances reported in the financial statements pursuant to Section 347 of R.A. No. 7160 and Sections 10, 70 and 72 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 10 of the same Manual, Volume II.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer submit certified copies of RAAOs for Continuing Appropriations from CY 2018 and below and submit the revised SAAOB containing report on the appropriations and obligations under CY 2019 SEF and Continuing Appropriations for CY 2017 and below;
b. Municipal Accountant immediately submit the Reconciliation Statement of Actual Amounts presented in the SCBAA and in the SFPer for CY 2019 and investigate the negative balances of appropriations reported in the SCBAA; and
c. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer reconcile their records on allotments and obligations monthly, verify resulting discrepancies and submit the required budget reports and reconciliation statement within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the disbursement vouchers, obligation requests, journal entry vouchers and supporting documents for the subject transactions since these transactions were already recognized in the books; and (b) henceforth, ensure strict adherence to the provisions of Section 347 of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that future projects funded by the National Government Agencies are implemented within the period stipulated in the MOA and strictly comply with the stipulations on the MOA and the prescribed procedures and guidelines related to implementation of LGSF, BuB and ESWM projects.

For the procurement of goods, we recommended that the:
LCE:
thru the Municipal Accountant, BAC and BAC Secretariat, (i) cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents; (ii) require the concerned officials to render satisfactory explanation for the deficiencies noted in audit; and (iii) henceforth, ensure strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, and RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR.; and
take appropriate action to collect the liquidated damages amounting to ₱326,016.00 from F&E Fleet Sales and Services Corporation who incurred delays in the delivery of three units of garbage trucks and henceforth, ensure that the terms and conditions indicated in the contracts are strictly complied with.
BAC strictly abide by the standards set forth by R.A. No. 9184, its Revised IRR and other relevant laws on government procurement;
Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of supporting documents before processing and certifying the DVs; and
Municipal Treasurer ensure that the payments to suppliers are evidenced with valid official receipts.
For the procurement of infrastructure projects, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and the BAC Secretariat (i) immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents which are necessary in the technical evaluation and validation of the projects; and (ii) henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001, R.A. No. 9184 and its Revised I.R.R. on the procurement and implementation of infrastructure projects.

We reiterated our previous recommendations for Management to take the following courses of action:
Prioritize the conduct of physical count of all the LGU’s PPE;
Prepare and submit copy of the RPCPPE to the Auditor in prescribed format, not later than January 31 of each year. In the preparation of the report, the different column should be properly fill-up to ensure that all items are properly identified and accounted for; and
Facilitate the preparation of documentary requirements and appropriate funds for the titling of the lands owned by the LGU.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of its road networks in order to ascertain the existence of the balance of the account Road Networks;
Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant of the complete description and cost segregation of the road networks to facilitate the preparation of the subsidiary ledgers for roads and its components; and
Require the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components, including Road Map. A Report on Local Road Network is to be prepared by the General Services Officer at the end of accounting period.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Analyze the details of the unreconciled amount of ₱1.94 million and effect necessary adjustments; and
Perform monthly reconciliation of the Construction in Progress accounts to timely detect and address variances on reports and records.

We recommended that Management maintain subsidiary ledgers and supporting schedules for the Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs accounts with complete information and specific breakdown of sub-accounts to facilitate reconciliation of accounts and serve as a monitoring tool for the implementation of projects and other related activities as well.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Revert the credited biological assets in the amount of ₱138,000.00 in the Breeding Stocks account and provide appropriate disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements with regards to the biological assets with total cost of ₱138,000.00 in order to provide the necessary information that may affect the fairness of the presentation of the financial statements; and
Require the Local Agriculturist to provide inventory report of the breeding stocks to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation and determination of appropriate action.
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the details of the Loans Payable pursuant to Paragraph 127 of PPSAS No. 1.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Reclassify the recorded confidential expense with total amount of ₱40,000.00 to its appropriate account;
Submit the cash advance voucher to the Audit Team for evaluation; and
Prospectively, strictly comply with the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01.
We recommended that the Management strictly adhere in the proper classification of accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
The Local Finance Committee should adopt appropriate austerity measures to ensure that sufficient cash is available to meet the obligations and improve the cash position of the Municipality, ensuring that monies held in trust be used only for the intended purpose, pursuant to Section Nos. 305(e) and 337 of R.A. No. 7160; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to review and scrutinize each item in the Current Liabilities accounts to ensure that only those valid and existing payables are recognized in the books.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to review and recheck the validity and correctness of claims appearing in the trial balance and financial statements. Necessary adjustments may be effected in case of invalid or insufficient claims by the agency to reflect the correct amounts, thus, present reliable accurate financial reports of the agency. A request for write-off maybe filed with COA for those long outstanding accounts as long as there is a proof that the agency has exhausted all available legal and administrative remedies to enforce collections pursuant to existing COA Regulations.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Require the concerned Heads of Offices to submit an explanation as to why the priority projects were not implemented;
Optimize the utilization of the 20 per cent LDF and implement the programs, activities and projects (PAPs) within the timelines in order to achieve the socio-economic and environmental outcomes, essential in the attainment of its goals and objectives of a desired quality of life for its constituents; and
Direct the Municipal Development Council to ensure that the development projects identified are feasible and can be implemented within the year with the proposed budget allocation.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Replenish the 20 per cent DF with the same amount expended for valid transactions but chargeable to the regular appropriation of concerned department/offices; and
Require the Local Development Council to ensure that only programs and projects geared towards the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes that partake the nature of investment of capital expenditures, as mandated by law will be given allocation under the 20 per cent DF to ensure the full realization of the purpose for which the Fund was created.

We recommended that Management include in the GPB appropriations for the attendance of its officials and employees to GAD trainings that will deepen their understanding of the GAD Program and their roles on the mainstreaming of the said program into local policy-making, planning, programming, budgeting, implementation, monitoring and evaluation.
We recommended that Management discontinue the practice of paying in advance the salaries and wages of personnel whose services were not yet rendered.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of actions:
Submit the duly filled-out Inspection and Acceptance Report and distribution list;
Consistently comply with existing rules and regulations on the utilization of DRRM Funds as set forth in COA Memorandum No. 2014-009 and other pertinent laws; and
Complete and adequate documents be attached to every transaction to establish their validity, propriety and existence.

We recommended that the LSB adhere strictly with the pertinent provisions of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1 s. 2017 including as topmost priority the improvement of educational facilities to maximize the purpose of the SEF and ensure the attainment of the purpose for the creation of the fund.
We recommended that Management ensure the full implementation of all the identified priority projects funded under SEF.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly implement the withholding of 10 per cent retention money on all payments of infrastructure projects to cover the defects that may arise before the final acceptance; and
Require the BAC to monitor the implementation of infrastructure projects to ensure that the Contractors are informed and required to renew their performance securities/warranty securities before its expiration.
We recommended that Management establish a clear-cut guidelines, policies and procedures on the selection and grant of cash assistance aligned with the DSWD MC No. 11, Series of 2019.
We recommended that Management ensure that grant of financial assistance are supported with the required documentary requirements prior to processing and releasing of financial assistance
We recommend that Management direct the BAC to regularly prepare and submit the PMR in accordance with the provisions of R-IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and in the format prescribed by the GPPB.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and other departments concerned to:
Promptly submit requests for contract review and technical inspection for completed projects to the Commission on Audit to avoid unnecessary delays in the conduct of contract evaluation and technical inspection; and
Observe the necessity of compliance on the required documentary requirements for submission within the prescribed timeframe for review and inspection.

We recommended that Management instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant-Designate to align the manner by which RPT shares are distributed with the provisions of the Local Government Code.

For the proper management and handling of the PPE, we recommended the following:
 Conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of December 31 of every year.  The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current  year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the physical existence of the PPE and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets.  Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments; and to pin point responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs.

Maintain/update the subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger cards, AREs and placing of property tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control and monitoring of the PPEs and for facility in identifying them.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 1, page 32 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all concerned officials/personnel strictly adhere to COA Circular Nos. 2016-004 and 2015-008 dated September 30, 2016 and November 23, 2015, respectively.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 2, page 33 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to correct the misclassified accounts in order to reflect the accurate balances of these accounts.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were already made to correct the misclassified accounts.
observations.reason: Verified the journal entries made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit all cancelled checks to the Municipal Accountant in conformity with Section 55 of the Manual on the NGAS for Local Government Units for inspection and proper monitoring.
observations.managementAction: Cancelled checks were presented.
observations.reason: Verified the cancelled checks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer only grant cash advance to employees who are designated as disbursing officer and to those who are bonded. Cash advance should only be allowed if previous cash advances are already liquidated. In case that there is no designated disbursing officer, cash advance should be granted to the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: Cash advance were still granted to employees who were not bonded.
observations.reason: Verified during post audit and finding about cash advance was presented in Observation No. 5, page 38 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality:

Require payees to sign the payroll as proof of payment.

Require all officials and employees to submit their DTRs monthly.
observations.managementAction: Payees already signed the payroll as proof of payment. DTRs were also submitted.
observations.reason: Verified during the post audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the PESO only receive the allowable monthly honorarium of P 600. Accomplishment Reports should be submitted before payment will be made.
observations.managementAction: Accomplishment Reports were already submitted by the PESO and rate received was already in accordance with guidelines.
observations.reason: Verified during the post audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management prepare a written contract for the comprehensive termite management of the municipality in order to put in writing the terms and conditions regarding the performance of obligations by the contracting parties, the municipality and the service provider. The written agreement also serves as a basis to determine the reasonableness of the amount paid and validity of the disbursement.
observations.managementAction: Written agreement was already prepared.
observations.reason: Verified the during the post audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management limit procurement of goods on a reimbursement basis, unless the circumstances warrant its application. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant should ensure that payments are made by check directly to dealers/suppliers to whom payments are due and withhold the corresponding tax.
observations.managementAction: There were still transactions that made through reimbursement but this were due to emergency and unforeseen events.
observations.reason: Verified during post audit that those transactions were made through reimbursement were reasonable since unforeseen and made during emergency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU adhere to the provisions of the existing laws, rules and regulations regarding the payment of RATA of members of the local sanggunian and other municipal officials and employees. RATA should be pro-rated in case there are absences.
observations.managementAction: Full payment of RATA was received despite the absences during session.
observations.reason: Verified during post audit. Notice of Disallowance will be issued.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management should harmonize the terms of the contract with the provision of the RIRR of RA 9184 to avoid confusion on the part of the contractor in performing his obligations.
observations.managementAction: The terms in the contracts were already harmonized with the provision of RA 9184.
observations.reason: Verified during contract review and post audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management enhance their strategy in the procurement planning. Management should always consolidate its procurement in order not to circumvent the requirements of the law. Procurement should be done through public bidding if the amount is above the threshold for alternative mode of procurement.
observations.managementAction: No splitting of contracts were made.
observations.reason: Verified during the post audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Requests for Quotations and Awards for alternative modes of Procurement above P 50,000.00 be posted in the PhilGEPS website and that the copies of printouts should be attached to the disbursement vouchers as part of the supporting documents thereof.
observations.managementAction: No posting of RFQ and award in the PhilGEPs website for Small Value Procurement with approved budget of the contract was more than               P 50,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management deduct retention money as prescribed by law from payment to contractors/suppliers or require them to post other form of authorized security in order to protect their interest in case of manufacturing defects arise in the procurement of goods and to assure that the contractor satisfactorily does his work on time for infrastructure projects.

Likewise, the monthly Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) should be prepared and submitted by the Municipal Treasurer to the audit team to ensure monitoring of the status of accountable forms issued to collecting officers.

observations.recommendation:
1.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget
Officer to submit the SAAOB and Comparison of Budget and Actual
Amounts and observe the complete submission of financial statements
pursuant to Par. 21 of PPSAS 1 and Section 4.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2016-
004.
1.9 We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer maintain and
regularly update the Registries of Appropriation, Allotment and Balances for
PS, MOOE, CO and Statutory Contractual Obligation for all funds of the
municipality.

2.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements using the balances
per books and bank as the Unadjusted Balances;
b. Identify and reconcile the unaccounted reconciling items, and record the
necessary adjusting entry;
c. Cause the closure of the account with the Philippine National Bank, and
transfer any remaining balance to accredited government depository
account;
d. Draw Journal Entry Voucher to revert to cash the value of stale checks or
replace the same, whichever is appropriate;
e. Prepare a subsidiary ledger for each depository bank account.
f. To verify the reconciling item that has existed for several years being the
existing Municipal Accountant who have the responsibility.

3.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to immediately coordinate with LBP-Catarman to inquire on
relevant information of the Savings Account and trace corresponding
document as basis for its recording in the books.

4.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:
a. Send demand letters for the immediate liquidation/settlement of officers
and employees' long-outstanding cash advances; 
b. Cause the withholding of salary of its employees to liquidate their
outstanding cash advances as prescribed in Section 5.9 of COA Circular
97-002;
c. Issue a certification that previous cash advance has been liquidated or
with no outstanding cash advance for every cash advance granted to
employees;
d. Refrain from giving additional cash advance to officers and employees
unless the previous cash advance is first settled or proper accounting
thereof is made; and
e. Locate source documents to at least identify the status of employment or
the offices of the employees granted cash advances with no details.

5.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the discrepancies between Due from Other Funds and
Due to Other Funds, and make adjustments in the books for those with
adequate corroborating documents to present the correct balances of the
affected accounts. Henceforth, exercise care in recording and classifying the
LGU’s financial transactions to ensure that the balances of reciprocal
accounts are at all times the same.

6.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory
Committee to immediately conduct the physical inventory of its depreciable
Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) and submit the Report on the Physical
Count of PPE (RPCPPE) to the Auditor. Also, direct the Municipal
Accountant to compute the deprecation of PPEs recognized in CY 2019, and
exert effort to trace and compute for the depreciation of recognized
depreciable PPEs in the prior year/s.

7.7 We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal
Accountant to trace the unaccounted fund transfers, disposed of the same
according to its purpose, or return unexpended balances. Also, to submit the
liquidation documents totaling P42,300.00 under Check No. 120710.

8.11 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant:
1. to exert efforts to trace records/documents concerning these accounts;
2. confirm with concerned agencies/personalities, and draw JEV to make
necessary adjusting entries; or
3. verify further the dormancy of receivable and fund transfer accounts for
10 years or more to be allowed write-off under COA Circular No. 2016-
005 dated December 19, 2016.

9.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Budget Office to coordinate and come up with an
agreement that cut-off date for submission of unpaid claims prior to the close
of accounting book of accounts, be established, to allow these claims to be
properly obligated and paid even after the cut-off date. Also, for the
Municipal Accountant to observe proper recording of prior year obligated
claims, which must be debited to appropriate Accounts Payable and credit to
Cash in Bank.

10.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Health
Officer to establish a program/plan addressed to implement the mandates of
the Per Family Payment Rate fund transfer.

11.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should direct the following
officers:
a. The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to
observe proper development project classification and refrain from
funding out of the 20% DF non-developmental projects. Henceforth,
comply with Item 3.0 of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular
(JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
b. The Municipal Budget Officer to be prudent in approving Obligation
Requests so as to refrain from utilizing the 20% DF for expenditures not
allowed to be charged therein. Henceforth, observe Item 4.0 of DILG and
DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22,
2017.
c. The Municipal Accountant to refrain from certifying in the Disbursement
Voucher, expenditures inappropriately funded out of the 20%
Development Fund.

12.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Prepare and cause to be submitted by the Local DRRM Officer, to OCD,
DILG and COA, the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of
DRRM Funds;
b. Revert to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, the CY 2013
unexpended balance of DRRM funds totaling P1,163,758.00;
c. Coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer in determining the actual
utilization of Local DRRM Fund, and thereafter transfer to Trust
Liabilities-DRRM in the Trust Fund books, the CY 2019 unexpended
balance of 30% QRF and the MOOE of the 70% allocation;
d. Report the unexpended balance of Local DRRM Fund in the Notes to the
Financial Statement using the format prescribed in Annex D of COA
Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. A copy of the
prescribed format is attached as Annex A.
12.8 Likewise, for the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Budget
Officer to maintain and update Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and
Obligations (RAAO) for DRRM funds.

13.5 We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal
Environmental and Natural Resources Officer to:
a) Submit the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan to the National Solid
Waste Management Commission (NSWMC) for evaluation and approval;
b) Prepare a timetable for the implementation of the solid waste
management program;
c) Develop a strategy every year which will contribute to achieving the
SWM Plan for 10 years; and
d) Utilize the amount appropriated for SWM Programs.

14.6 We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the identified Persons
Liable in the Notice of Suspension or the Notice of Disallowance to settle
their audit suspension or disallowance.
14.7 We recommended further for the Municipal Accountant to send copy of the
Notices to the Persons liable for their information on their unsettled
suspensions/disallowance.

15.6 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare Subsidiary Ledger for each person liable and draw
JEV to record the disallowances with Notice of Finality of Decision as
receivables in the books of accounts with the following accounting entry:
Particulars Debit Credit
Receivables-Disallowances/Charges P 12,551.77
Prior Year’s Adjustments P 12,551.77
To record the final and executory
disallowance of prior year’s transactions

16.12 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive immediately institutionalize
its GAD Focal Point System through issuances appointing officials and
employees of concerned agencies, as members of the Executive Committee,
Technical Working Group and the Secretariat. Also, for the GAD Focal Point
System, if institutionalized, to observe the proper preparation and timely
submission of GAD Plan and Budget and corresponding GAD
Accomplishment Report.

We recommended that a) the Municipal Treasurer update the Real Property
Tax Account Registers or Taxpayer’s Index Card, prepare and submit to the
Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and
collectible in the ensuing years; and b) the Municipal Accountant set up the Real
Property Tax Receivable, Special Education Tax Receivable, Deferred Real
Property Tax Income and Deferred Special Education Tax Income accounts for CY
2018 and onwards based on the certified list submitted by the Municipal Treasurer
showing not only the taxes accruing for the current year but uncollected taxes on
prior years as well.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 2 of this AAR

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal
Accountant, cause the immediate liquidation of cash advances totaling
P15,512,034.09. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant
reconstruct the subsidiary ledgers for the Advances to Special Disbursing Officer and
Due from Officers and Employees account balances of P4,840,451.91 as of
December 31, 2017 and immediately submit copies thereof to the Auditor. The Municipal
Accountant should also submit the following documents relevant to the
unliquidated cash advances for filing of appropriate cases against erring
accountable officers:
Authenticated copy of Appointment of the Accountable Officer (AO)
granted cash advance
Designation or authority to draw cash advance
Authenticated copy of AO’s fidelity bond
Authenticated copy of demand letter of the local chief executive and proof
of receipt by the AO and answer/explanation thereto
Disbursement voucher/s and supporting documents pertinent to cash
advances granted to the AO/employee/recipient or equivalent and partial
liquidation documents
Paid check for the cash advance
Other relevant documents
Henceforth, the subsidiary schedule or Schedule of Unliquidated Cash
Advances must be submitted together with the financial statements, the total
thereof duly reconciled with the balances reported in the Trial Balance and
Statement of Financial Position. The subsidiary schedule must completely
contain details as to name of accountable officer, position/designation,
purpose of cash advance, date of grant, unliquidated amount, due date for
liquidation, age of cash advances and status of employment of the accountable officers
whether they are still connected with the municipal government, retired,
resigned, dead or can no longer be traced.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 4 of this AAR

observations.recommendation:

Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts, determine the cause of
disparity in balances and record the necessary adjustments in the books
to present the correct balances in the financial statements.

maintenance/repair record of each equipment/vehicle owned and/or

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor immediately re-organize the
Municipal Solid Waste Management Board and prioritize the
preparation of the 10-year solid waste management plan, including the
closure plan for the existing controlled dump, for approval of the

government’s solid waste management system, construction of sanitary

SEF.

other DRRM activities pursuant to Section 5.1.11 of COA Circular No.
2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee see to it that the
provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR on advertisement and posting of

the procurement process.

We recommended that the Head of the Requesting Office, the Municipal
Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant thoroughly review the
propriety of charges against appropriations/allotments and verify the

the Obligation Requests and Disbursement Vouchers. Further, we
recommended that a separate depository account be opened for the 20%
Development Fund to better monitor the expenditures paid out of the
fund. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a
special account in the General Fund and prepare separate financial
statements for the 20% Development Fund in accordance with the
provisions of Section 313 of RA No. 7160 and Sections 107 and 110 of
the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 13 of this AAR

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that (a) the Municipal Engineer, the official in-charge
of the motor pool and the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the
documents required for audit and technical review; (b) the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer, in coordination with the

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to monitor and reconcile the Due to BIR account to determine the cause of the abnormal balances and effect necessary adjustments.
