observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management ensure that only expenditures that have direct bearing or falling within the concept of disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation activities are included in the LDRRM plan and charged to the Fund. We likewise recommended that the LDRRM Council monitor and evaluate the use and disbursement of the LDRRMF as required.
observations.managementAction: Various expenditures charged to the LDRRMF in CY 2018 still did not fall within the concept of disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation activities.
observations.reason: Various expenditures charged to the LDRRMF in CY 2018 still did not fall within the concept of disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation activities.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the Audit Team’s previous recommendation that Management submit requests for technical inspection for completed projects to the COA to avoid unnecessary delays in the conduct of contract evaluation and technical inspection of infrastructure contract/projects, observe the necessity of compliance on the required documentary requirements for submission within the timeframe for review and inspection.
observations.managementAction:
The requests for contract review for CY 2016 were already indorsed to the Office of the Supervising Auditor (OSA) for submission to COA Region V TAS. The requests for technical inspection for these projects, however, were not yet submitted to COA. In addition, CY 2015 copies of contracts for 16 infrastructure projects and its complete supporting documents amounting to ₱16,863,585.87 were not submitted.

All requests for contract review returned to Agency due to incomplete supporting documents were already re-submitted to COA. Related finding incorporated in Part II of this report.
observations.reason: No request for technical inspection was submitted for all completed projects for CY 2018 and prior.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MPDC select/consider strictly PPAs that would be charged to the 20 per cent DF in accordance with DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1. Also, financial transactions be scrutinized carefully to ensure that only items that are directly associated in the implementation of the targeted projects are properly charged to the Fund to maximize its utilization for more beneficial purpose.
observations.reason: Various disbursements in CY 2018 charged under the 20 per cent DF were not related and/or not connected with the implementation of development projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the concerned officials/employees to refund the excess payment of TA which the official is no longer entitled due to the use of a government vehicle.
observations.reason: Excess payment of TA to official with issued government vehicle not yet refunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to strictly observe the provisions of Department of Education, Culture and Sports (DECS), DBM and DILG JC No. 1 and Section 272 of R.A. No. 7160 on the utilization and prioritization of the SEF that will improve the standard and quality of education. We also recommended the Management to advise the Local School Board officials to give priority to programs, activities and projects enumerated in the cited law and regulations in the preparation of SEF budget.
observations.reason: Ineligible expenditures were charged to the SEF due to the inability of the LGU to properly identify disbursements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that only expenditures that have direct bearing or falling within the concept of disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation activities are included in the LDRRM plan and charged to the Fund.
observations.reason: Expenditures which are either doubtful or not directly related to disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation activities were still charged to the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the subsidiary schedules of General Ledger accounts and year-end adjusting and closing entries in addition to the SAAOB in the prescribed format be submitted on time. Provide the explanatory notes on the Notes to FS to present adequate disclosure of financial data. Its timely submission in the prescribed formats could facilitate the fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the Municipality’s financial statement but the same were not supported with schedules and lack other necessary data.
observations.reason: The FS and Notes to FS submitted by the Agency lack complete components to present adequate disclosure of accounts presented in the financial statement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management comply with the prescribed check disbursement process and required documentation for common government transactions in accordance with Section 44 (c) of MNGAS and COA Circular No.2012-001, respectively.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Various transactions in CY 2018 still lack the necessary supporting documents but were authorized for disbursements.

observations.recommendation:
For the proper management and handling of the PPE, we recommended the following:
Conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of December 31 of every year.  The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current  year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the physical existence of the PPE and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets.  Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments; and to pin point responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs.
Maintain/update the subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger cards, AREs and placing of property tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control and monitoring of the PPEs and for facility in identifying them.
We recommended that all concerned officials/personnel strictly adhere to COA Circular Nos. 2016-004 and 2015-008 dated September 30, 2016 and November 23, 2015, respectively.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to correct the misclassified accounts in order to reflect the accurate balances of these accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit all cancelled checks to the Municipal Accountant in conformity with Section 55 of the Manual on the NGAS for Local Government Units for inspection and proper monitoring.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer only grant cash advance to employees who are designated as disbursing officer and to those who are bonded. Cash advance should only be allowed if previous cash advances are already liquidated. In case that there is no designated disbursing officer, cash advance should be granted to the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Municipality:
Require payees to sign the payroll as proof of payment.
Require all officials and employees to submit their DTRs monthly.
We recommended that the PESO only receive the allowable monthly honorarium of P 600. Accomplishment Reports should be submitted before payment will be made.

We recommended that the management prepare a written contract for the comprehensive termite management of the municipality in order to put in writing the terms and conditions regarding the performance of obligations by the contracting parties, the municipality and the service provider. The written agreement also serves as a basis to determine the reasonableness of the amount paid and validity of the disbursement.

We recommended that the management limit procurement of goods on a reimbursement basis, unless the circumstances warrant its application. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant should ensure that payments are made by check directly to dealers/suppliers to whom payments are due and withhold the corresponding tax.

We recommended that the LGU adhere to the provisions of the existing laws, rules and regulations regarding the payment of RATA of members of the local sanggunian and other municipal officials and employees. RATA should be pro-rated in case there are absences.
We recommended that the Management should harmonize the terms of the contract with the provision of the RIRR of RA 9184 to avoid confusion on the part of the contractor in performing his obligations.

We recommended that the Management enhance their strategy in the procurement planning. Management should always consolidate its procurement in order not to circumvent the requirements of the law. Procurement should be done through public bidding if the amount is above the threshold for alternative mode of procurement.

We recommended that Requests for Quotations and Awards for alternative modes of Procurement above P 50,000.00 be posted in the PhilGEPS website and that the copies of printouts should be attached to the disbursement vouchers as part of the supporting documents thereof.

We recommended that the management deduct retention money as prescribed by law from payment to contractors/suppliers or require them to post other form of authorized security in order to protect their interest in case manufacturing defects arise in the procurement of goods and to assure that the contractor satisfactorily does his work on time for infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the municipality revisit their Municipal’s Market Code to enable them to impose the provisions and enhance their earning capacity by collecting more from the market stall owners. Likewise, Contract of Lease should be updated so that stall owners will be informed of their obligations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the transfer of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF from the General Fund to the Trust Fund books by debiting the Cash in Bank account and crediting the Trust Liability – LDRRMF account.

We reiterate our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Accountant submit the necessary documents to avoid the issuance of corresponding Notices of Disallowance. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Treasurer immediately enforce the settlement of the audit disallowances to avoid the imposition of remedies provided in Section 23.4 of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts.

In consonance with the principle of fiscal responsibility, we recommended
that the Municipal Mayor, as the one primarily responsible for all government
funds and property of the municipal government, require the 1) Municipal
Treasurer to maintain updated Cashbooks for all cash accounts, obtain copies of
Subsidiary Ledgers and bank statements from the Municipal Accountant,
immediately reconcile the same with the cashbook balances as of December 31,
2018, submit a reconciliation statement to the Audit Team and record the necessary
adjusting entries in the cashbooks; 2) Municipal Accountant to immediately prepare
and submit to the Audit Team the bank reconciliation statements as of December
31, 2018 and record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and
correction in the books; and 3) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to
strictly comply with the provisions of Section 63 of the LTOM and COA Circular
No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that a) the Municipal Treasurer update the Real Property
Tax Account Registers or Taxpayer’s Index Card, prepare and submit to the
Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and
collectible in the ensuing years; and b) the Municipal Accountant set up the Real
Property Tax Receivable, Special Education Tax Receivable, Deferred Real
Property Tax Income and Deferred Special Education Tax Income accounts for CY
2019 and onwards based on the certified list submitted by the Municipal Treasurer
showing not only the taxes accruing for the current year but uncollected taxes on
prior years as well.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the Due from
Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts, determine the cause of disparity in
balances and record the necessary adjustments in the books to present the correct
balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconstruct the subsidiary
ledgers for the Advances to Special Disbursing Officer and Due from Officers and
Employees account balances of P4,489,678.30 as of December 31, 2018 and
immediately submit copies thereof to the Audit Team. Further, we recommended
that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the following documents
relevant to the unliquidated cash advances for filing of appropriate cases against
erring accountable officers:
a. Authenticated copy of Appointment of the Accountable Officer (AO)
granted cash advance
b. Designation or authority to draw cash advance
c. Authenticated copy of AO’s fidelity bond
d. Authenticated copy of demand letter of the local chief executive and proof
of receipt by the AO and answer/explanation thereto
e. Disbursement voucher/s and supporting documents pertinent to cash
advances granted to the AO/employee/recipient or equivalent and partial
liquidation documents
f. Paid check for the cash advance
g. Other relevant documents
Henceforth, the subsidiary schedule or Schedule of Unliquidated Cash
Advances must be submitted together with the financial statements, the total
thereof duly reconciled with the balances reported in the Trial Balance and
Statement of Financial Position. The subsidiary schedule must contain complete
details as to name of accountable officer, position/designation, purpose of cash
advance, date of grant, unliquidated amount, due date for liquidation, age of cash
advances and status of employment of the accountable officers whether they are
still connected with the municipal government, retired, resigned, dead or can no
longer be traced.
In all instances, the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant must
ensure that each provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 are strictly observed and
that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of each year.

We recommended that the a) Municipal Accountant reconstruct the Real
Property Ledger Cards for land account costing P589,333.35; and b) Municipal
General Services Officer conduct inventory of all real properties of the municipal
government whether acquired, donated or reclaimed, institute proceedings for
issuance of tax declarations and certificates of title in the name of the municipal
government in coordination with the Municipal Assessor and regularly inspect the
real properties owned by the municipal government to keep track of their condition.

We recommended that the a) Inventory Committee complete the actual
physical inventory, reconcile the results of the count with available records in the
accounting and general services offices and submit the RPCI and RPCPPE to the
Audit Team pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I; and
b) concerned Accounting Office personnel and the General Services Officer
reconstruct the property cards and ledgers based on source documents or vouchers
to facilitate reconciliation of physical and book inventories.

We recommended that the concerned Accounting Office personnel
reconstruct the maintenance/repair record of each equipment/vehicle owned and/or
operated by the municipal government to establish the necessity of repairs and
maintenance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) trace, locate and
recover the documents related to the above accounts totaling P31,893,729.09; (b)
record the details obtained in the respective subsidiary ledgers; and (c) coordinate
with the Municipal Treasurer and provide the report of long overdue receivables to
facilitate its collection.

Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the
Municipal Accountant maintains the required books of accounts and submits the
financial reports and schedules in such form and within the period prescribed in the
NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget
Officer reconcile their records on allotments and obligations monthly, verify
resulting discrepancies and submit the required budget reports and reconciliation
statement within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the a) MEEDO/Market Administrator ensure that
lease of market stalls are covered by lease contracts stipulating the stall reference
numbers, area of stall being leased, amount and duration of lease, strictly enforce
the terms and provisions of the lease contracts and of the Market Code and propose
amendment to the Market Code and Municipal Economic Enterprise Code of 2014
to stipulate duration of lease, time of payment of monthly rental fees and
surcharge/penalty for late payment thereof; b) the Municipal Treasurer
immediately demand payment of the rental fees totaling P649,130.90 as of December
31, 2018, submit proof of collection and deposit thereof to the Audit Team and
henceforth, collect monthly rental fees on or before the due date stipulated in the
lease contracts; and c) the License Inspector conduct annual inspection of business
establishments to ensure compliance with the provisions of Sections 28 and 29 of
the Revenue Code.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor a) immediately secure, through
authorized representative, the required water permits from the NWRB and submit
authenticated copies thereof to the Audit Team; b) immediately request for NWRB
inspection of the completed water supply systems and obtain Certificates of
Compliance therefor; c) submit the water tariffs to the NWRB for approval; and d)
ensure that necessary permits and licenses are secured prior to implementation of
projects.

Further, we recommended that the MEEDO institute actions to collect the
receivables from water bills totaling P1,169,643.31 at year end and ensure that the
provisions of Sections 13(6) and 17(2) of the Municipal Economic Enterprise Code
of 2014 are complied with.

We recommended that the 1) Municipal Accountant record the necessary
adjustments in the books to reflect the correct balance of the Trust Liabilities-
DRRMF account; 2) Municipal Accountant and the MDRRMO cause the immediate
transfer of the unexpended amount of P36,143,923.09 to the separate depository
account for DRRMF; and 3) Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and
the MDRRMO ensure that the utilization of DRRMF is in accordance with the
provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002, NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1
and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1.

We recommended that the a) Head of the Requesting Office, the Municipal
Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant thoroughly review the propriety of
charges against appropriations/allotments and verify the balances of
appropriations/allotments before affixing their signatures in the Obligation
Requests and Disbursement Vouchers; and b) Municipal Budget Officer
immediately submit authenticated copies of revised RAAOs and SAAOB recording
the amounts of P7,263,678.78 and P7,308,939.08 against their corresponding project
appropriations.

Further, we recommended that a separate depository account be opened for
the 20% Development Fund and that the Municipal Accountant maintain a special
account in the General Fund and prepare separate financial statements for the 20%
Development Fund in accordance with the provisions of Section 313 of RA No. 7160
and Sections 107 and 110 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, to better
monitor the expenditures paid out of the fund.

We recommended that the:

a. Municipal Engineer, the official in-charge of the motor pool and the
Municipal Accountant immediately submit the documents required for
technical evaluation and inspection;

b. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer, in
coordination with the Municipal Engineer, reconcile their records to
determine the total cost incurred for each project;

c. Municipal Budget Officer i) provide the Audit Team with certified list of
projects charged against each lump sump appropriation and their
corresponding costs based on Program of Works and ii) henceforth
verify the existence of specific appropriations before certifying to that
effect in Box B of the Obligation Request;

d. Municipal Accountant see to it that disbursements on infrastructure
projects are supported with sufficient technical documents and that
vouchers and supporting documents of completed projects are
immediately submitted for audit; and

e. Municipal Mayor, as the one primarily responsible for all government
funds and property of the municipal government, ensure that the
required reports and documents are submitted by the concerned
municipal government officials to the Audit Team in such form and
within the period prescribed by the Commission pursuant to the
provisions of Sections 39 and 122 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the contractors to (a)
undertake, at their own expense, the repair works on the damaged portions and
complete the unperformed items of work; and (b) refund the excess contract cost on
the Baywalk Development project. In case of the contractor’s failure or refusal to
comply with its obligations, the municipal government should undertake the
necessary works and demand full reimbursement of the expenses from the
contractors.

Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer and project
engineers (a) prepare the ABC strictly in accordance with the guidelines set forth in
DPWH DO No. 197 to ensure the reasonableness of contract costs; (b) supervise and
thoroughly inspect work accomplishments on infrastructure projects to ensure that
these were undertaken in accordance with the approved plans and specifications
and that the costs pertaining to uncorrected defects and unperformed work items
may be immediately deducted from the amount to be paid to the contractors
pursuant to Section 5, Annex “E” of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and (c)
regularly monitor and inspect infrastructure assets of the municipal government
particularly those that are still within the defects liability period and the applicable
warranty period for structural defects so that the Municipal Mayor can
immediately demand repair or reconstruction works on damaged portions thereof
from the contractors or any party found to be liable therefor pursuant to Section
62.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Designated In-charge of SWM secure approval of
the San Vicente Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan for CY 2018-2028 from
the Palawan Council for Sustainable Development, move for the re-organization of
the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board and propose for the provision of
appropriations for the construction of sanitary landfill and implementation of the
projects and activities in the 10-year solid waste management plan including closure
plan for the existing controlled dump.

We recommended that a) the Municipal Mayor secure copies of Memoranda
of Agreement entered into and signed in behalf of the municipal government and all
its annexes and supporting documents and furnish the department head or official
in-charge implementing the projects and the Municipal Accountant copies of such
documents prior to implementation to ensure that the trust funds are utilized in
accordance with the trust agreements; b) the officials concerned immediately submit
the required documents for audit; and c) the Municipal Accountant ensure that the
provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 and Sections 97, 100 and 101(b) of the
NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, are strictly observed.

To compensate the lack of accounting personnel and facilitate efficient review of voluminous transactions, we recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Design a checklist of documentary requirements for common government transactions, based on COA Circular No. 2012-001 and other relevant laws and regulations, for information and compliance of concerned Offices before submission of the DVs and its supporting documents to the Office of the Municipal Accountant; and
Enhance its review by ensuring that all the required documents enumerated in the checklist to be designed are submitted to establish the propriety and validity of the disbursements and henceforth, payment should be made only for claims supported with complete documents.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Review the accounting entries identified by the Audit Team and effect the necessary correcting entries in the JEVs; and
Prospectively, verify the entries reflected in the JEV to minimize errors in the classification of accounts.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Dispose the unserviceable properties in accordance with the most appropriate mode of disposition of property as provided in COA Circular Nos. 95-006 and 89-296 which are either through (a) public auction; (b) sale through negotiation; (c) transfer without cost to other offices or departments or other government agencies; or (d) by destruction;
Facilitate the immediate disposal of the aforementioned unserviceable properties to recoup any salvage value thereof; and
Ensure proper safekeeping of the unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Direct the Officer-In-Charge (OIC)-Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant-Designate for the immediate reconciliation of the individual balances of the Cash in Treasury and Cash in Bank accounts in the GL and Cashbooks/Passbooks, respectively and make the necessary corrections and/or adjustments thereon and furnish the Audit Team copy of the JEVs for verification and audit purposes. Henceforth, Management strictly require both the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant-Designate to reconcile their records at least quarterly, as required by existing regulations; and
Strictly require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to prepare and timely submit the monthly bank reconciliations.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to comply strictly with the proper accounting of LDDRMF as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the LDRRMF unexpended balance of ₱1.33 million in the Trust Liability-DRRM account under the TF; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the corresponding cash to the appropriate TF bank account.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of actions:
Exercise control over the use of government vehicles through properly accomplished and duly approved and serially numbered DTTs, a summary of which shall be made at the end of the month in a Monthly Report of Official Travels for audit purposes; and
Submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Auditor for verification. The report should show among others, the type of motor vehicles used during the month total distance traveled, total fuel used and the normal fuel consumption for each vehicle.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
The Municipal Accountant document the request for write-off for account balances which were considered dormant and where collectability could no longer be ascertained pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005;
The Municipal Mayor to file request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts; and
Substantiate the undocumented receivable balances for proper action.

We recommended that Management require the DepEd representative to furnish the LSB with the required Three-Year Strategic Program and Recommendation of the DepEd PSDS and SDS prior to the preparation and approval of the Annual LSB Budget.

We recommended that Management prepare and submit the required quarterly and annual reports to the oversight department/agency in the prescribed form within the period, and observe the posting of utilization report in the LGU website to facilitate effective monitoring of the allocation and utilization of the SEF thereby, promoting transparency and accountability.

We recommended that the LSB:
Prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting;
Request for inputs from DepEd Official/SDS in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures in accordance with the DepEd criteria and with due regard to the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund; and
Require the LSB to coordinate with DepEd for the issuance of the requirements, specifications, policy and guidelines in order to ensure that the facilities and equipment, educational research, books and periodicals and sports equipment being requisitioned and procured conformed with DepEd standards pursuant to the pertinent provisions of DepEd, DBM and DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Require the MHO and BAC to ensure that drugs and medicines requested to be procured are in accordance with the latest PNDF. For drugs not listed in the PNF, a written request with corresponding justification shall be submitted to the PNF Secretariat for evaluation of completeness of submission before it will be presented to the Formulary Executive Council;
Direct the MHO and BAC to visit the Pharmaceutical Division’s website (https://pharmadiv.doh.gov.ph) regularly to be abreast with the updates in PNF; and
Submit the required documentation for the above-listed disbursements for drugs and medicines to avoid future suspension in post audit.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the BAC to promptly submit requests for contract review and technical inspection for completed projects to the COA to avoid unnecessary delays in the conduct of contract evaluation and technical inspection, and observe the necessity of compliance on the required documentary requirements for submission within the timeframe for review and inspection; and
Impose administrative sanctions to erring LGU personnel.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Advise the BAC/BAC-Technical Working Group (TWG) to thoroughly verify, validate and ascertain that all the statements and documents submitted by the bidder with Lowest Calculated Bid including the entries made to determine and ensure the technical and operational competence of the winning contractors during the post-qualification evaluation;
Submit comment on why the projects were still awarded to bidders not compliant with the equipment requirements; and
Impose administrative sanctions to erring personnel.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Accountant to immediately submit the unsubmitted CY 2018 Monthly Trial Balances and Quarterly FS and take appropriate immediate remedial measures, such as augmenting the Accountant’s Office of personnel from other Departments, especially during report preparations and/or allowing them to render overtime, subject to the availability of funds, in order to comply with the timely preparation and submission of these mandatory financial reports, pursuant to mandated deadlines.  Henceforth, to prepare and submit the same, with complete Schedules and Annexes within the prescribed timeframe in accordance with the abovementioned existing rules and regulations; and
Allow the Accountant to recommend qualified personnel of the Accounting Office to attend Trainings or Seminars relative to their works, to equip them with the required knowledge and skills in order to improve their performance.

We recommended that Management require the SWM Board to make the necessary revision on the SWMP to include appropriate Incentive Programs to encourage active participation of the constituents in the LGU’s SWMP.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Direct the BAC to require the concerned Contractors to renew/ extend its performance bond and furnish the Auditor a copy thereof; and
Require the BAC and MEO to designate personnel to monitor the implementation of infrastructure projects to ensure that the Contractors are informed and required to renew their performance securities before its expiration.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineer and/or Project Inspector or any agency personnel mandated to supervise the implementation of the infrastructure projects of the agency to submit to the Audit Team copies of the Results of Test Analysis/Status of Quality Control Test or a valid justification for our review and evaluation why the related disbursement transaction shall not be suspended in audit for inability to comply such legal requirements.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Coordinate with the Source Agencies on the proper disposition of unspent balances of completed projects; and
Submit the required supporting certifications from government agency to the Audit Team for evaluation.

We recommended that Management augment the manpower needs in the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to update its records and conduct intensive tax campaign by sending treasury personnel to the barangays especially during barangay assemblies for tax information drive in order to invite more taxpayers to pay taxes on time.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Direct the Municipal Accountant to evaluate the inventory account and
require from the officials concerned the SSMI, RIS and distribution list on
the issued supplies. Recognize all supplies under Inventory account and
record expense based on the SSMI with RIS/Distribution list; and
2. Require the Property Custodian to prepare and submit the SSMI supported
with RIS/Utilization Report for all issued supplies and materials so that
proper accounting thereof could be made.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Accountant to compute and provide depreciation for the current and
prior years depreciable PPEs to establish the correct balances, proper valuation
and reliable presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to (1) direct the Municipal
Accountant to look into the history of the account, trace accounting entries to
ascertain the cause of negative balances and (2) reclassify payments of tax
deficiency to Other Payables account and establish a receivable account. We
further recommended that the responsible officials should be personally liable
for the payment of the interest, surcharges and penalties from their failure to
withhold the appropriate taxes from payments of goods/services and
compensation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze and trace
transactions and accounting entries to ascertain the cause of the negative
balance and effect necessary adjustments/reclassifications to correct the account
balance reported in the financial statements.

We recommended that the management provide legal basis on the granting of
honoraria/allowance to designated officials and employees, otherwise require
the employees concerned to refund and henceforth refrain from the payment
thereof. Moreover, strictly adhere to pertinent laws and regulations of the
DBM relative to the grant of honoraria/allowances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the concerned
officials/employees to submit approved Driver’s Trip Ticket corresponding to
the official travels made and the monthly report of fuel consumption. See to
it that the future claims relative to such expenses are supported by the
required documents in the use of government vehicles.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the all
expenditures are recorded for the period when they are incurred in compliance
with the provision of Section 350 of RA No. 7160. We further recommend that
a cut-off date for the submission of unpaid claims prior to the close of the fiscal
year be established to allow the obligation and recording of expenses during the
year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer to stamp PAID all disbursement vouchers including the supporting
documents upon release of payment to prevent the double payment and other
irregular use of the same on similar transaction.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal
Engineer to submit the Inspection Report and Statement of Time Elapsed as of
the date of progress billing and henceforth, comply with the documentary
requirements in COA Circular No. 2012-001. Moreover, direct the Municipal
Accountant to process only claims that are complete and properly supported
with documents in compliance to COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to submit
the contract and all the documents forming part thereof to the Auditor within
five working days from approval of the contract and submit/provide copies of
the abovementioned lacking documents and explanation on the noted
deficiencies. Henceforth, direct to carefully prepare the Bidding Documents
to ensure substantial compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Assign a full-pledged MDRRMO to identify, monitor and implement
programs/projects activities in order to effectively increase the level of
preparedness of the municipality from the occurrence of natural calamities
and mitigate the risk of damage in case of disaster;
b. Direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to
determine the unutilized capital outlay from prior years on the amount
transferred to Trust Fund and shall be made continuing in the General
Fund books;
c. Instruct the MDRRMO to include the utilization of the unexpended
MOOE and QRF of prior years for DRRM which is under the Trust
Liability – DRRM, in the LDRRMFIP for programs/projects/activities
related to disaster management and prevention;
d. Require the MDRRMO to prepare and timely submit the duly certified
Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th day
after the end of each month, in compliance with Section 5.1.5 of COA
Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and
e. Require the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial
Statements the amount and details of the unexpended balances of
LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
School Board to convene, prepare and submit the SEF budget to fund the
priority programs/projects/activities of public elementary and secondary
schools in the locality. Likewise, take earnest efforts to develop high impact
and laudable programs/projects/activities consistent with the guidelines on the
utilization of SEF to ensure efficient and effective use of the fund.

We recommended that Municipal Development Council refrain from using the
20% Development Fund to pay salaries of personnel. We further
recommended to observe the guidelines provided under Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2011-1 of the DILG and DBM in the preparation and utilization
of funds. Likewise, assure that all programs/projects/activities which would
redound to the immediate benefit of the constituents be prioritized for
implementation.

We recommended that the LGU refrain from funding salaries of personnel
from the Solid Waste Management Program and observe the guidelines
provided under R.A. No. 9003. We further recommended that the Municipal
Solid Waste Management Board (MSWB) implement the monitoring and
evaluation system to determine municipality’s compliance on the SWMP and
update/review the SWMP to improve measures and effective implementation.

We recommended that management appropriate the required amount of at
least 5% of its annual budget to GAD programs; thoroughly prepare the GAD
Plan and Budget and submit the same to the PPDO of the Provincial
Government; and ensure that GAD fund is utilized in accordance with PCW-
DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated January
12, 2013.

We have recommended that the General Services Officer-Designate complete inventory taking and submit to the Audit Team copies of the RPCPPE and RPCLRN to facilitate the verification of the existence and actual condition as well as accuracy in the recording of items comprising the PPE accounts as appearing in the financial statements of the Municipal Government. The Municipal Accountant should transfer in the books public infrastructures as provided under COA Circular No. 2016-004.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was not able to comply with our recommendation.
observations.reason: No inventory report was submitted in our office.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit copies of such to the Auditor for review and verification of the validity and accuracy of the balance of the Cash in Bank accounts as appearing in the Municipality’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Monthly Bank Reconciliation was not yet prepared by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: No report was submitted in our office.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor enforce immediate settlement of the cash advances which were granted in 2015 and 2016. We have also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer immediately submit the liquidation documents for the cash advance withdrawn in 2017 for supplementary feeding to enable us to evaluate the utilization of the said cash advance.  Moreover, we have recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly follow the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 in the granting of cash advances and should see to it that cash advances are properly recorded in the books to ensure fairness of financial statement presentation.
observations.managementAction: The accountable officer already liquidated or refunded the unliquidated cash advance.
observations.reason: Post- audit of the transactions and collections of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the timely submission of financial reports and accounts as provided in the Manual on the New Government Accounting System and COA Circular No. 95-006 to facilitate the timely verification and review thereon.
observations.managementAction: No management action.
observations.reason: No report was submitted in our office.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the proper recording of supplies and materials purchased by the LGU to ensure the accuracy of inventory and expense accounts.  Moreover, the GSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant should maintain inventory records which shall be used to establish the correct amount of inventories.
observations.managementAction: Supplies and materials were recorded through inventory method.
observations.reason: Verification on the Journal entries made by the Municipal Accountant in recording their transactions.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant should revisit COA Circular 2012-001 and see to it that all claims against the funds of the Municipality are duly supported with all the requisite documents in accordance with Section 4, Item 6 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445 before certifying the completeness of supporting documents in the Disbursement Voucher. We have also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should see to it that all supporting documents are completely and properly accomplished.
observations.managementAction: There were still transactions of the municipality that lacked complete documentation.
observations.reason: Post audit of the transactions of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Technical Working Group and Bids and Awards Committee strictly observe the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 that would specifically address the infractions that were noted in order to ensure the validity and propriety of procurements made by the Municipal Government.
observations.managementAction: The TWG and the BAC partially complied with the observation of previous year like requiring the contractor of the submission of CSHP.
observations.reason: Post audit of the transaction of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Officials and employees involved in the implementation of GAD programs/projects/activities should see to it that the usage of the fund is maximized for the purpose it was created. The GAD Focal Person should also submit the GPB and corresponding Accomplishment Report within the required time to facilitate immediate review of the same.
observations.managementAction: GAD Plan and Budget was already submitted by the municipality and the GAD Fund was utilized in accordance with the law.
observations.reason: Review of the GAD Plan and utilization of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer provide all the details needed in the Obligation Requests to allow us to fully evaluate the charges that were made against the Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: No codes were indicated in the Obligation Requests.
observations.reason: Post audit of the transactions of the municipality.

observations.recommendation:
The Municipal Accountant and the LDRRMO observe the monthly submission of Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF as required by Paragraph 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to facilitate timely evaluation of transactions that were charged against the fund. The Municipal Budget Officer should also fill-up all the necessary entries in the Obligation Requests to allow us determine the fund from which the expenses were charged.

The Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries as follows:

GF Books   Prior Period Adjustment	      ₱ 1,540,927.12
		Due to Other Funds/Cash	  ₱ 1,540,927.12

TF Books   Due from Other Funds/Cash      ₱ 1,540,927.12
                        Trust Liabilities-DRRMF    	      ₱ 1,540,927.12
observations.managementAction: Reports were submitted in the office however, the unexpended portion of the DRRMF –MOOE was not yet transferred to Trust Liability.
observations.reason: Verification of the reports submitted in our office and review and analysis of the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer remit taxes withheld on time and should personally shoulder penalties incurred for late remittances.
observations.managementAction: No payment of penalties during the year.
observations.reason: Post audit of the transaction of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the necessary documents to avoid the issuance of corresponding Notices of Disallowance. We recommended that Management direct the BAC to submit promptly all contracts with their complete supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five days from perfection thereof in compliance with existing regulations. Likewise, request for technical inspection for completed projects must be submitted to COA. Sound and effective internal control system must also be implemented in the procurement process relative to infrastructure projects to avoid deficiencies and promote good governance.

Any unjustified failure of the officials and employees concerned to comply with the requirements herein imposed shall be subject to the administrative disciplinary action provided in (a) Section 127 of P.D. No. 1445; (b) Section 55, Title I-B, Book V of the Revised Administrative Code of 1987; and (c) Section 11 of R.A. No. 6713.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to reconstruct the subsidiary ledgers for Due from Officers and Employees account as of December 31, 2016 and immediately submit copies thereof to the Auditor. The Municipal Accountant should also immediately submit the following documents relevant to the unliquidated cash advances for filing of appropriate cases against erring accountable officers: Authenticated copy of Appointment of the Accountable Officer (AO) granted cash advance; Designation or authority to draw cash advance; Authenticated copy of AO’s fidelity bond; Authenticated copy of demand letter of the local chief executive and proof of receipt by the AO and answer/explanation thereto; Disbursement voucher/s and supporting documents pertinent to cash advances granted to the AO/employee/recipient or equivalent and partial liquidation documents; Paid check for the cash advance; Other relevant documents. Henceforth, the subsidiary schedule or Schedule of Unliquidated Cash Advances must be submitted together with the financial statements, the total thereof duly reconciled with the balances reported in the General Ledger, Trial Balance and Statement of Financial Position. The subsidiary schedule must completely contain details as to name of accountable officer, position/designation, purpose of cash advance, date of grant, unliquidated amount, due date for liquidation, age of cash advances and status of employment of the accountable officers whether they are still connected with the municipal government, retired, resigned, dead or can no longer be traced.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 3 of this AAR
