observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the one who is primarily responsible for all government funds and property of the municipality, should require the Municipal Accountant to submit the financial statements, journals and supporting schedules/documents in such form and within the period prescribed in Sections 4(e), 70 and 72 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010 and Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The management commented that the late submission of the financial statements for CY 2016 was still due to the delay in the approval of the budget for fiscal year 2015. As its domino effect, financial reports for CY 2016 were also delayed. Final balances to be taken up in the books as beginning balance for CY 2016 was only available on the latter part of October 2016, and at the latter part of November 2017 for the financial reports of CY 2017. However, this has been acted upon by the Municipal Accounting Office. Extra effort has been dedicated by the said office, extending overtime even on weekends just to address the backlog. Two additional technical staff were also hired, additional computers were purchased as committed and the electronic New Government Accounting System (eNGAS) was programmed for acquisition too. Due to some problems with the schedule of the team from the national office of the Commission on Audit (COA), the planned training in the Municipality of Coron, Palawan, however, did not materialize. This CY 2018, the management hopes to have the eNGAS installed in the office. The municipal government committed to follow the audit recommendations, i.e. to arrange a travel in Manila to personally undergo training from the COA’s Government Accountancy Sector and have their computer units installed with eNGAS. Once this is done, the municipal government believes this and many other problems related to financial reporting will finally be addressed. As a result of the above, the Municipal Accounting Office was able to submit the monthly financial reports for the months of January to March 2017 last January 31, 2018 and for the months of April to September 2017 last February 6, 2018. The municipal government’s final report will hopefully be submitted on or before February 25, 2018.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 10 of this AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the accounting and planning offices to submit the required reports on projects/programs/activities within the period prescribed in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 as amended by COA Circular No. 2015-006 dated August 20, 2015.
observations.managementAction: The management commented that the Acting Municipal Mayor has directed the Municipal Engineering Office and the Municipal Planning and Development Office to produce the required reports and submit the same to the Acting Municipal Mayor and to the auditor.
observations.reason: In CY 2017, the management still failed to provide the Auditor with a list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year, submit monitoring reports on PPAs and to inform the Auditor within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the concerned municipal government officials to seek refund of the amounts paid to former Sangguniang Bayan Members that represent the cash equivalent of the mandatory five-day leave that were not deducted from their respective vacation leave credits. Henceforth, we recommended strict observance of the provisions of CSC Memorandum Circular No. 06, s. 2003 and Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292.
observations.reason: ND Nos. SV-18-001-100-(2016) to SV-18-006-100-(2016) were issued for this audit observation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict adherence to the provisions of DBM LBC No. 63 dated October 22, 1996 and that grant of subsidies to barangays must be made payable to the name of the barangays and must be duly receipted by the barangays.
observations.managementAction: The management commented that effective CY 2018, all subsidies have been made payable to the name of the barangays, duly receipted, and not under the name of the Punong Barangays.
observations.reason: In CY 2017, the municipal government still released the subsidies directly to each Punong Barangay at a rate of P2,000.00 per month.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for strict adherence to the aforesaid provisions of RA No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 in that the documents discussed above shall be signed/approved by the Municipal Mayor herself in the exercise of the powers, duties and functions accorded to the local chief executive by RA No. 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: The management commented that a new office order was issued by the Acting Municipal Mayor in CY 2018 following the audit recommendations and since thereafter, signing authorities were clearly defined between the Acting Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Administrator.
observations.reason: In CY 2017, the Municipal Mayor still delegated the signing authority for disbursement vouchers, obligation requests, travel orders, purchase requests, purchase orders, job-order and other contracts to the Municipal Administrator.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the immediate submission of the vouchers covering partial payments for road rights-of-way made in CY 2015 as shown in Annex 7 and of the lacking supporting documents for payments and acquisitions made in CY 2016 to avoid audit suspension. Henceforth, we recommended for the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly review the Disbursement Vouchers and attachments to determine completeness of supporting documents as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and the Journal Entry Vouchers to ensure that transactions are properly recorded in the books in accordance with the Chart of Accounts prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
observations.reason: NS Nos. SV-18-001-100-(2016) to SV-18-003-100-(2016) and SV-18-004-100-(2015 and 2016) and SV-18-010-100-(2015 and 2016) were issued for this audit observation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that 1) the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer be required to immediately reconcile their records on DRRMF in coordination with the MDRRMO; 2) the Municipal Accountant be required to immediately record the necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the correct balances of the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF and Government Equity accounts; 3) the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRMO be required to cause the immediate transfer of the amount of P20,127,287.81 to the separate depository account for DRRMF; and 4) the MDRRMO be required to review the unexpended/unobligated balances of the DRRMF for CO to determine savings from already completed programs or projects that can be appropriated for other DRRM activities pursuant to Section 5.1.11 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 13 of this AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the immediate submission of the aforesaid lacking supporting documents together with the inventory of heavy equipment owned by the municipal government and their pertinent area of operation and operation hours for CY 2016, consistent with the provisions of Section 39 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 or the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and to avoid audit suspension.
observations.reason: ND No. SV-19-001-100-(2016) was issued for this audit observation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Head of the Requesting Office, the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly review the propriety of charges against appropriations/allotments, verify the balances of appropriations/allotments and check if declared savings in Augmentation Ordinances and Supplemental Budgets are deducted from the original appropriations prior to affixing their signatures in the Obligation Requests and Disbursement Vouchers. Further, we recommended that a separate depository account be opened for the 20% Development Fund to better monitor the expenditures paid out of the fund. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a special account in the General Fund and prepare separate financial statements for the 20% Development Fund in accordance with the provisions of Section 313 of RA No. 7160 and Sections 107 and 110 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 15 of this AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: * Discontinue the practice of granting TA to its officers when such officers uses government vehicle; and e Require the concemned officials/femployees to refund the excess payment of TA which the official is no longer entitled due to the use of a government vehicle.
observations.managementAction: Excess payment of TA to official with issued government vehicle not yet refunded.
observations.reason: Excess payment of TA to official with issued government vehicle not yet refunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to strictly observe the provisions of Department of Education, Culture and Sports (DECS), DBM and DILG JC No. 1 and Section 272 of R.A. No. 7160 on the utilization and prioritization of the SEF that will improve the standard and quality of education. We . also recommended the Management to advise the Local School Board officials to give priority to programs, activities and projects enumerated in the cited law and regulations in the preparation of SEF budget.
observations.managementAction: Ineligible expenditures were charged to the SEF due to the inability of the LGU to properly identify disbursements.
observations.reason: Similar observation incorporated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that only expenditures that have direct bearing or falling within the concept of disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation activities are included in the LDRRM plan and charged to the Fund.
observations.reason: Expenditures which are either doubtful or not directly related to disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation activities were still charged to the LDRRMF in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant comply with the requirements of R.A. No. 10121 that the unexpended LDRRMF shall accrue to a Special Trust Fund for the purpose of supporting DRRM activities within its five-year validity period guided by the accounting and reporting procedures under COA Circular No. 2012-002. Likewise, we recommended that the appropriation for MF-MOOE and MF-Capital Outlay (CO) be specifically identified in the budget and utilization report for easy determination of the amount to be transferred to the special trust fund or to be made continuing in the General Fund books.
observations.managementAction: Unexpended balance of LDRRMF was not yet transferred to a Special Trust Fund. The Municipal Accountant will reconcile the balance of the LDRRMF to determine the amount that will be transferred to the Special Trust Fund.
observations.reason: Unexpended balance of LDRRMF was not yet transferred to a Special Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the subsidiary schedules of General Ledger accounts and year-end adjusting and closing entries in addition to the SAAOB in the prescribed format be submitted on time. Provide the explanatory notes on the Notes to Financial Statements to present adequate disclosure of financial data. Its timely submission in the prescribed formats could facilitate the fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The financial statements and Notes to Financial Statements submitted by the Agency lack complete components to present adequate disclosure of accounts. The Municipal Accountant submitted the Municipality’s financial statement per fund on various dates but the same were not supported with schedules and lack other necessary data such as the pre-closing trial balances.
observations.reason: The financial statements and Notes to Financial Statements lack complete components and supporting schedules.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management comply with the prescribed check disbursement process and required documentation for common government transactions in accordance with Section 44 (c) of MNGAS and COA Circular No.2012-001, respectively.
observations.managementAction: Various transactions in CY 2017 still lacks the necessary supporting documents but was authorized for disbursements. Management agreed to comply with the recommendation. Some payees have not yet completed their supporting documents for liquidation of their cash advances.
observations.reason: Various transactions in CY 2017 still lacks the necessary supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the General Services Officer-Designate complete inventory taking and submit to the Audit Team copies of the RPCPPE and RPCLRN to facilitate the verification of the existence and actual condition as well as accuracy in the recording of items comprising the PPE accounts as appearing in the financial statements of the Municipal Government. The Municipal Accountant should transfer in the books public infrastructures as provided under COA Circular No. 2016-004.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit copies of such to the Auditor for review and verification of the validity and accuracy of the balance of the Cash in Bank accounts as appearing in the Municipality’s financial statements.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor enforce immediate settlement of the cash advances which were granted in 2015 and 2016. We have also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer immediately submit the liquidation documents for the cash advance withdrawn in 2017 for supplementary feeding to enable us to evaluate the utilization of the said cash advance.  Moreover, we have recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly follow the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 in the granting of cash advances and should see to it that cash advances are properly recorded in the books to ensure fairness of financial statement presentation.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the timely submission of financial reports and accounts as provided in the Manual on the New Government Accounting System and COA Circular No. 95-006 to facilitate the timely verification and review thereon.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the proper recording of supplies and materials purchased by the LGU to ensure the accuracy of inventory and expense accounts.  Moreover, the GSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant should maintain inventory records which shall be used to establish the correct amount of inventories.

We have reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant should revisit COA Circular 2012-001 and see to it that all claims against the funds of the Municipality are duly supported with all the requisite documents in accordance with Section 4, Item 6 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445 before certifying the completeness of supporting documents in the Disbursement Voucher. We have also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should see to it that all supporting documents are completely and properly accomplished.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Technical Working Group and Bids and Awards Committee strictly observe the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 that would specifically address the infractions that were noted in order to ensure the validity and propriety of procurements made by the Municipal Government.

We have recommended that the Officials and employees involved in the implementation of GAD programs/projects/activities should see to it that the usage of the fund is maximized for the purpose it was created. The GAD Focal Person should also submit the GPB and corresponding Accomplishment Report within the required time to facilitate immediate review of the same.
We have recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer provide all the details needed in the Obligation Requests to allow us to fully evaluate the charges that were made against the Development Fund.

We have recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant and the LDRRMO observe the monthly submission of Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF as required by Paragraph 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to facilitate timely evaluation of transactions that were charged against the fund. The Municipal Budget Officer should also fill-up all the necessary entries in the Obligation Requests to allow us determine the fund from which the expenses were charged.
The Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries as follows:
GF Books   Prior Period Adjustment	      ₱ 1,540,927.12
Due to Other Funds/Cash		  ₱ 1,540,927.12
TF Books   Due from Other Funds/Cash      ₱ 1,540,927.12
Trust Liabilities-DRRMF    	      ₱ 1,540,927.12
We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer remit taxes withheld on time and should personally shoulder penalties incurred for late remittances.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the necessary documents to avoid the issuance of corresponding Notices of Disallowance. We have also recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Treasurer immediately enforce the settlement of the audit disallowances to avoid the imposition of remedies provided in Section 23.4 of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts.

In consonance with the principle of  fiscal  responsibility,  we
      recommended for the Municipal Mayor,  as  the  one  who  is  primarily
      responsible for all government funds and property of the municipality,
       to 1) require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to
      maintain updated Cashbooks and Subsidiary Ledgers,  respectively,  for
      all cash accounts; 2) require the Municipal Treasurer to obtain copies
      of Subsidiary Ledgers on cash accounts and bank  statements  from  the
      Municipal Accountant, immediately reconcile the same with the cashbook
      balances as of December 31, 2017, submit a reconciliation statement to
      the  Auditor  and  record  the  necessary  adjusting  entries  in  the
      cashbooks; 3) require the Municipal Accountant to immediately  prepare
      and submit to the Auditor the bank  reconciliation  statements  as  of
      December 31, 2017 and record all valid reconciling items that  require
      adjustment and correction in the books; and 4) require  the  Municipal
      Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to  strictly  comply  with  the
      provisions of Section 63 of the Local Treasury Operations  Manual  and
      COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that a) the Municipal Treasurer update  the  Real
      Property Tax Account Registers or Taxpayer’s Index Card,  prepare  and
      submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers and
      the amount due and collectible  in  the  ensuing  years;  and b)  the
      Municipal Accountant set up the Real Property Tax Receivable,  Special
      Education Tax  Receivable,  Deferred  Real  Property  Tax  Income  and
      Deferred Special Education Tax Income accounts for CY 2018 and onwards
      based on the certified  list  submitted  by  the  Municipal  Treasurer
      showing  not  only  the  taxes  accruing  for  the  current  year  but
      uncollected taxes on prior years as well.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through  the  Municipal
      Accountant, cause the immediate liquidation of cash advances  totaling
      P15,512,034.09. We also  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant
      reconstruct  the  subsidiary  ledgers  for  the  Advances  to  Special
      Disbursing  Officer  and  Due  from  Officers  and  Employees  account
      balances of P4,840,451.91 as of  December  31,  2017  and  immediately
      submit copies thereof to the Auditor. The Municipal Accountant  should
      also submit the following documents relevant to the unliquidated  cash
      advances for filing of appropriate cases  against  erring  accountable
      officers:


           a. Authenticated copy of Appointment of the Accountable  Officer
              (AO) granted cash advance
           b. Designation or authority to draw cash advance
           c. Authenticated copy of AO’s fidelity bond
           d. Authenticated copy  of  demand  letter  of  the  local  chief
              executive   and   proof   of   receipt   by   the   AO    and
              answer/explanation thereto
           e. Disbursement voucher/s and supporting documents pertinent  to
              cash  advances  granted  to  the   AO/employee/recipient   or
              equivalent and partial liquidation documents
           f. Paid check for the cash advance
           g. Other relevant documents


           Henceforth, the subsidiary schedule or Schedule of  Unliquidated
      Cash  Advances  must  be  submitted  together   with   the   financial
      statements, the  total  thereof  duly  reconciled  with  the  balances
      reported in the Trial Balance and Statement of Financial Position. The
      subsidiary schedule must completely contain  details  as  to  name  of
      accountable officer, position/designation, purpose  of  cash  advance,
      date of grant, unliquidated amount, due date for liquidation,  age  of
      cash advances and status of employment  of  the  accountable  officers
      whether they  are  still  connected  with  the  municipal  government,
      retired, resigned, dead or can no longer be traced.


           In  all  instances,   the  Municipal  Mayor  and  the  Municipal
      Accountant must ensure that each provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002
      are strictly observed and that all cash advances are fully  liquidated
      at the end of each year.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile  the  Due
      from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts, determine the  cause
      of disparity in balances and record the necessary adjustments  in  the
      books to present the correct balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that  a)  the  Municipal  Accountant  immediately
      reconstruct the Real Property Ledger Cards; b) the  Municipal  General
      Services  Officer,  as  the  custodian  of  all  properties,  real  or
      personal, owned by the  municipal  government  [Section  490(b)(3)(i),
      Title V, Book III of RA No. 7160], conduct inventory of all  its  real
      properties  whether  acquired,  donated  or  reclaimed  and  institute
      proceedings for issuance of tax declarations and certificates of title
      in the name of the  municipal  government  in  coordination  with  the
      Municipal Assessor; and c)  the  Municipal  General  Services  Officer
      regularly inspect the real properties owned  by  the  Municipality  to
      keep track of their condition.

We recommended that a) the Inventory  Committee  conduct  actual
      physical count of inventories, reconcile the results of the count with
      available records in the accounting and general services  offices  and
      submit the RPCI and RPCPPE to the Auditor pursuant to Section  124  of
      the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and that all discrepancies  between
      physical and book inventories are investigated, cleared and reconciled
      immediately; and b) the accounting and the  general  services  offices
      reconstruct the property cards and ledgers based on  source  documents
      or  vouchers  to  facilitate  reconciliation  of  physical  and   book
      inventories.

We  recommended  that  the  accounting  office  reconstruct  the
      maintenance/repair  record  of  each  equipment/vehicle  owned  and/or
      operated by the municipal government.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a)  trace,  locate
      and recover the documents  related  to  the  above  accounts  totaling
      P61,441,451.73; (b) record the  details  obtained  in  the  respective
      subsidiary ledgers; and (c) coordinate with  the  Municipal  Treasurer
      and provide the report of long overdue receivables to  facilitate  its
      collection.

           Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure  that
      the Municipal Accountant maintain the required books of  accounts  and
      submit the financial reports and schedules in such form and within the
      period prescribed in the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require  the  accounting
      and  budget  offices  to  submit  the  required  reports  within   the
      prescribed period and to reconcile their  records  on  allotments  and
      obligations monthly so that resulting discrepancies can be immediately
      verified and corrected.

We recommended for the  Municipal  Mayor,  as  the  one  who  is
      primarily responsible for all government funds  and  property  of  the
      municipal  government,  to  ensure  that  the  required  reports   and
      documents  are  submitted  by  the  concerned   municipal   government
      officials in such form and within the period prescribed in Section  70
      of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I  and  Section  7.2.1(a)  of  COA
      Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor immediately  re-organize
      the  Municipal  Solid  Waste  Management  Board  and  prioritize   the
      preparation of the 10-year solid waste management plan, including  the
      closure plan for the existing controlled dump,  for  approval  of  the
      National Solid Waste Management Commission. We also  recommended  that
      funds  be  appropriated  for  the   improvement   of   the   municipal
      government’s solid waste management system, construction  of  sanitary
      landfill, operationalization of  MRF  in  every  barangay  and  public
      education and information activities on ecological waste management.

We recommended that the members of the Local School Board  (LSB)
      1) ensure that the  allocation  and  utilization  of  the  SEF  is  in
      accordance with the provisions of Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and DepEd-
      DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s.2017; 2) furnish the  Municipal  Budget  Officer,
      Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer with a copy of  the  duly
      approved Annual SEF Budget as basis for disbursement and certification
      of availability of funds;  and  3)  submit  to  the  Auditor  the  LSB
      Resolutions bearing the  names  and  signatures  of  the  LSB  members
      approving the budget for calendar years 2016 to 2017.


           We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the refund of
      P168,000.00 representing RATA of the District Supervisor covering  CYs
      2016 and 2017 charged against the SEF.

We recommended that 1) the Municipal Accountant and the  Municipal
    Budget  Officer  immediately  reconcile  their  records  on  DRRMF   in
    coordination with the MDRRMO; 2) the Municipal  Accountant  immediately
    record the necessary adjustments in the books to  reflect  the  correct
    balances of the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF and Government Equity accounts;
    3) the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRMO cause the immediate transfer
    of the unexpended amount to the separate depository account for  DRRMF;
    and 4) the MDRRMO review the  unexpended/unobligated  balances  of  the
    DRRMF for Capital Outlay to determine savings from  completed  programs
    or projects that can be appropriated for other DRRM activities pursuant
    to Section 5.1.11 of COA Circular  No.  2012-002  dated  September  12,
    2012.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee see to it that
      the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR on advertisement and posting
      of  procurement-related  notices  are   complied   with   to   enhance
      transparency of the procurement process.

We recommended that the  Head  of the Requesting  Office,  the
      Municipal Budget  Officer  and  the  Municipal  Accountant  thoroughly
      review the propriety of charges against appropriations/allotments  and
      verify the balances of appropriations/allotments before affixing their
      signatures in the Obligation Requests and Disbursement Vouchers.


           Further, we recommended that a separate  depository  account  be
      opened for the 20% Development Fund to better monitor the expenditures
      paid out of the fund. Likewise,  we  recommended  that  the  Municipal
      Accountant maintain a special account in the General Fund and  prepare
      separate  financial  statements  for  the  20%  Development  Fund   in
      accordance with the provisions of Section  313  of  RA  No.  7160  and
      Sections 107 and 110 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that (a) the Municipal Engineer, the official in-
      charge of the motor pool  and  the  Municipal  Accountant  immediately
      submit the documents required for audit and technical review; (b)  the
      Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer, in coordination
      with the Municipal Engineer, reconcile their records to determine  the
      total cost incurred for each project; and (c) the Municipal Accountant
      must see to it  that  disbursements  on  infrastructure  projects  are
      supported with sufficient technical documents and  that  vouchers  and
      supporting documents of completed projects are  immediately  submitted
      for audit.

We recommended that 1) the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) ensure
      that the GPB is reviewed  and  endorsed  by  the  DILG  prior  to  its
      incorporation in the Annual Budget; and 2) the GAD Focal  Person,  the
      Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant ensure that  GAD
      budgets  are  spent  for  the  purposes  for  which  they  have   been
      appropriated prior to affixing  their  signatures  in  the  Obligation
      Requests and Disbursement Vouchers.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Budget
Officer to prepare the Statement of Appropriations, Allotments, Obligations and
Balances (SAAOB) and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant on the
preparation of the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts
(SCBAA) for submission to the Auditor as part of the financial statements.
Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to maintain hard copy of general
ledgers and subsidiary ledgers, and exert effort to reconcile the surplus/deficit
from ordinary activities with the net cash flow from operating activities.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:
1. require the Accountant to identify, reconcile the unaccounted discrepancies
and record adjusting entries to establish correct balances of the cash
accounts;
2. require the Accountant to draw JEV to record the necessary adjustments on
the identified reconciling items and cancelled checks duly supported with
valid documents;
3. direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the original copies of the cancelled
checks; and
4. direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to transfer the accounts
maintained with PNB to any of the accredited government servicing bank
accounts.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal
Accountant to demand the immediate liquidation/settlement of past due cash
advances of all concerned accountable officers and personnel and enforce strict
compliance with the rules and regulations on cash advances. Further, require the
Municipal Accountant to identify the unaccounted amount and analyze the
negative balances to determine accurate outstanding balances of personnel
concerned.

We reiterate that the Local Chief Executive to compel the inventory
committee to conduct the complete physical count of all PPE and prepare and
submit the RPCPPE. Likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to
maintain and update the respective property records to support the reported
balances.

We reiterate that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal
Accountant to compute and provide depreciation consistently on all depreciable
PPEs to come up with an accurate and reliable presentation of asset accounts in the
financial statements.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal
Accountant to determine how much is the unspent/unexpended balance of the fund
in a yearly basis from CY 2012 to the present, once determined effect its transfer to
a Special Trust Fund, and present in the Notes to the Financial Statement the
amount and details of the fund in accordance with the prescribed format in Annex
D of COA Circular 2012-002. It is further recommended that the Municipal
LDRRM Officer be instructed to prepare and submit the LDRRMF Investment
Plan and Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRRM Fund. To correct
the erroneous entry we suggested the following entry :

Trust Fund Books General Fund Books
Cash in Bank – TF 105,000.00 Cash in Bank LCCA 105,000.00
Due to Other Funds 105,000.00 Training Expenses 105,000.00

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive thru the concerned officials to
appropriate the required one (1)% from its IRA in the Annual Budget and
maximize the allocated LCPC Funding compliance with RA 9344 and DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
and Treasurer to render their accounts and submit the required financial reports
within the reglementary period. Henceforth, strictly observe Section 347 of RA 7160
and COA Circular No. 2009-006 on the submission of financial accounts.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommend Local Chief Executive to
require the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents and process only
claims that are complete and properly supported with documents. This
recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension/Disallowance.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal
Health Officer to ascertain the requisitions for drugs and medicines conform with
the PNDF Vol. 1 prior to procurement, and the BAC to strictly require from the
supplier the Certificate of Product Registration from FDA, pursuant to Joint DOH-
DTI-IPO-BFAD Administrative Order No. 2008-01 dated November 8, 2008.

We recommend that the GAD Focal Person submit the annual GAD Plan and
Budget, and GAD Accomplishment Report, within the prescribed period, to
give the auditor ample time to review identified GAD activities and its judicious
implementation. Also, we recommend that at least 5% of the Annual Appropriation
be set aside to address gender issues within the locality.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to require the presentation of official receipt for taxi fare or a certificate
of expenses duly signed by the immediate supervisor in lieu of the official receipt
and the boarding pass as evidence that the passenger took the flight as stated
therein.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to require valid Official Receipts with ATPs that conform with BIR
RMC No. 52-2013 in the liquidation and/or reimbursement of claims.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:

Strictly adhere to the above-cited laws, rules and regulations;

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to
immediately submit the subject 174 DVs together with its complete
supporting documents;

e Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that paid DVs are intact as
listed in the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) upon transmittal to the Office
of the Accountant; and

e Instruct the Municipal Accountant to always transmit complete
submission of accounts to facilitate thorough audit and verification of the
agency’s financial transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office:

e Prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and take up the
necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all
reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis; and

e Perform constant review of recorded transactions to deter error and
omissions.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Accounting Unit and
Property/Supplies Unit:

Prioritize the conduct of physical count of all the LGU’s PPE;

Prepare and submit copy of the complete RPCPPE to the Auditor in
prescribed format, not later than January 31 of each year. In the
preparation of the report, the different column should be properly fill-up
to ensure that all items are properly identified and accounted for;

e Perform periodic reconciliation of property cards to ensure that all
discrepancies are immediately investigated, cleared and reconciled with
the books of accounts; and

* Maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and property cards,
for each class of PPE listing therein the data as to its acquisition,
description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation, disposal, and
other information about the property, plant and equipment, based on the
source documents of the transactions.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant-
Designate to update the posting of the financial transactions in the GL and maintain
SLs, where necessary, as required by Section 114 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 10 of
MNGAS, Volume IL

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:

o Prioritize the preparation and submission of the monthly BRS to ascertain
the reconciling items pertaining to the difference amounting to
?33,388.(i1 in order to establish the correct balances of the Cash in Bank
accounts for all funds as required in Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445 and
Section 3.{2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996;

o Necessary adjustments, if any, should be affected to avoid accumulation
thereof and to present the correct balance of the cash in bank accounts in
the ﬁmn?hl statements; and

e Prepare sqbsidiary ledgers for the cash accounts of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management regularly monitor its bank accounts to
avoid occurrence of inactive/dormant account. Likewise, Management should
coordinate with the LBP on the proper handling of cash balance in the dormant
Current Account No. 0412-1066-87.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:

o Transfer all the unexpended QRF and MF-MOOE to a Special Trust
Fund while all unobligated balance of LDRRMEF for CO should be made
continuing in the GF until the projects funded therefore are completed in
accordance with the accounting and reporting guidelines provided under
COA Circular No. 2012-002;

* Submit a revised AIP as approved by the local sanggunian and LCE upon
recommendation of the LDRRMC in accordance with the approved
LDRRMP for the release and utilization of the unexpended balances of
LDRRMF;

o Observe strictly the requirement set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-
002 specifically Section 5.1.16 on the proper and adequate
disclosure/discussion in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the
amounts and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF; and

o Prepare and certify the correctness of the Report on the Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF and the LDRRM Officer and submit the same to
the identified offices on or before the 15" day after the end of each
month. Also, the LDRRM Officer should comply with the posting
requirements of such reports in conspicuous places including websites, if
any, of the LGU/LDRRMO.

We recomménded that Management appropriate one per cent of the IRA of
the Municipality for the strengthening and implementation of the programs of the
LCPC. Also, prepare and submit the necessary reports enumerated in Item No. 1 of
Part I of the above-mentioned DILG MC for the proper and effective
implementation of PPAs.

We recommjanded that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to
monitor and reconcile the Due to BIR account to determine the cause of the
abnormal balances ;’md effect necessary adjustments.

We reiwerate:d our previous recommendation that Management ensure that
only expenditures t{lat have direct bearing or falling within the concept of disaster
preparedness, prevention and mitigation activities are included in the LDRRM plan
and charged to th% Fund. We likewise recommended that the LDRRM Council
monitor and evaluate the use and disbursement of the LDRRMF as required.

We reiterated the Audit Team’s previous recommendation that Management
submit requests for. technical inspection for completed projects to the COA to avoid
unnecessary delays jn the conduct of contract evaluation and technical inspection of
infrastructure contract/projects, observe the necessity of compliance on the required
documentary requirements for submission within the timeframe for review and
inspection.

We recommi;nded that Management take the following courses of action:
|
* Make sy: stematic and realistic forecasting of revenue taking into account
prior years’ experience and statistics as aids in setting targets; and

¢ Accomplish the Quarterly Report of Income (LBAc Form No. 1) on a
periodic basis so as to keep track of the status of revenue generation and
at the same time adopt measures that will likely improve collection for
accounts not reaching the targets.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Council
select/consider strictly PPAs that would be charged to the 20 per cent DF in
accordance with DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1. Also, financial transactions be
scrutinized carefully to ensure that only items that are directly associated in the
implementation of the targeted projects are properly charged to the Fund to
maximize its utilization for more beneficial purpose.

We recommqnded that Management comply with the provnsmns of the MOA
entered into by and between the DILG and the Municipality in the operation and
maintenance of the evacuatlon center through:

i
i
. Provisinni of, and eventual transfer to, an appropriate office space for the
MDRRMO:; and

o Prospectively, limit the utilization of the evacuation center to its intended
purpose only.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to
prepare the certified list of taxpayers and amounts due and collectible and submit
this to the Municipal Accountant as his basis in taking up the receivables and
deferred income in order for the financial statements to be fairly presented.

We recommended that Management effect the necessary adjusting entries to
reflect the correct account balances and prospectively, utilize the pro-forma journal
entries indicated in the MNGAS.

We recommended that Management observe prompt remittance of monthly
premiums due to PhilHealth in accordance to PhilHealth Circular No. 0001 series of
2014.
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting entry to recognize in the books at least 25% of Public Infrastructures.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant was not able to transfer public infrastructures in the books during the year.
observations.reason:
Reviewed the financial statements.
There was no transfer of public infrastructures reflected in the Statement of Changes in Net Assets/Equity.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit copies of such to the Auditor for review and verification of the validity and accuracy of the balance of the Cash in Bank accounts as appearing in the Municipality’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: None.
observations.reason: No BRSs were submitted in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the concerned Official of the Municipality refund the additional cash advance that was granted to him in the amount of ₱35,000.00 which remain unliquidated as of today and to submit immediately the liquidation papers for his cash advance in the amount of ₱70,000.00. We have also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit the liquidation documents of withdrawals from the Trust Fund totaling to ₱943,050.00 to enable us to evaluate the utilization of the cash advances.  Moreover, we have recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly follow the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 in the granting of cash advances and to properly record the cash advances in the books to ensure fairness of financial statement presentation.
observations.managementAction: The liquidation papers of the cash advances totaling to ₱943,050.00 were submitted.
observations.reason: Verified liquidation and reviewed financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Purchase Orders and delivery documents be furnished to the Office of the Auditor within the required period to allow us to execute timely evaluation of contracts and inspection of goods purchased.
observations.managementAction: None.
observations.reason: No purchase orders and delivery documents were furnished to the Office.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant attach the Journal Entry Vouchers to transaction documents to facilitate the verification of the propriety and correctness of entries made to record the transactions of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Attached JEVS to General Fund disbursement vouchers only.
observations.reason: Reviewed JEVs attached to transaction documents.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the General Services Officer-Designate, being the one in-charge of monitoring the use of municipal vehicles, see to it that use of a government motor vehicle shall be through a properly accomplished and duly approved trip ticket.  There must be placed a system of internal control that would ensure that issuances of gasoline are reasonable and only for official purposes.  We have also recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the validity of supporting papers before certifying on the completeness of documents.
observations.managementAction: Trip tickets attached to 2017 disbursement vouchers were not completely and properly accomplished.
observations.reason: Post audited transactions.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipality purchase a safety vault where collections and other funds should be kept. We have also recommend that collections be deposited intact daily.
observations.managementAction: Management purchased a safety vault where to keep collections.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant revisit COA Circular 2012-001 and see to it that all claims against the funds of the Municipality are duly supported with all the requisite documents in accordance with Section 4, Item 6 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445 before certifying the completeness of supporting documents in the Disbursement Voucher. We have also recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that all supporting documents are properly accomplished and bear the signatures of all signatories.
observations.managementAction: Management paid and processed transactions that lacked supporting documents.
observations.reason:
Post audited transactions.
It was noted that there were transactions in 2017 that lacked supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management require the Local School Board to strictly comply with the provisions of Sections 272 and 100 of RA No. 7160 in the formulation of the annual school board budget so that the utilization of SEF will be maximized for purposes for which the fund is intended for.
observations.managementAction: Budgeted the 2017 SEF for projects allowed under RA 7160.
observations.reason: Reviewed the 2017 SEF budget approved by the Local School Board.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipality purchase common-use supplies and equipment from the Procurement Service to take advantage of the low prices. We have also recommended that the PS Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks and Certificate of PhilGEPS Registration be attached to disbursement vouchers when purchasing from local suppliers in cases when the needed supplies/equipment are not available at the Procurement Service.
observations.managementAction: The LGU purchased only from local suppliers.
observations.reason: Post audited transactions.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat of the LGU prepare a complete APP for the year to make sure that procurements will be undertaken using the most appropriate method and avoid splitting of contracts which is prohibited under Section 54.1 of RA 9184. Supplemental APP can also be prepared if necessary subject to availability of funds.
observations.managementAction:
Made purchases not reflected in the submitted APP. 

 Did not submit some supplemental APPs to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: Checked purchases against the APP.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant withhold 1% retention money or require a special bank guarantee for the procurement of goods and 10% retention money for the procurement of infrastructure projects to warrant repair on discovered defects. Where the contractor requests for the substitution of the retention money for each progress billing on infrastructure projects, the Municipal Accountant should require irrevocable standby letters of credit from a commercial bank, bank guarantees or surety bonds callable on demand, of amount equivalent to the retention money substituted for and acceptable to Government, provided that the project is on schedule and is satisfactorily undertaken.
observations.managementAction: None.
observations.reason: Post audited transactions and performed auditorial contract reviews.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Officials and employees involved in the implementation of GAD programs/projects/activities see to it that the usage of the fund is maximized for the purpose it was created and the GAD Focal Person submit the GPB and corresponding Accomplishment Report within the required time to facilitate immediate review of the same.
observations.managementAction:
Programmed some GAD activities that are not specifically addressing gender issues.

Submitted the required reports only upon verbal request by the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Reviewed the GAD Plan and Accomplishment Report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and all LGU officials and employees who are responsible for the preparation and implementation/utilization of the 20% Development Fund ensure that usage of the fund will be maximized only for purposes for which the fund is intended for.
observations.managementAction: Management told the Audit Team that the utilization of the 20% Development Fund was based on the resolution approved by the Development Council.
observations.reason: Reviewed submitted utilization report of the 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that:

The DRRMO-Designate strictly see to it that all expenditures of the DRRM Fund are in accordance with the corresponding Plan in order to achieve the expected outputs;

The Municipal Accountant and the LDRRMO observe the monthly submission of Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF as required by Paragraph 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to facilitate timely evaluation of transactions that were charged against the fund; and

The Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: Management did not submit the required reports.
observations.reason:
Details of the expenses charged against the fund were not evaluated because no utilization reports were submitted; 

Reviewed financial statements and noted that there was no Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer ensure that taxes withheld are correct and remitted on time.
observations.managementAction: Remitted taxes monthly.
observations.reason: Post audited transactions.

observations.recommendation: submit to the Auditor the bank reconciliation statements as of December 31, 2016 and record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and correction in the books; and 4) require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 63 of the Local Treasury Operations Manual and COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that funds be appropriated for payment of insurance premiums and that a staff in the general services office be assigned to conduct inventory of all insurable properties and to monitor expiration and renewal of insurance policies.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has already appropriated funds and paid insurance permiums covering the various properties of the municipal government. A staff has also conducted inventory of all insurable properties and has been assigned to monitor their respective expirations and renew insurance policies on a yearly basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the immediate submission of the distribution lists duly acknowledged/received by the beneficiaries or a report on the remaining unaccounted balance to avoid audit suspension. Further, we recommended for the Municipal Budget Officer to verify the existence of appropriations with the Annual and Supplemental Budgets and the balances of appropriations in the Registries of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAOs) before certifying in the Obligation Requests as to existence of available appropriation and for the Municipal Accountant to maintain records on allotment and verify its balances before certifying in the Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) as to obligation of allotment.
observations.managementAction: The former MDRRMO-Designate submitted together with the Management Comment for CY 2016 the official list of beneficiaries who received relief goods during one southwest monsoon when the municipal government declared a state of calamity. The former MDRRMO-Designate explains that there was proper distribution of the subject relief goods. The office just failed to submit the official listing of beneficiaries during the submission of reports as release of said goods during the declared state of calamity was prioritized.
observations.reason: The management still failed to submit the distribution lists duly acknowledged/received by the beneficiaries or a report on the remaining unaccounted balance.

observations.recommendation: We  recommended  that Management direct the BAC to submit promptly all contracts with their complete supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five days from perfection thereof in compliance with existing regulations. Likewise, request for technical inspection for completed projects must be submitted to COA. Sound and effective internal control system must also be implemented in the procurement process relative to infrastructure projects to: avoid deficiencies and promote good governance. Any unjustified failure of the officials and employees concerned to comply with the requirements _herein _imposed shall be subject to the complete administrative disciplinary action provided in (a) Section 127 of P.D. No. 1445; (b) Section 55, Title I-B, Book V of the Revised Administrative Code of 1987, and (c) Section 11 of R.A. No. 6713.
observations.managementAction: All requests  for contract review returned to  Agency due to incomplete Supporting documents were already  re-submitted to COA. The complete supporting documents amounting to $16,863,585.87 were not submitted.
observations.reason: The requests for contract review for CY 2016 were already indorsed to the Office of the Supervising Auditor (OSA) COA Region V Technical and Audit Specialist (TAS). The requests for technical inspection for these  projects, however, were not yet submitted to COA. In addition, CY 2015 copies of contracts for 16 infrastructure projects and its complete supporting documents amounting to $16,863,585.87 were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Unit and Property/Supplies Unit: * Prepare and submit copy of the RPCPPE to the Auditor in prescribed format, not later than January 31 of each year. In the preparation of the report, the different column should be properly filled-up to ensure that all items are properly identified and accounted for; e Perform periodic reconciliation of property cards to ensure that all discrepancies are immediately investigated, cleared and reconciled with the books of accounts; and * Maintain separate and complete PPE Ledger Cards and property cards, for each class of PPE listing therein the data as to its acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation, disposal, and other information about the property, plant and equipment, based on the source documents of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: RPCPPE was submitted to the Accounting  Office for reconciliation but no action was taken due to lack of records especially those existing prior to CY 2013. The Property Office has property cards for recording of acquired PPE and other assets of the LGU.
observations.reason: No PPE Ledger Cards and property cards maintained in the Accounting Office. Thus, difficulty in reconciling PPEs as recorded in the books and in the records of the Property Officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: o Require the SB to submit the monthly list of attendance to sessions for SB members and Daily Time Records (DTRs) for department heads prior to the preparation of payroll; o Strictly observe the grant of RATA and the prescribed schedule of rates in accordance with Local Budget Circular (LBC) No. 103 dated May 15, 2013 to avoid overpayment of the same; and e Require the concerned officials to submit travel order and original certificate of appearance in case they are on official business or travel and or approved application for leave in case of forced leave or special leave, otherwise, refund the excess RATA claimed amounting to P60,838.75.
observations.managementAction: The SB Secretary submits monthly list to the Municipal Accountant for preparation of payroll. The excess RATA claimed was not yet refunded. The Municipal Accountant ensures that disbursements for payment of wages of JOs are supported with complete documentation. However, he said that he cannot fully monitor all transactions due to lack of manpower.
observations.reason: The submitted list of attendance was not properly filled up as to whether absence in sessions of the officials was for official business or with approved application for leave. No travel orders and original appearance for official business or travel and approved application for leave submitted. The excess RATA claimed was not yet refunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: e Require the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to judiciously plan the PPAs to be funded out of the 20 per cent DF in accordance with the mandate of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 - dated April 13,2011; and e Promptly implement all the programs/projects/activities as embodied in the approved Annual Development Plan in order to optimize the delivery of basic services to the people.
observations.managementAction: There are still some projects that are not among the allowable projects chargeable to the 20 per cent DF. Appropriation for the projects for CY 2017 not optimally utilized leaving a balance amounting to $2,755,048.80 for identified projects.
observations.reason: There are still some projects that are not among the allowable projects chargeable to the 20 per cent DF. Appropriation for the projects for CY 2017 not optimally utilized leaving a balance amounting to $2,755,048.80 for identified projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: o Enforce the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances by requiring the Accounting Office to strictly implement the rules and regulations prescribed under Section 89 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002; e Require all concerned officials and employees to liquidate immediately their cash advances; o Failure to liquidate after the issuance of another demand, suspend the salaries of erring officials and employees, or - cause the filing and institution of appropriate charges for failure to liquidate the cash advances, pursuant to the same regulations; and e Exert effort to cause the liquidation of the cash advances of personnel who were no longer in the service, otherwise ; those officers/employees who issued clearance shall be held personally liable, including the Accountant who failed to faithfully comply with his duty to monitor the liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Late submission of liquidation report and its supporting documents to the Accounting Office. Some cash advances were not liquidated despite completion/accomplishment of the purpose for the grant of the said cash advances. Cash advances of personnel who were no longer in the service totalling P52,936.33 still exist in the books. No action taken.
observations.reason: There are still some cash advances that were not liquidated. No demand letters issued to officials and employees with outstanding cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Accountant to strictly comply with R.A. No. 8424 and BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998. Facilitate the immediate remittance to the BIR of any unremitted taxes to reduce if not avoid the penalties that may be imposed by the said agency.

We recommended that Management enforce the proper observance of sound control measures in the granting and liquidation of cash advances by requiring the Accounting Office to strictly implement the rules and regulations prescribed under Section 89 of P.D. No. 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002, and Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) 23 in order to properly capture data indispensable for the LGU’s preparation of periodic financial statements for all funds.
observations.managementAction: Sound control measures in the granting and liquidation of cash advances, such as grants to officers with unliquidated balances, were not strictly implemented. The new designated Municipal Accountant will attend all necessary seminars and trainings for him to be equipped with the knowledge on the rules and regulations in the granting and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.reason: There are still some disbursements not strictly implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: Ensure that the Gender and Development (GAD) Focal Point System (GFPS) is in place, fully functional and that the members have the capacity and influence to promote gender mainstreaming that will promote efficient planning, implementation and monitoring of GAD activities; Follow the Calendar Schedule for the preparation of GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) for LGUs and incorporate the DILG-approved/endorsed GPB in the Annual Budget to ensure that the programs, projects and activities reflected thereat are in accordance with the existing regulations and guidelines; Submit annual GAD Accomplishment Report accompanied by the required documents; Set-up and maintain GAD database as spearheaded by the Local Planning and Development Office that will serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; With collaboration with the SB, formulate a GAD Code and implement it within the LGU and its barangays; and Follow PCW, DILG, DBM, NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by JMC No. 2016-01.
observations.managementAction: Reconstitution of the GFPS of the Municipality. The GPB, as incorporated in the Annual Budget were submitted on May 4, 2017. The Management was unable to submit the GAD Accomplishment report as of this date to the Audit Team. No GAD database established. The LGU GAD Code is already formulated. Orientation/revisit/re-view of the amended JMC 2016-01 to fully address and promote gender equality.
observations.reason: Reconstitution of the GFPS of the Municipality. The GPB, as incorporated in the Annual Budget was still not submitted on time. The Management was unable to submit the GAD Accomplishment report as of this writing. No GAD database established. Not all members of the GFPS are fully aware of the Circular.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: e Give prority in the establishment of functioning Municipal SWM Board that will monitor and ensure the effective implementation of the solid waste management plan and programs; e Hasten the acquisition of suitable land for SWM Facility. Make it so that the Municipality aims not only to purchase land to construct a control dumpsite which was also prohibited after five years in the enactment of RA. No. 9003, but a sanitary landfill which was required by the same law; e Submit a certification from the Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR), authorizing the type of dump site which the municipality can use; and e Fully implement the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 for the optimum management of the Municipality's solid waste.
observations.managementAction: Members are not fully aware of their involvement in the Board. Lack of funds to purchase land for SWM Facility. Dumpsite still on rehabilitation process. Management will reconstitute the MSWMB and reorientation on R.A. No. 9003 will be conducted. Management is considering outsourcing of funds to purchase land intended for SWM Facility. Request for authorization process from DENR-EMB.
observations.reason: Members are not fully aware of their involvement in the Board. Lack of funds to purchase land for SWM Facility. Dumpsite still on rehabilitation process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: e Municipal Accountant be required the timely submission of the monthly trial balances, year-end Financial Statements and other reports/schedules for CY 2015 in accordance with the regulations; e Appropriate remedial measures should be effected by Management to ensure that year-end financial statements and required reports are submitted promptly; and e Strict adherence to the cited rules and regulations on the rendition of reports be enforced by the concerned officials in order not to incur penalty as provided for in Section 122 (2) of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Delay in the submission of disbursement vouchers from the Treasury Office caused delay in the recording of transactions and ultimately affected the preparation of the financial statements by the Municipal Accountant. Lack of personnel in the Accounting Office. The Municipal Accountant is also designated as OIC Municipal Budget Officer, thus, he cannot attend to the timely preparation and submission of reports. Management will hire additional manpower. The Municipal Accountant submitted the financial statements of the Municipality per fund on various dates but the same were not supported with schedules and lack other necessary data such as the pre-closing trial balances.
observations.reason: Delay in submission of disbursement vouchers from the Treasury Office. Lack of personnel in the Accounting Office. Not all reports were submitted or were not made available to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following courses of action: e The Municipal Treasurer should submit to the Accounting Office all the disbursements vouchers, official receipts including the corresponding reports each month on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month. e The Municipal Accountant should strictly comply with the timetable on the submission of accounts as required under existing laws, rules and regulations; and o Strict adherence to the cited rules and regulations on the rendition of accounts and reports be enforced by the concerned officials in order not to incur penalty as provided in Section 122 (2) of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer was not able to submit the disbursement vouchers, official receipts and other reports on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month. The Municipality has a newly designated Municipal Accountant who is not yet fully aware of all the needed reports especially those requiring prior year’s information. Management promised to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: DVs submitted were for January and February 2015 only. The newly designated Municipal Accountant promised to exert more effort to submit the necessary documents and reports on time.

observations.recommendation: Accomplish and submit immediately the deficiencies noted for the settlement of the government funds; Ensure that claims against government funds are covered with duly approved vouchers and supported with complete documents before payment of the same; The Municipal Accountant should not process transactions that are not properly supported with the required documents; Advise other concerned officials not to approve and disburse the funds of the government without satisfying the requirements set by rules and regulations; and Strictly enforce the rules enunciated in COA Circular No. 2012-001, Sections 4(6) and 61 of P.D. No. 1445 before payment of claims.
observations.managementAction: According to Management, supporting documents for the transactions were already complied. Some disbursements were processed despite lack of necessary documentation of all documents. The Municipal Accountant will now strictly check complete documentation for all disbursements of the Municipality. Concerned officials were advised not to approve and disburse funds without supporting complete documents.
observations.reason: Not all transactions were supported with complete documents. Some supporting documents were not forwarded to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit immediately the required monthly BRS for the period covering January 2010 to December 2015 and remind him to submit the same within the prescribed period in succeeding months, otherwise withhold the payment of his salary until such time that these reports are submitted in accordance with Section 122 of P.D. No. 1445. Reconciliation of accounts should be given the utmost priority among the functions of the Municipal Accountant because the reliability of the recorded cash in books of the Agency is at stake.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliation Statements are now being prepared by the Agency and was already updated except for the SEF which is until December 31, 2009 only. BRS for CYs 2015, 2016 and January to July 2017 was already prepared and will be submitted to COA. The Municipal Accountant is still in the process of preparing the BRS for August to December 2017.
observations.reason: Monthly BRS was still not submitted to COA. The Municipal Accountant could no longer attend to BRS of LGU’s different bank accounts being a one-man office.

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommfended that Municipal Treasurer: e Update the cashbook daily based on the Report of Collections and Deposits; e Closely monitor = cash balances by reconciling daily the cash on hand with the cash balance in the cashbook to ensure the accuracy and physical existence of cash on hand; e Reconcile the cashbook balances with the accounting records :every end of the month. Take up the immediately the necessary corrections should there be a difference between these two records; e Close the accounts and transactions at the end of each month by double-ruling below the last entries in the cashbook. Totals should be properly indicated, ruled and footed; and ® Accomplish ceniﬁcationas to the correctness; and completeness of the recorded transactions at month-end after the last entry in the cashbook.
observations.managementAction: Cashbook was not updated. Also no cashbook maintained for Trust Fund accounts. Reconciliation of the balances of cash on hand and as recorded in the cashbook was not made daily. Cashbook balances not reconciled with the accounting records. Certification was not accomplished at month-end.
observations.reason: Cashbook was not updated. No Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption was submitted. Certification was not accomplished at month-end.

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommended the following courses of action: e Prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002 to facilitate the timely review of the sources of fund, ascertain the validity of expenditures and to determine whether the programs, projects and activities were included in the LDRRMF Plan; and e Maintain separate RAAO for QRF, MOOE and CO that will serve as monitoring tool in determining the accuracy of balances and the amount that will be transferred to Special Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Reports on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF were not prepared on a monthly basis and submitted on or before the 15% day after the end of each month. No separate RAAO for QRF, MOOE and CO of the LDRRMF was maintained in the Budget Office. The newly designated Municipal - Budget Officer said that he is not aware that a separate RAAO should be maintained for LDRRMF.
observations.reason: Reports on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF are prepared every quarter. No separate RAAO for QRF, MOOE and CO was maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: o Comply strictly with the pertinent provisions of CSC Memorandum Circular No. 40, series of 1998, as amended regarding the hiring of job orders; e Ensure compliance with CSC laws, rules and regulations in the preparation of contract of services or job orders. Job order contract should contain the specific : duties and responsibilities in order to match their skills the work to be performed; Instruct the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to strengthen the internal control system in hiring additional workforce through job orders; Require department heads concerned to submit request on the need to augment their manpower requirement to substantiate and justify the necessity of hiring the JOs and payment of wages; Require JOs to prepare and submit DTR and accomplishment reports of the specific services assigned to them to establish actual services rendered and validity of payments; and Require the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe laws, rules and regulations particularly as to completeness of documentary requirements before payment of wages of JOs pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Contracts do not contain the specific duties and responsibilities of the job orders hired. Department heads submit request for augmentation of internal personnel to the HRMO.
