observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

1) Require the  Local Development Council during the preparation of the annual budget and continuing appropriations  for ~ General Fund and 20% DF to include only PPAs which are feasible, specific and properly identified as to target date of execution and the personnel in-charge for implementation. Likewise, coordinate with the Municipal Engineering Office so that the same shall be supported with Program of Works (POWs) to ensure optimum utilization of the funds and timely and efficient implementation of the projects

2) Require the Local Finance Committee to review the balances of  continuing appropriations for every end of the year for possible inclusion in the annual budget preparation of the unexpended balances of appropriations for projects/program/activities which were already completed/accomplished.
observations.managementAction: Due to non- submission of monitoring reports on PPAs and SAAOB, the status of implementation of audit recommendations could not be fully and properly validated thus presented herein as not implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We rtecommended for the accounting office to stop recognizing  income  from inter-fund transactions as this practice renders the results of operations  of  economic enterprise  misleading. The management should re-assess and re-evaluate the operation of the heavy equipment and decide if it would be better that the management and maintenance of heavy equipment remained in the MEEDO or should be returned to the General Fund under the Engineering Office which is the lone wuser of heavy equipment.

The MEEDO  collecting officer should be instructed to temit daily and intact the collections to the treasurer’s office. The Municipal Treasurer should monitor the daily collections and issuances of Official Receipt (OR), compare the total collections per OR against the cash temitted and should there be under-remittance of collections, demand immediately the full remittance of collections from the collecting officer.

The  practice of using collections to defray various expenses must be stopped. All procurement must be included in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) of the agency.
observations.managementAction: Since the MEEDO collection receipts and deposit  slips  for January to March 2016 and September to December 2016 and the financial reports for  October to December 2016 were not yet submitted, the status of implementation of audit recommendations could not be fully and properly validated thus presented herein as not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting entry to recognize in the books at least 25% of Public Infrastructures.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit copies of such to the Auditor for review and verification of the validity and accuracy of the balance of the Cash in Bank accounts as appearing in the Municipality’s financial statements.

We have recommended that the concerned Official of the Municipality refund the additional cash advance that was granted to him in the amount of ₱35,000.00 which remain unliquidated as of today and to submit immediately the liquidation papers for his cash advance in the amount of ₱70,000.00. We have also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit the liquidation documents of withdrawals from the Trust Fund totaling to ₱943,050.00 to enable us to evaluate the utilization of the cash advances.  Moreover, we have recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly follow the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 in the granting of cash advances and to properly record the cash advances in the books to ensure fairness of financial statement presentation.

We have recommended that Purchase Orders and delivery documents be furnished to the Office of the Auditor within the required period to allow us to execute timely evaluation of contracts and inspection of goods purchased.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant attach the Journal Entry Vouchers to transaction documents to facilitate the verification of the propriety and correctness of entries made to record the transactions of the LGU.

We have recommended that the General Services Officer-Designate, being the one in-charge of monitoring the use of municipal vehicles, see to it that use of a government motor vehicle shall be through a properly accomplished and duly approved trip ticket.  There must be placed a system of internal control that would ensure that issuances of gasoline are reasonable and only for official purposes.  We have also recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the validity of supporting papers before certifying on the completeness of documents.
We have recommended that the Municipality purchase a safety vault where collections and other funds should be kept. We have also recommend that collections be deposited intact daily.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant revisit COA Circular 2012-001 and see to it that all claims against the funds of the Municipality are duly supported with all the requisite documents in accordance with Section 4, Item 6 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445 before certifying the completeness of supporting documents in the Disbursement Voucher. We have also recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that all supporting documents are properly accomplished and bear the signatures of all signatories.

We have recommended that Management require the Local School Board to strictly comply with the provisions of Sections 272 and 100 of RA No. 7160 in the formulation of the annual school board budget so that the utilization of SEF will be maximized for purposes for which the fund is intended for.

We have recommended that the Municipality purchase common-use supplies and equipment from the Procurement Service to take advantage of the low prices. We have also recommended that the PS Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks and Certificate of PhilGEPS Registration be attached to disbursement vouchers when purchasing from local suppliers in cases when the needed supplies/equipment are not available at the Procurement Service.

We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat of the LGU prepare a complete APP for the year to make sure that procurements will be undertaken using the most appropriate method and avoid splitting of contracts which is prohibited under Section 54.1 of RA 9184. Supplemental APP can also be prepared if necessary subject to availability of funds.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant withhold 1% retention money or require a special bank guarantee for the procurement of goods and 10% retention money for the procurement of infrastructure projects to warrant repair on discovered defects. Where the contractor requests for the substitution of the retention money for each progress billing on infrastructure projects, the Municipal Accountant should require irrevocable standby letters of credit from a commercial bank, bank guarantees or surety bonds callable on demand, of amount equivalent to the retention money substituted for and acceptable to Government, provided that the project is on schedule and is satisfactorily undertaken.

We have recommended that the Officials and employees involved in the implementation of GAD programs/projects/activities see to it that the usage of the fund is maximized for the purpose it was created and the GAD Focal Person submit the GPB and corresponding Accomplishment Report within the required time to facilitate immediate review of the same.

We have recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and all LGU officials and employees who are responsible for the preparation and implementation/utilization of the 20% Development Fund ensure that usage of the fund will be maximized only for purposes for which the fund is intended for.

We have recommended that:
The DRRMO-Designate strictly see to it that all expenditures of the DRRM Fund are in accordance with the corresponding Plan in order to achieve the expected outputs;
The Municipal Accountant and the LDRRMO observe the monthly submission of Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF as required by Paragraph 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to facilitate timely evaluation of transactions that were charged against the fund; and
The Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries as follows:
GF Books	Prior Period Adjustment	   ₱ 135,643.58
Due to Other Funds/Cash			   ₱ 135,643.58
TF Books	Due from Other Funds/Cash     ₱ 135,643.58
Trust Liabilities-DRRMF			               ₱ 135,643.58
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer ensure that taxes withheld are correct and remitted on time.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Budget Officer to prepare the Statement of Appropriations, Allotments,
Obligations and Balances (SAAOB) and coordinate with the Municipal
Accountant on the preparation of the Statement of Comparison of Budget
and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) for submission to the Auditor as part of the
financial statements. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to
maintain hard copy of general ledgers and subsidiary ledgers, and exert
effort to reconcile the surplus/deficit from ordinary activities with the net
cash flow from operating activities.

We reiterate our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive
require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit within the
reglementary period, the monthly bank reconciliation statements on all
depository accounts maintained by the LGU, together with the paid checks,
bank statements, and debit/credit memos, if any, in accordance with Section
74 of PD 1445 and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011, to
validate the reliability and accuracy of the Cash in Bank – LCCA balance as
shown in the financial statements.

We reiterate our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive
oblige/compel the Inventory Committee to conduct the annual physical
inventory of all its properties, and render the required report thereon.
Likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to
maintain property cards and property, plant and equipment ledger cards,
respectively, to record and monitor the receipt and issuances of properties to
end-users.
Further, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant exert effort to
determine the details of each Property, Plant and Equipment accounts,
particularly, its acquisition cost and date of purchase for her to compute the
corresponding depreciation and the correct carrying value of each property.

We recommend that the LGU strictly adhere to Sections 18 and 19 of
PPSAS 12 and the specific provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated
April 15, 2014, on the accounting and reporting of inventories and the
LDRRMF. Likewise, require the submission of the lacking documents
including the distribution list of relief goods to affected families and report
on receipt and distribution of in-kind donations received from DSWD RO
VIII and UEP. Moreover, require the preparation of adjusting entries,
reconciliation of Accounting and LDRRMO records, and the immediate
transfer of unexpended LDRRMF balance as of December 31, 2016 in
compliance to Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to
submit the lacking documents enumerated above. Henceforth, ensure that
procurements are properly supported with relevant documents before any
payments are made and strictly observe the prescribed period of submission
of perfected contracts as provided for under COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommend that management prepare and submit the Annual
Procurement Plan for CY 2016 in accordance with Section 7 and Appendix 2
of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. Likewise, procure common-use supplies and
equipment only at the DBM, PS Depot pursuant to AO No. 17 and DBM
Circular No. 2011-6. Common-use supplies not available at the PS Depot
should be procured in accordance with the IRR of RA 9184. Further,
procurements should be completely supported in compliance with COA
Circular No. 2012-001 and procurement documents should be properly
accomplished to ascertain the legality, propriety and validity of procurement
transactions.

We recommend that management revise the LGU’s 10 year Ecological
Solid Waste Management Plan (ESWMP) in accordance with Sections 16
and 17 of RA 9003 and submit the same to the National Solid Waste
Management Commission for its approval. Implement immediately all
infrastructure projects included in the ESWMP and strictly enforce the
provisions of RA 9003 in order to attain a systematic and comprehensive
solid waste management for the protection of public health and the
environment.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
School Board to prepare and submit annually the Special Education Fund
Budget. Likewise, ensure that available funds from SEF will be used to
finance priority projects as enumerated in Section 100(c) of RA 7160.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer and Accountant to submit the report of disbursements for the
period January 2016 under the General Fund, the liquidation
reports/payrolls for the period March 2016 under Trust Fund, and the two
check disbursement vouchers totaling P3,589.00. Henceforth, management
should strictly observe the timely and complete submission of accounts and
financial reports in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section
7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommend that management strictly observe the pertinent
provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013. This
recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension
and/or Disallowance resulting from post-audit of transactions, contracts
review, and inspection of the projects.

We recommended that management direct the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to
submit promptly all contracts with their complete supporting documents to the Audit Team
for review within five days from perfection thereof in compliance with existing regulations.
Likewise, request for technical inspection for completed projects must be submitted to COA.
Sound and effective internal control system must also be implemented in the procurement
process relative to infrastructure projects to avoid deficiencies and promote good
governance.

Any unjustified failure of the officials and employees concerned to comply with the
requirements herein imposed shall be subject to the administrative disciplinary action
provided in (@) Section 127 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1443 (b) Section 55, Title I-B,
Book V of the Revised Administrative Code of 1987; and (c) Section 11 of RA. No. 6713.

We recommended that the Accounting Unit and Property/Supplies Unit:

» Prepare and submit copy of the RPCPPE to the Auditor in prescribed format, not
later than January 31 of each year. In the preparation of the report, the different
column should be properly filled-up to ensure that all items are properly
identified and accounted for;

» Perform periodic reconciliation of property cards to ensure that all discrepancies
are immediately investigated, cleared and reconciled with the books of accounts;
and

» Maintain separate and complete PPE Ledger Cards and property cards, for each
class of PPE listing therein the data as to its acquisition, description, custody,
estimated 1ife, rate of depreciation, disposal, and other information about the
property, plant and equipment, based on the source documents of the
transactions.

We recommended that Management:

» Discontinue the practice of granting TA to its officers when such officers uses
government vehicle; and

% Require the concerned officials/employees to refund the excess payment of TA
whick the official is no longer entitled due to the use of a government vehicle.

We recommended that Management:

> Require the SB to submit the monthly list of attendance to sessions for SB
members and DTRs for department heads prior 1o the preparation of payroll;

» Strictly observe the grant of RATA and the prescribed schedule of rates in
accordance with LBC No. 103 dated May 15, 2013 to avoid overpayment of the
same; and

» Require the concerned officials to submit travel order and original certificate of
appearance in case they are on official business or travel and approved
application for leave in case of forced leave or special leave, otherwise, refund
the excess RATA claimed amounting 1o P60,838.75.

We recommended the Management to strictly observe the provisions of DECS-DBM-
DILG Joint Circular No. 1 and Section 272 of RA. No. 7160 on the utilization and
prioritization of the SEF that will improve the standard and quality of education. We also
recommended the Management to advise the Local School Board officials to give priority to
programs, activities and projects enumerated in the cited law and regulations in the
preparation of SEF budget.

We recommended that the Management:

» Reguire the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to judiciously plan the
programs/projects/activities to be funded out of the 20% DF in accordance with
the mandate of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011; and

» Promptly implement all the programs/projects/activities as embodied in the
approved Annual Development Plan in order to optimize the delivery of basic
services to the people.

We recommended that Management:

» Enforce the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances by
requiring the Accounting Office to strictly implement the rules and regulations
prescribed under Section 89 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002;

% Require all concerned officials and employees to liquidate immediately their cash
advances;

» Failure to liquidate afler the issuance of another demand, suspend the salaries of
erring officials and employees, or cause the filing and institution of appropriate
charges for failure to liquidate the cash advances, pursuant to the same
regulations; and

» Exert effort to cause the liquidation of the cash advances of personnel who were
no longer in the service, otherwise those officers/employees who issued clearance
shall be held personally liable, including the Accountant who failed to faithfully
comply with his duty to monitor the liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that Management require the Accountant to strictly comply with
RA. No. 8424 and BIR Revenue Regulation 2-98 dated April 17, 1998. Facilitate the
immediate remittance to the BIR of any unremitted taxes to reduce if nof avoid the penalties
that may be imposed by the said agency.

We recommended that Management enforce the proper observance of sound control
measures in the granting and liquidation of cash advances by requiring the Accounting
Office to strictly implement the rules and regulations prescribed under Section 89 of P.D.
No. 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002, and PPSAS 23 in order to properly capture data
indispensable for the LGU's preparation of periodic financial statements for all funds.

We recommended that Management ensure that only expenditures that have direct
bearing or falling within the concept of disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation
activities are included in the LDRRM plan and charged to the Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant
comply with the requirements of R.A. No. 10121 that the unexpended LDRRMF shall accrue
to a Special Trust Fund for the purpose of supporting DRRM activities within its five-year
validity period guided by the accounting and reporting procedures under COA Circular No.
2012-002. Likewise, we recommended that the appropriation for Mitigation Fund (MF)-
MOOE and MF-CO be specifically identified in the budget and utilization report for easy
determination of the amount to be transferred to the special trust fund or to be made
continuing in the General Fund books.

We recommended that management:

> Ensure that the GFPS is in place, fully functional and that the members have the
\ capacity and influence to promote gender mainsireaming that will promote
efficient planning, implementation and monitoring of GAD activities;

> Follow the Calendar Schedule for the preparation of GPB for LGUs and
incorporate the DILG-approved/endorsed GPB in the Annual Budget to ensure
that the programs, projects and activities reflected thereat are in accordance with
the existing regulations and guidelines;

» Submit annual GAD Accomplishment Report accompanied by the required
documents specified in the above-cited provisions;

> Set-up and maintain GAD database as spearheaded by the he Local Planning and
Development Office that will serve as basis for gender-responsive planning,
programming and policy formulation;

» With collaboration with the Sanggunian Bayan, formulate a GAD Code and
implement it within the LGU and its barangays; and

» Follow PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by JMC No.
2016-01 to fully address and implement applicable provisions in the convention
on the elimination of all forms of discrimination against women and promote
gender equality within the municipality.

We recommended that Management:

> Give priority in the establishment of fully functioning Municipal and SWM Board
that will monitor and ensure the effective implementation of the solid waste
management plan and programs;

» Hasten the acquisition of suitable land for SWM Facility. Make it so that the
Municipality aims not only lo construct a control dumpsite which was also
prohibited after five years in the enactment of RA. No. 9003, but a sanitary
landfill which was reguired by the same law;

» Submit a certification from the Department of Environment and Natural
Resources (DENR), authorizing the type of dump site which the municipality can
use; and

> Fully implement the provisions of RA. No. 9003 for the optimum management of
the Municipality's solid waste.

In consonance with the principle of fiscal responsibility, we recommended for the
Municipal Mayor, as the one who is primarily responsible for all government funds and
property of the municipality, to 1) require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal
Accountant to maintain/update the Cashbooks and Subsidiary Ledgers for all cash
accounts; 2) require the Municipal Treasurer to obtain copies of Subsidiary Ledgers on
cash accounts and bank statements from the Municipal Accountant, immediately
reconcile the same with the cashbook balances as of December 31, 2016, submit a
reconciliation statement to the Auditor and record the necessary adjusting entries in the
cashbooks; 3) require the Municipal Accountant to immediately prepare and submit to the
Auditor the bank reconciliation statements as of December 31, 2016 and record all valid
reconciling items that require adjustment and correction in the books; and 4) require the
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the provisions of
Section 63 of the Local Treasury Operations Manual and COA Circular No. 96-011 dated
October 2, 1996.

We recommended that a) the Municipal Treasurer be required to update the Real
Property Tax Account Registers or Taxpayer’s Index Card, prepare and submit to the
Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible
for CY 2016 and the ensuing years; and b) the Municipal Accountant be required to set
up the Real Property Tax Receivable, Special Education Tax Receivable, Deferred Real
Property Tax Income and Deferred Special Education Tax Income accounts for CY 2017
and onwards based on the certified list submitted by the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to reconstruct the
subsidiary ledgers for Due from Officers and Employees account as of December 31,
2016 and immediately submit copies thereof to the Auditor. The Municipal Accountant
should also be required to immediately submit the following documents relevant to the
unliquidated cash advances for filing of appropriate cases against erring accountable
officers:

a. Authenticated copy of Appointment of the Accountable Officer (AO) granted
cash advance

b. Designation or authority to draw cash advance

Authenticated copy of AO’s fidelity bond

Authenticated copy of demand letter of the local chief executive and proof of
receipt by the AO and answer/explanation thereto

e. Disbursement voucher/s and supporting documents pertinent to cash advances
granted to the AO/employee/recipient or equivalent and partial liquidation
documents

f. Paid check for the cash advance

g. Other relevant documents

Henceforth, the subsidiary schedule or Schedule of Unliquidated Cash Advances must be
submitted together with the financial statements, the total thereof duly reconciled with the
balances reported in the General Ledger, Trial Balance and Statement of Financial
Position. The subsidiary schedule must completely contain details as to name of
accountable officer, position/designation, purpose of cash advance, date of grant,
unliquidated amount, due date for liquidation, age of cash advances and status of
employment of the accountable officers whether they are still connected with the
municipal government, retired, resigned, dead or can no longer be traced.

In all instances, the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant must ensure
that each provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 are strictly observed and that all cash
advances are fully liquidated at the end of each year.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to reconcile the Due from
Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts, determine the cause of disparity in
balances and record the necessary adjustments in the books to present the correct
balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that a) the Municipal Accountant be required to immediately
reconstruct the Real Property Ledger Cards; b) the Municipal General Services Officer,
as the custodian of all properties, real or personal, owned by the municipal government
[Section 490(b)(3)(i), Title V, Book III of RA No. 7160], be instructed to inventory all
real properties of the municipal government whether acquired, donated or reclaimed and
to institute proceedings for issuance of tax declarations and certificates of title in the
name of the municipal government in coordination with the Municipal Assessor; and c)
the Municipal General Services Officer be instructed to regularly inspect the real
properties owned by the municipal government to keep track of their condition.

We recommended that a) the Inventory Committee be required to conduct actual
physical inventory, reconcile the results of the count with available records in the
accounting and general services offices and submit the RPCI and RPCPPE to the Auditor
pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and that all
discrepancies between physical and book inventories must be investigated, cleared and
reconciled immediately; and b) the accounting and general services office be required to
reconstruct the property cards and ledgers based on source documents or vouchers to
facilitate reconciliation of physical and book inventories.

We recommended that funds be appropriated for payment of insurance premiums
and that a staff in the general services office be assigned to conduct inventory of all
insurable properties and to monitor expiration and renewal of insurance policies.

We recommended that the Municipal General Services Officer be required to
locate/obtain Certificates of Registration of subject vehicles costing £7,065,883.23 and
that a staff in the general services office be assigned to monitor and process vehicle
registrations.

We recommended for the accounting office to reconstruct the maintenance/repair
record of each equipment/vehicle owned and/or operated by the municipal government.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to trace, locate and
recover the documents related to the above accounts totaling £29,833,153.01 and record
the details obtained in the respective subsidiary ledgers and to demand payment for long
overdue receivables.

Henceforth, we recommended for the Municipal Mayor to ensure that the Municipal
Accountant maintains the required books of accounts and submits the financial reports
and schedules in such form and within the period prescribed in the NGAS Manual for
LGUs.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the accounting and budget
offices to submit the required reports within the prescribed period and to reconcile their
records on allotments and obligations monthly so that resulting discrepancies can be
immediately verified and corrected.

In consonance with the principle of fiscal responsibility, we recommended that

the Municipal Mayor, as the one who is primarily responsible for all government funds
and property of the municipality, should require the Municipal Accountant to submit the
financial statements, journals and supporting schedules/documents in such form and
within the period prescribed in Sections 4(e), 70 and 72 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs,
Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010 and Section 7.2.1(a) of
COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to ensure that the required reports on
projects/programs/activities are submitted within the period prescribed in COA Circular
No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 as amended by COA Circular No. 2015-006 dated
August 20, 2015. The monitoring report on the municipal government’s
projects/programs/activities should contain details as to project name, location, total cost,
date started, extensions, target and actual completion date, project status and total cost
incurred to date duly reconciled with the accounting records.

We recommended for the concerned municipal government officials to seek
refund of the amounts paid to former Sangguniang Bayan Members that represent the
cash equivalent of the mandatory five-day leave that were not deducted from their
respective vacation leave credits. Henceforth, we recommended strict observance of the
provisions of CSC Memorandum Circular No. 06, s. 2003 and Rule XVI of the Omnibus
Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292.

We recommended strict adherence to the provisions of DBM LBC No. 63 dated October
22, 1996 and that grant of subsidies to barangays must be made payable to the name of
the barangays and must be duly receipted by the barangays.

We recommended for strict adherence to the aforesaid provisions of RA No. 7160
and COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 in that the documents discussed
above shall be signed/approved by the Municipal Mayor herself in the exercise of the
powers, duties and functions accorded to the local chief executive by RA No. 7160 or the
Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended for the immediate submission of the vouchers covering partial
payments for road rights-of-way made in CY 2015 as shown in Annex 7 and of the
lacking supporting documents for payments and acquisitions made in CY 2016 to avoid
audit suspension. Henceforth, we recommended for the Municipal Accountant to
thoroughly review the Disbursement Vouchers "and attachments to determine
completeness of supporting documents as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001
dated June 14, 2012 and the Journal Entry Vouchers to ensure that transactions are
properly recorded in the books in accordance with the Chart of Accounts prescribed in
COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that 1) the Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Budget Officer be required to immediately reconcile their records on
DRRMEF in coordination with the MDRRMO; 2) the Municipal Accountant be required to
immediately record the necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the correct balances
of the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF and Government Equity accounts; 3) the Municipal
Accountant and MDRRMO be required to cause the immediate transfer of the amount of
£20,127,287.81 to the separate depository account for DRRMF; and 4) the MDRRMO be
required to review the unexpended/unobligated balances of the DRRMF for CO to
determine savings from already completed programs or projects that can be appropriated
for other DRRM activities pursuant to Section 5.1.11 of COA Circular No. 2012-002
dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended for the immediate submission of the distribution lists duly
acknowledged/received by the beneficiaries or a report on the remaining unaccounted
balance to avoid audit suspension.

Further, we recommended for the Municipal Budget Officer to verify the existence of
appropriations with the Annual and Supplemental Budgets and the balances of
appropriations in the Registries of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAOs)
before certifying in the Obligation Requests as to existence of available appropriation and
for the Municipal Accountant to maintain records on allotment and verify its balances
before certifying in the Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) as to obligation of allotment.

We recommended for the immediate submission of the aforesaid lacking
supporting documents together with the inventory of heavy equipment owned by the
municipal government and their pertinent area of operation and operation hours for CY
2016, consistent with the provisions of Section 39 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 or the
Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and to avoid audit suspension.

We recommended for the Head of the Requesting Office, the Municipal Budget
Officer and the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly review the propriety of charges
against appropriations/allotments, verify the balances of appropriations/ allotments and
check if declared savings in Augmentation Ordinances and Supplemental Budgets are
deducted from the original appropriations prior to affixing their signatures in the
Obligation Requests and Disbursement Vouchers.

Further, we recommended that a separate depository account be opened for the
20% Development Fund to better monitor the expenditures paid out of the fund.
Likewise, we recommended for the Municipal Accountant to maintain a special account
in the General Fund and prepare separate financial statements for the 20% Development
Fund in accordance with the provisions of Section 313 of RA No. 7160 and Sections 107
and 110 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume 1.

In consonance with the principle of fiscal responsibility, we recommended that the Municipal
Mayor, as the one who is primarily responsible for all government funds and property of the
municipality, should require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking
financial statements, journals and supporting schedules/documents to comply with the
provisions of Section 347 of RA No. 7160, Sections 4(e), 70 and 72 of the NGAS Manual for
LGUs, Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010 and Section 7.2.1(a)
of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

The management has not yet commented on the matter.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to ensure that the required reports on
projects/programs/activities are submitted within the period prescribed in COA Circular No.
2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 as amended by COA Circular No. 2015-006 dated August
20, 2015. The monitoring report on the municipal government’s projects/programs/activities
should contain details as to project name, location, total cost, date started, extensions, target
and actual completion date, project status and total cost incurred to date duly reconciled with
the accounting records.

. The management also has not yet commented on the matter.

We have recommended that the Municipality conduct a complete    physical inventory of all its PPE and to furnish us a copy of the corresponding report. An Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Properties should also be prepared for LGU assets which will be determined to be beyond economical repair to facilitate dropping of costs of these properties from the books of the LGU make sure that only serviceable assets are reported in the Statement of Financial Position of the Municipality to ensure fairness of financial statement presentation.

No. 1445 before payment of claims.

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommended that Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain all the cashbooks required pursuant to Section 63 (a) of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommended that Municipal Treasurer: Update the cashbook daily based on the Report of Collections and Deposits; Closely monitor cash balances by reconciling the cash on hand with the cashbook to ensure the accuracy and physical existence of cash on hand; Reconcile the cashbook balances with the accounting records every end of the month. Take up immediately the necessary corrections should there be a difference between these two records; Close the accounts and transactions at the end of each month by double-ruling below the last entries in the cashbook. Totals should be properly indicated, ruled and footed; and Accomplish certification as to the correctness and completeness of the recorded transactions at month-end after the last entry in the cashbook.
observations.managementAction: Cashbooks are updated daily. reconciliation of balances with the accounting records every end of the month.
observations.reason: The agency still fails to make reconciliation of the cashbook balances with the accounting records every end of the month.

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommended the following courses of action: Prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002 to facilitate the timely review of the sources of fund, ascertain the validity of the expenditures and to determine whether the programs, projects and activities were included in the LDRRMF Plan; and Maintain separate RAAO for Quick Response Fund (QRF), Maintenance and Other Operating Expense (MOOE) and Capital Outlay (CO) that will serve as monitoring tool in determining the accuracy of balances and the amount that will be transferred to Special Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: LDRRMF Utilization Report was already submitted.
observations.reason: Separate RAAO for LDRRMF was still not yet maintained.

observations.recommendation: Management should exercise full compliance to the Public Disclosure policy to further enhance transparency in the implementation of DRRM activities and utilization of funds. Efforts should be maximized to provide the public of timely information.
observations.managementAction: LDRRMF Utilization as of December 31,2016 posted in the full disclosure policy board of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Chief Executive appoint immediately the lacking members of the LDRRMC in compliance with R.A. No. 10121 to achieve the purpose of promoting and strengthening partnership with all sectors and all stakeholders concerned towards effective delivery of disaster risk reduction programs, activities and services to citizenry.
observations.managementAction: Reorganization of the LDRRMC was made and all mandatory members were appointed in compliance with R.A. No. 10121.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: Management should strictly comply with the provisions of the JMC No. 2013-01 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA to ensure that proper planning and budgeting is provided for the Gender and Development (GAD) program and activities which will address the gender related issues and concerns identified in the GAD plan; Allocate at least five percent of the agency’s total appropriations authorized under the Annual Budget to support GAD plans and programs; GAD Focal Person should take the lead in mainstreaming gender in agency programs, activities and projects in the Municipality and the preparation of GAD Plan & Budget (GPB) and Accomplishment Report (AR) in the prescribed format. Also, require the submission of the GPB and AR to the oversight agencies; Full implementation of the GAD fund for realization of intended result and extending the expected benefits to beneficiaries; and Set time and prioritize the establishment of a local GAD Code to address adequately and satisfactorily all reported gender issues and concerns.
observations.managementAction: Management GPB was allocated at least 5% of the total appropriations. Accomplishment Teport was not accompanied by the required reports. Related finding on GAD incorporated in Part IT of this report.
observations.reason: Submission of GPB was submitted late to DILG. Accomplishment Report was not accompanied by the required reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: Ensure compliance with CSC laws, rules and regulations in the preparation of contract of services or job orders; Instruct the Human Resource Management Officer to strengthen the internal control system in hiring additional workforce through job orders; Require department heads concerned to submit request on the need to augment their manpower requirement to substantiate and justify the necessity of hiring the JOs and payment of wages; Require JOs to prepare and submit Daily Time Records (DTR) and accomplishment reports of the specific services assigned to them to establish actual services rendered and validity of payments; and Require the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe laws, rules and regulations particularly as to completeness of documentary requirements before payment of wages of JOs pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: DTRs were attached to the vouchers for payment of wages of job orders and contract of services. Specific duties and responsibilities were not indicated in the contract of services.
observations.reason: Specific duties and responsibilities were not indicated in the contract of service.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the subsidiary schedules of General Ledger accounts and year-end adjusting and closing entries in addition to the financial agency year-end Financial Statement of Statements be submitted in the prescribed format on time. Provide the explanatory notes on the Notes to Financial Statements to present adequate disclosure of financial data. Its timely submission in the prescribed formats could facilitate the fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Subsidiary schedules were attached to the financial agency statement. Subsidiary schedules were incomplete. Further, the agency failed to make complete disclosures on the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.reason: Subsidiary schedules were incomplete. Further, the agency failed to make complete disclosures on the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency reclassify the CIV in the amount of 58,953.77 as Due from Officers & Employees. In as much as the former Accountable Officer is already dead, make representation with the Commission on Account for the write-off of the account balance. Submit the death certificate, cashbooks of the late Municipal Treasurer, inventory of his accountabilities and copy of turn-over of accountabilities between the Municipal Treasurers who succeeded Mr. Azarias Samonte to establish the actual amount for write-off.
observations.managementAction: Balance of Cash Local Treasury (formerly known as CIV) amounted to $59,053.77 as of December 31, 2016.
observations.reason: Documents needed for justification were not submitted to COA to establish the actual amount for write-off of the Cash in Vault balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency: Appropriate funds for the titling of the lands owned by the LGU; Exert effort to secure TCTs of the parcels of land to establish ownership/rights over the properties; and Ensure ownership/rights over the properties before additional improvements are constructed.
observations.managementAction: Management is still waiting for the approval of funds for the survey of the land owned by the LGU to facilitate the issuance of title.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to create an inventory committee to conduct physical count of inventories along with the property, plant and equipment of the agency. We likewise, recommended the inventory committee to coordinate with the operating units on the types of inventories received and still on hand every first and second semesters of a given year and prepare report for submission to the Accountant as basis in recording in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Physical inventory was completed on June 2016 based on the submitted report of each offices and was verified by the inventory committee as to existence of listed PPE.

observations.recommendation: We required the Supply Officer or the official in charge of accepting deliveries to submit all AIRs to the Office of the Auditor within 24 hours from acceptance thereof, so as not to forego opportunities for identifying defects and/or deficiencies that might subsist and could provide disadvantage to the government. Unjustified failure on the part of the official or employee concerned to submit the report mentioned herein shall be considered a ground for the automatic suspension of payment of his/her salary until he/she shall have complied with the aforesaid requirements (Section 122, P.D. No. 1445).
observations.managementAction: Submission of AIRs is now being complied by the Property Management Supply Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency: Require the drivers to accomplish the driver’s trip tickets for every trip undertaken as required; Prepare and submit Monthly Report of Official Travels per vehicle for the month; Prepare and submit Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Auditor for verification. The report shall show among others, the type of motor vehicles used during the month, total distance travelled, total fuel used and the normal fuel consumption for each vehicle.
observations.managementAction: Driver’s trip tickets are now required for every trip undertaken. Also, report on the weekly fuel consumption is already being prepared. Monthly report of official travels per vehicle and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption showing among others, the type of motor vehicles used during the month total distance travelled, total fuel used and the normal fuel consumption for each vehicle was not prepared and submitted to COA for verification.
observations.reason: Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption showing among others, the type of motor vehicles used during the month total distance travelled, total fuel used and the normal fuel consumption for each vehicle was not prepared and submitted to COA for verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management comply strictly with Section 47 of RA. No. 9184 (Conflict of Interest). Disqualify all bidders related to the Procuring Entity (HOPE) and members of BAC, Technical Working Group (TWG), BAC Secretariat, Project Management Office (PMO) and the designers of the project by consanguinity or affinity to participate in the procurement of goods and services to avoid imposition of penal sanctions on criminal offenses committed.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer/Vice Chairman of BAC had already retired.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that municipality prioritize the submission of the Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan as required under R.A. No. 9003 and implement the attainable/possible control measures pending approval of the plan upon its submission. The garbage truck should be maintained in operating capacity and not left to further deteriorate so as not to diminish available equipment for waste disposal. Seek the active involvement of the local citizenry in the composting and recycling of their waste in their household. Enhance their bayanihan spirit and personal pride in maintaining a clean environment. Instruct the personnel concerned on the minimal use of open dump and effective garbage collection. Controlled dumps shall not be allowed five years after effectivity of this Act.
observations.managementAction: Related finding incorporated in Part I of this report.

observations.recommendation: We rtecommended that agency prepare programs, projects and activities that are responsive to the identified gender issues to facilitate budgeting. The agency should allocate at least five-percent of the total agency appropriations to support GAD plans and programs. Please incorporate team-building activities. Promote gender responsive governance and address gender issues.
observations.managementAction: Programs, projects and activities were identified through gender mainstreaming. The agency should allocate at least five-percent of the total agency appropriations to support GAD plans and programs. Please incorporate team-building activities. Promote gender responsive governance and address gender issues.
observations.reason: funds were not specifically allotted for the purpose as required under existing government rules and regulations, and thus, hindering the fulfilment of desired targets of the program.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the required monthly BRS for the period covering January, 2010 to December, 2013 and remind him to submit the same promptly in succeeding months, otherwise withhold the payment of his salary until such time that these reports are submitted in accordance with Section 122 of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: BRS are now being prepared by the agency and was already updated except for the SEF which is until December 31,2009 only.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly with the use of perpetual inventory method in the recording and issuance of drugs and medicines intended including medical, dental and laboratory supplies; Refrain from dropping inventory items from the books of accounts without adequate documentation; and Require the concerned Property Officer to submit the Summary of Supplies/Materials Issued (SSMI) and its supporting documents for the drugs, medicines, medical, dental/laboratory supplies to the Accounting Division to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for recording the expense.
observations.managementAction: Due to minimum drugs, medical dental and laboratory supplies purchased, same were issued directly to its beneficiaries/intended users, hence, applying the perpetual inventory is impractical.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management expedite the complete physical count of existing properties and equipment to validate the accuracy and correctness of the account balances. Then the personnel of the General Service Office (GSO) and the accounting office reconcile the results of the physical count with the property and accounting records; investigate the differences noted in the two reports and adjustments, if any should be effected. The reconciliation of records will help vouch the reliability of the PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: Physical inventory was completed on June 2016 based on the submitted report of each offices and was verified by the inventory committee as to existence of listed PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management comply with the prescribed check list and required supporting documents for common government transactions in accordance with Section 44 (c) of MNGAS and COA Circular No.2012-001, respectively.
observations.managementAction: Management justified that some of the payees have not yet completed their required supporting documentation for liquidation of their cash advances.
observations.reason: Some payees not yet completed their required supporting documents for liquidation of their cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately to the Audit Team the 397 JEVs for verification/post-audit to determine the correctness of the transactions recorded for fair presentation of the agency’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Based on the submitted disbursement vouchers for CY 2016 under General Fund, JEV were already attached.
observations.reason: JEVs for the 397 disbursement vouchers identified in the observations in CY 2013 are not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepares separate financial report for special account for KALAHI-CIDSS in the General Fund in conformity with Section 313 of R.A. No. 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991 and Sections 105, 106 and 110 of the MNGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Separate financial report for KALAHI was already prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant comply with the requirement of R.A. No. 10121 that the unexpended LDRRMF shall accrue to a special trust fund for the purpose of supporting DRRM activities within the next five years guided by the accounting and reporting procedures under COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Unexpended balances of LDRRMF was recorded as debit to Due from Other Funds and credit to Trust Liabilitles — DRRM under the Trust Fund account.
observations.reason: No actual transfer of cash. Related finding incorporated in the Part IT of this report.

observations.recommendation: Management should require the BAC to submit to COA all contracts including each of all the documents forming part thereof within five days from execution thereof in order for us to conduct the timely review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Some contracts entered into by the LGU are submitted. Contracts submitted are incomplete and lacks the required supporting documents.
observations.reason: Contracts submitted are incomplete and lacks the required supporting documents.

municipality stop the practice
of granting medical assistance
which do not conform with the
provisions of Administrative
Order No. 402 and CSC
Memorandum Circular No. 33,
5.1997. We further
recommend the Municipality
to establish the annual medical
check-up program for LGU
officials and employees.

The management
commented that
granting of medical
assistance was
practiced by the
municipality in the

considering that the

and Csc

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Officer strictly observe the monthly submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the COA Auditor.

We recommended that the procuring office comply with the regulations and furnish the Auditor with the required copies of contract documents and their supporting documents as well as notice of deliveries of procured items within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The management had already started of submitting copies of some contracts and supporting documents and notice of deliveries of procured items to the Auditor in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor:

1)  Require the Local
Development Council during
the preparation of the annual
budget and  continuing
appropriations for  General
Fund and 20% DF to include
only PPAs which are feasible,
specific and properly
identified as to target date of
execution and the personnel
in-charge for implementation.
Likewise, coordinate with the
Municipal Engineering Office
so that the same shall be
supported with Program of
Works (POWs) to ensure
optimum utilization of the
funds and timely and efficient
implementation of the projects

2) Require the Local Finance
Committee to review the
balances of  continuing
appropriations for every end
of the year for possible
inclusion in the annual budget
preparation of the unexpended
balances of appropriations for
of

2014

We recommended for the
accounting office to stop
recognizing income  from
inter-fund transactions as this
practice renders the results of
operations  of  economic
enterprise misleading. The
management should re-assess
and re-evaluate the operation
of the heavy equipment and
decide if it would be better
that the management and
maintenance of heavy
equipment remained in the
MEEDO or should be returned
to the General Fund under the
Engineering Office which is
the lone wuser of heavy
equipment.
The MEEDO collecting
officer should be instructed to
remit daily and intact the
collections to the treasurer’s
office. The Municipal
Treasurer should monitor the
daily collections and issuances
of Official Receipt (OR),
compare the total collections
per OR against the cash
remitted and should there be
under-remittance of
collections, demand
immediately the full
remittance of collections from
the collecting officer.

The practice of  using
collections to defray various
expenses must be stopped. All
procurement must be included
in the Annual Procurement
Plan (APP) of the agency.
observations.managementAction:
The management
commented that fund
transfer was indeed
recorded as rent
income in the past.
However, upon
recognition that this
practice was neither
acceptable nor within |
the prescribed
accounting  practices
and ultimately
renders the result of
operations of
economic  enterprise
misleading, the same
was discontinued and
is no longer being
practiced.
observations.reason:
In CY 2016, MEEDO
collections were still
not fully remitted to
the Municipal
Treasurer and
collections were still
used to pay various
expenses through
reimbursement
scheme.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Officer strictly observe the monthly submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the COA Auditor. Submitted Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund for January to September 2016.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Officer 40 submitted of accomplishment AAR 2015 The Municipal Accountant has not yet submitted Monthly reports in CY 2016 Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund for October to December 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the procuring office comply with the regulations and furnish the Auditor with the required copies of contract documents and their supporting documents as well as notice of deliveries of procured items within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The management had already started of submitting copies of some contracts and supporting documents to the Auditor in CY 2016.
